This statement is before "Mah Tovu," etc., and it extends through all the levels and stages of prayer: "Mah Tovu," "Hodu LaShem," etc., the *Amidah* prayer, and up to the "Aleinu" prayer, etc., and "Therefore we hope," which Joshua the son of Nun said when he conquered the Land of Israel for the first time, in a manner of an eternal inheritance. All these details and levels in prayer reach their perfection through the acceptance of the positive commandment of *Ahavas Yisrael*.

6) May it be the will that every man and woman does everything within their power to fulfill the request and the empowerment of the Baal HaGeulah to spread the wellsprings outward. It is known that the main matter of "your wellsprings shall spread outward" began after Petersburg. It can be said that this is not only after (and caused by) the redemption from Petersburg, but also caused by the imprisonment, for "this olive, when it is crushed, it releases its oil." One might say (in a deeper sense) even more: that this is why our Rebbe the Alter Rebbe was in prison for fifty-three days, so that afterwards there would be the matter of spreading the wellsprings in the garden of the Holy One, blessed be He—this world. From this, one understands the greatness of the merit of engaging in the spreading of the wellsprings, to spread them throughout all the dispersions of Israel—men, women [as the Rebbe Rayatz said that women are also obligated in the study of the teachings of Chassidus, for this Torah leads to the fulfillment of the commandments of love and awe of Hashem and the like, in which women are also obligated (and their obligation is constant, as brought in the introduction to *Sefer HaChinuch*)] and children, so that they may hear and so that they may learn, etc., and observe to perform all the words of this Torah.

May it be the will that everyone engages in this, as is proper, and even more so in these final days that remain of the days of *galus*. This will bring about, very soon, and with joy and goodness of heart, that we will go with our youth and our elders, with our sons and our daughters to greet our righteous Moshiach. And we will go together with our righteous Moshiach, and with all the *Sifrei Torah* that are in the diaspora, and generally with the synagogues and houses of study that are in Bavel (in the diaspora), to the Land of Israel, the Holy Land, speedily and truly in our days, with joy and goodness of heart.

## 19 Kislev 5715

"Petach Eliyahu" begins: "Ribono shel Olam, You are One, but not in calculation; You are higher than all the high, hidden from all the hidden; no thought can grasp You at all. You are the One who brought forth ten *tikkunim*, and we call them ten *Sefiros*." The Rebbe Rayatz explains in his *maamar* beginning with the word "Zeh", delivered on 19 Kislev (5702), that this passage enumerates all the levels from the *Atzmus* of *Ohr Ein Sof* down to the *Sefiros* of *Atzilus*. "You are One" refers to the *Atzmus* of *Ohr Ein Sof*, and in *Keter*, it refers to the *pnimiyus* of *Keter*, which is *Atik*. Regarding this level, it says "not in calculation," for it is not included in the calculation of the matters that follow; all subsequent matters (including "higher than all the high") are incomparable to "You are One," and therefore are not counted with It—"not in calculation." "You are higher than all the high" refers to the *chitzoniyus* of *Keter*, which is *Arich*. "Hidden from all the hidden" refers to the *Chochma* of *Arich*. "No thought can grasp You at all" refers to the *pnimiyus* of *Chochma*. "You are the One who brought forth ten *tikkunim*" refers to the *Sefiros* of *Atzilus*. He explains that the reason the *Sefiros* in *Atzilus* are numbered (ten *tikkunim*) is that *Atzilus* is part of the *Seder Hishtalshelus* (limitation), and within *Hishtalshelus*, the concept of number applies. From this, it is understood that the matters enumerated here are generally divided into three categories: "You are One," which refers (as above) to *Atik*, which is removed and distinct from *Hishtalshelus*; "You are higher than all the high," which is above *Hishtalshelus* but relates to *Hishtalshelus*; and "You are the One who brought forth ten *tikkunim*," which are the *Sefiros* of *Atzilus*, the *Hishtalshelus* itself. The *maamar* continues that through the *avodah* of *mesiras nefesh*, one draws down from the level of "You are One" into the *Sefiros*. Thereby, the revelation of that which is above nature is drawn into nature.

2) From the fact that the *maamar* states, "You are the One who brought forth ten *tikkunim*... to conduct the worlds through them," implying that the reason for the emanation of the *Sefiros* of *Atzilus* is so that through them there would be the conduct of the worlds, it is understood that the levels above the *Sefiros* are above the conduct of the worlds. Accordingly, the revelation of "You are higher than all the high, etc." is also above nature. The *maamar* states that the drawing down of the revelation that is above nature is through the *hemshech* from the level of "You are One." It must also be understood: since even the conduct of the world according to nature is such that *Ohr Ein Sof* conducts the world in this manner—"You are the One who brought forth ten *tikkunim*... to conduct the worlds through them"—why is it necessary for there to be a drawing down of the revelation that is above nature into nature? Even though the fact that *Ohr Ein Sof* conducts the worlds through the *Sefiros* is by way of His *hitlabshus* in the *keilim* of the *Sefiros* [for the conduct of the worlds is from the light *that is clothed* in the worlds, and for the light to be clothed in the worlds, it must (first) be clothed in the *keilim*], nevertheless, even after the light is clothed in the *keilim* and in the worlds, it remains connected with the *Bli Gvul Ohr Ein Sof*. As explained in *Hemshech 5666*, the reason there is a sense of constancy in the conduct of nature (even though nature is from the *Elokus* vitality clothed in the created beings, which is a limited vitality) is that the root of the vitality clothed in the created beings is from the *Ohr HaKav*, whose root is from the *Ohr Ein Sof* before the *Tzimtzum*, and the perfection of nature is through the revelation that is above nature being drawn into it.

Seemingly, one could say that since the fact that Hashem conducts the world according to nature is always uniform [for every single day the sun rises in the east and sets in the west, and so too in all matters of nature], it is not openly apparent that Hashem is the One conducting the world, and there is room to err that the world conducts itself. Through the miracles where Hashem sometimes *changes* the conduct of nature, it is proven that even the conduct of nature is from Hashem. But according to this, the perfection of nature would be that it be openly apparent that nature is *Elokus*, yet the *maamar* says that through *avodah* (of *mesiras nefesh*), the revelation *of above nature* is drawn into nature.

3) It is explained in several *derushim* that the root of nature is from the name *Elokim* (*Elokim* in *gematria* is *HaTeva*), and the root of miracles is from the name *Havayah*. It is explained there that the name *Havayah* is *Da'as Elyon*, where above there is "is" and below there is "is not"—meaning that all existence is nothing and null, and *kula kami k'la chashiv*. The name *Elokim* is *Da'as Tachton*, where creation is *me'ayin l'yesh*—meaning that the created beings are a reality of "is" [and the *Elokus* power that brings them into being is called by the name *Ayin*, an *Ayin* that is not grasped]. [It can be said that this is similar to what is explained elsewhere, that the creation from the name *Elokim* is by way of *hitlabshus* (*Bereishis bara Elokim*), which indicates that it is a source for the matter of creation. Therefore, the created beings that come into being from it occupy space relative to it, and their *bitul* is only the *bitul* of "yesh"—*Da'as Tachton* where below there is "yesh." The creation from the name *Havayah* is in a manner of "by itself" (*yehalelu es shem Havayah ki hu tzivah v'nivrau*), which indicates that it is wondrously removed from the matter of creation, and therefore the created beings that come into being from it do not occupy space relative to it—*bitul b'metzius*—*Da'as Elyon* where below there is "is not"].

It is explained in the *derushim* that just as it is regarding the names—that in the name *Havayah* it is *Da'as Elyon* and in the name *Elokim* it is *Da'as Tachton*—so too it is regarding miracle and nature (which are drawn from *Havayah* and *Elokim*): the conduct of nature is from the perspective of *Da'as Tachton*, and the miracles that are above nature are from the perspective of *Da'as Elyon*. [It can be said that the connection of miracle and nature to *Da'as Elyon* and *Da'as Tachton* is not only from the perspective of their root—that the root of miracles is from *Havayah* and the root of nature is from *Elokim*—but also from the perspective of the matter of miracle and nature themselves. The matter is that the conduct of nature is that Hashem conducts every created being according to its nature, which indicates that in this conduct, the created beings occupy space—below there is "yesh." Miraculous conduct is that Hashem conducts the world according to His will and not according to the nature and characteristics of the created beings, for in this conduct, the created beings do not occupy space—below there is "is not"]. Through the miracles drawn into the world, whereby the revelation of above nature is drawn even into nature, the connection of *Da'as Tachton* and *Da'as Elyon* is achieved, to be "One in One", so that even in *Da'as Tachton*, the revelation of *Da'as Elyon* shines.

4) It is explained in the *derushim* that both views are true, for both have a root in the *Atzmus* of *Ohr Ein Sof*, for in *Ohr Ein Sof* there are two matters: that He is "higher and higher" to *Ein Ketz* and that He is "lower and lower" to *Ein Tachlis*. These two matters are the root of *Da'as Tachton* and *Da'as Elyon*. From the perspective of the revelation of *Ohr Ein Sof*, which is revealed everywhere "lower and lower" to *Ein Tachlis*, *kula kami k'la chashiv*—*Da'as Elyon* where below there is "is not." And from the perspective of the concealment of *Ohr Ein Sof*, which is elevated in His *Atzmus* "higher and higher" to *Ein Ketz*, the created beings are a reality of "yesh"—*Da'as Tachton* where below there is "yesh."

It must be understood that since the root of *Da'as Tachton* is also in the *Atzmus* of *Ohr Ein Sof*, the perfection of *Da'as Tachton* would seemingly be that it should have the revelation that the "below there is yesh" is (not that the created beings are a reality of "yesh" in and of themselves, but) because *Ohr Ein Sof* is "higher and higher" to *Ein Ketz*. The *derushim* explain that the perfection of *Da'as Tachton* is through the revelation of *Da'as Elyon* shining within it. It must also be understood, on the other hand, regarding what is stated in the *derushim* that even *Da'as Tachton* is true because it has a root in the *Atzmus* of *Ohr Ein Sof*: seemingly, since the matter of "higher and higher" to *Ein Ketz* (the root of *Da'as Tachton*) is the *concealment* of *Ohr Ein Sof*, the feeling of "below there is yesh" (as it is felt in *Da'as Tachton*) is seemingly not from *Ohr Ein Sof*, but on the contrary, because *Ohr Ein Sof* is *not* felt within it, and nevertheless it is said that the view that "below there is yesh" (as it is in *Da'as Tachton*) is a true view.

5) The matter is that it is explained in the *derushim* that there are several interpretations of "nature" (*teva*). In *Shu"t Chacham Tzvi*, it is stated that the name *teva* is a new name coined by the later sages. Since everything (even nature) is from Him, may He be blessed, nature is also a miracle. It is just that these miracles are constant, continuous miracles. Others say that *teva* is from the word *matbe'a* (coin/imprint). For it is stated in the *Mishnah* : "A person strikes many coins with one seal, and they are all similar to one another; but the King of Kings, the Holy One, blessed be He, imprinted every person with the seal of the first man, and not one of them is like his fellow." In *Tanya*, it is stated that *teva* is a name borrowed for anything that is not according to reason and intellect.

It is explained in the *derushim* that the three interpretations above are three matters. According to the *Chacham Tzvi*, who says *teva* is continuous miracles, the name *teva* refers to the conduct of the world. According to the interpretation that *teva* is from the word *matbe'a*—that Hashem imprinted every person with the seal of the first man, and the Rambam explains that the "seal of the first man" is the form of the human species through which a human is a human, meaning (primarily) the spiritual form within him —the name *teva* refers to the *Elokus* vitality in the world, which is the soul of the world. The connection of this matter to *teva* in its simple sense is that the design and nature of every created being is according to the manner of the *Elokus* vitality within it. According to the interpretation that *teva* is a name borrowed for anything that is not according to reason and intellect—the name *teva* refers to the *Elokus* power that brings every created being into being *me'ayin l'yesh*, for creation *me'ayin l'yesh* is above intellect. For the fact that *Elokus* vitality *animates* the created beings has a place in intellect, and the matter of *teva* (according to the interpretation that it is that which is not according to reason and intellect) is in the creation of the created beings, for creation *yesh me'ayin* is above intellect.

6) It is explained in the *derushim* that the two interpretations (matters) of *teva*—that it is the *Elokus* vitality that animates the created beings and that it is the *Elokus* power that brings the created beings into being *me'ayin l'yesh*—relate to the interpretation that *teva* is from the word *tvi'ah* (sinking/drowning), as in "they sank (*tavu*) in the Red Sea". Just as in sinking in its simple sense, when an object is sunk in water, the water covers the object sunk within it, so too it is with the *Elokus* vitality that is clothed in the created beings to animate them: the created beings cover the *Elokus* vitality that is clothed within them. And so too it is with the *Elokus* power that brings the created beings into being *me'ayin l'yesh*: the matter of *teva* within it is also from the word *tvi'ah*, for since the creation is in a manner of concealment and hiding, where the power that brings into being is hidden and concealed within the created beings, the matter of *teva* from the word *tvi'ah* and concealment applies to it.

It can be said that the reason the matter of *teva* from the word *tvi'ah* regarding the creation of the created beings is brought in the *derushim* in the style of a novelty [*that also* in this *it can be said* there is *teva* from the word *tvi'ah*] is because one of the differences between the vitality of the created beings and the creation of the created beings is that the *Elokus* vitality that animates the created beings is revealed within the created beings—it is felt in them that they have vitality, and the matter of concealment is only that it is not felt that the vitality within them is *Elokus* vitality —whereas the creation of the created beings (that they were created *me'ayin l'yesh*) is not felt in them at all. Not only is it not felt in them that the creation is from *Elokus*, but the very matter of their creation is not felt in them. The reason for this is that the *Elokus* vitality is *clothed* in the created beings through concealing garments. [And since it is clothed in the created beings, it is therefore felt in them, only that due to the concealing garments, it is not felt in them that it is *Elokus* vitality]. The *Elokus* power that brings the created beings into being is distinct from the created beings and is not clothed in them. [And therefore, their creation is not felt in the created beings].

Accordingly, it can be explained that the matter of *teva* from the word *tvi'ah* is primarily in the vitality of the created beings, for the matter of *tvi'ah* is that the sunk object is found in the water, and thereby the water covers it; in the analogy, the *Elokus* vitality is clothed in the created beings, and thereby they cover it. Whereas the concealment in the matter of creation is because the *Elokus* power that brings the created beings into being is distinct from them and not clothed in them. The matter of *teva* from the word *tvi'ah* regarding the power that brings into being is (primarily) in the matter of concealment (and not in the manner of concealment).

7) It is explained elsewhere that there are two matters in "form" (*tzurah*): the true form, which is the soul, and the material of the body, which is also called by the name "form," the form of the matter. Both matters relate to one another, for the form of the body is a vessel for the soul. Accordingly, even though the primary matter of the "seal of the first man" (the form of the human species) is the spiritual form (as mentioned above in section 5 from the *derushim*), this also relates to the form of the human body. From this, it is also understood regarding *teva* that the primary matter of *teva* (according to the interpretation that *teva* is from the word that Hashem imprinted every person with the seal of the first man) is the *Elokus* vitality of the world, which is the soul of the world, and this also relates to the form of the created beings, the form of the matter. For the form of the created beings is the vessel for the vitality and the soul within them. The matter is that it is written, "All that Hashem desired, He did" (that creation is from the perspective of desire and will), and it is written, "By the word of Hashem, the heavens were made" (that creation is from the perspective of speech). The explanation of this is that the creation of the matter of everything is from the perspective of speech. This is what the Sages said : "With ten utterances the world was created," for with the utterance "Let there be light," light was created, and with the utterance "Let there be a firmament," the firmament was created, and so too it is with all created beings. The creation of the form within everything is from the perspective of will. For the form of the created beings was not specified in the ten utterances, and the fact that every created being has a form [both its physical design, and its nature and characteristics] is from the perspective of will. And what is stated, "You made them all with wisdom," is because the drawing down of the will is through wisdom.

The reason that creation is from two matters, from the perspective of speech and from the perspective of will, is that the ultimate intent in the creation of the world is that the world (from its own perspective) should be a "yesh," and through the *avodah* of man (specifically) *bitul* should be drawn into it, until such *bitul* that the world becomes a dwelling place for Him, may He be blessed. Both matters in the world—that from its own perspective it is a "yesh," and that nevertheless it is possible to draw *bitul* into it —are through its creation being from the perspective of will and from the perspective of speech. For if all creation (even the creation of the matter of the created beings) were from the perspective of will, the world would be null in reality; and for the world to be in a reality of "yesh" is through it being created by the word of Hashem. And if all creation (even the creation of the form of the created beings) were from the perspective of speech, the world would not relate to the matter of *bitul*. Through the creation of the form of the created beings being from the perspective of will, as it is drawn into the perspective of speech [for the fact that the form of the created beings is the design *of the matter* is because the will is drawn *into the speech*], through this it is possible to draw down (through *avodah*) that even the "yesh" that was created from the speech should be in *bitul*.

8) It can be said that the reason the *bitul* of the created beings is drawn from the perspective of will and the "yesh-ness" of the created beings from the perspective of speech is because the will is not a reality and is only a revelation of the *Atzmus* (similar to the will in the human soul, which is only an inclination and drawing of the soul), and therefore the creation from the will also has the matter (not that created beings are created, but) that through the creation, the will is completed. Speech is a separate garment. For in addition to the fact that speech is a reality, since its matter is that it speaks to another, it is in this itself separate from the person speaking, until the speech goes out and is separate from the person in practice as well. And therefore, the creation from speech is a reality of "yesh."

9) According to what is explained above (section 7) that the form of the created beings (the form of the matter) is a vessel for the *Elokus* vitality within them (their soul), it can be said that the vitality and the soul of the created beings are the will. From this it is understood that the fact that the *Elokus* vitality is clothed in every created being and animates it according to its nature, which indicates that the created beings occupy space relative to it (as above in section 3), is [not because the created beings in and of themselves occupy space relative to it, for the matter of creation from the will is that through them the *will* is completed (as above in section 8), but] because it so arose in His will, may He be blessed, that there should be an occupation of space for the created beings, and therefore He is clothed in the created beings and animates every created being according to its nature.

Accordingly, the difference between natural conduct and miraculous conduct—that natural conduct is in *Da'as Tachton* and miraculous conduct is in *Da'as Elyon* (as mentioned above from the *derushim*)—is that the will in natural conduct is to conduct the world in a manner that the world is a reality, below there is "yesh," and the will in miraculous conduct is to conduct the world in a manner that the world is *k'la chashiv*, below there is "is not."

10) It can be said that so too it is in the general matter of *Da'as Tachton*, which has two modes: that the view that below there is "yesh" is because relative to this level, the "below" in and of itself is a reality (and its *bitul* is only the *bitul* of "yesh"); and that the view that below there is "yesh" is so that there should be a revelation of the matter of "higher and higher" to *Ein Ketz*. One of the differences between the two modes is that in the first mode, where the "below" in and of itself is a reality, *Da'as Tachton* and *Da'as Elyon* are two opposite views in their very essence. For *Da'as Tachton* is that the "below" is "yesh," and *Da'as Elyon* is that the "below" is "is not." And in the second mode, where the view that below there is "yesh" is so that there should be a revelation of the matter of "higher and higher" to *Ein Ketz*, the opposition of the two views is only in their depiction, but in their very essence they are one matter. For the matter of both views is the revelation of the matters in *Ohr Ein Sof*, only that *Da'as Elyon* is the revelation of "lower to *Ein Tachlis*," and *Da'as Tachton* is the revelation of "higher to *Ein Ketz*."

11) This is why even *Da'as Tachton* is true, and nevertheless there must be the connection of *Da'as Tachton* with *Da'as Elyon* (see above section 4), for the matter of the truth of *Da'as Tachton* is because its matter (in its *pnimiyus*) is the revelation of "higher to *Ein Ketz*," and this matter within it is revealed through its connection with *Da'as Elyon*.

So too it is in the matter of the conduct of nature; even though the conduct of nature is also that "You are the One who brought forth ten *tikkunim*, etc., to conduct the worlds through them," nevertheless, the perfection of the conduct of nature is when the revelation of above nature is drawn into it, for specifically through this it is revealed that the matter of "to conduct the worlds through them" has its root from the perspective of "You."

12) The fact explained above (section 10) that when it is felt in both views (of *Da'as Elyon* and *Da'as Tachton*) that their matter is to reveal the matters in *Ohr Ein Sof* (which is higher to *Ein Ketz* and lower to *Ein Tachlis*), the two views are one matter, is in a general sense. But in particular, even the matters of "higher to *Ein Ketz*" and "lower to *Ein Tachlis*" (as they are in *Ohr Ein Sof*) are two matters. Accordingly, for there to be the connection of *Da'as Tachton* with *Da'as Elyon*, and so too the connection of nature with above nature, it is through the revelation of the *Atzmus* that is above both. It can be said that this is what is stated in the *maamar* that the drawing down of the revelation of above nature into nature is through the *hemshech* from the level of "You are One," even though the revelation of above nature is also through the *hemshech* of the level of "You are higher," because the matter of above nature that is from the perspective of "You are higher"—above *Hishtalshelus*—is not drawn into nature (*Hishtalshelus*), and the drawing down of the revelation of above nature into nature is through the *hemshech* of the level of "You are One." This is also the reason why there must be the connection of the two matters of nature and above nature, for each of the two matters has its root in *Ohr Ein Sof*, and through the connection of the two matters is the *hemshech* of the *Atzmus*. That is, through this there is the possibility (also from the perspective of the world) that through *avodah* there will be in the world a *hemshech* of the revelation of the *Atzmus*, and through this the intent of the creation of the world (nature) is completed—that Hashem, may He be blessed, desired to have a dwelling place in the lower worlds.

## B's"d. Shabbos Parshas Vayeishev, Mevarchim HaChodesh Teves, 5715

"Blessed is He who performed miracles for our forefathers in those days, at this time." The Rebbe (Rashab) n"e explains in his maamar on this subject that "miracles" (*nissim*) is in the plural form, implying not only a multitude of miracles, but a multitude of *types* of miracles. One must understand: the concept of multiplicity (division) belongs to the realm of *Seder Hishtalshelus* (the chain of progression). Since the root of miracles is above *Seder Hishtalshelus*—as explained previously in the maamar *Pasach Eliyahu*, that the root of natural conduct is from the level of *Hishtalshelus* while the root of miracles is from above *Hishtalshelus*—what is the nature of this multiplicity (the division into levels) within miracles? Furthermore, one must understand what is explained at the end of that maamar regarding the phrase "for our forefathers": that "our forefathers" refers to *Chochma* and *Binah*, and the word *nes* (miracle) comes from the root *harama* (raising), as in "I will raise (*arim*) my banner (*nisi*)." Thus, the concept of "miracles for our forefathers" is a raising and elevation in *Chochma* and *Binah*. This requires explanation: what is the nature of this elevation in *Chochma* and *Binah*? And what is the connection between this elevation in *Chochma* and *Binah* and miracles in their literal sense?

2) The crux of the explanation is that the root of both types of conduct (natural conduct and miraculous conduct) is from the union of *Chochma* and *Binah*, for there are two aspects to the union of *Chochma* and *Binah*. There is the external *zivug* (union) of *Chochma* and *Binah*, which is constant for the sake of the existence of the worlds, and this is the root of natural conduct. Since this *zivug* is for the sake of the existence of the worlds, the conduct of the world that is drawn down from this level is such that Hashem leads the creatures in the world according to their nature—each creature according to its specific properties and nature. The root of miraculous conduct—where Hashem leads the world according to His will and not according to the properties and nature of the world—is from the internal *zivug* of *Chochma* and *Binah*. This is the connection between "miracles for our forefathers" and the interpretation that it refers to the raising and elevation of *Chochma* and *Binah* alongside miracles in their literal sense (the changing of nature). For the conduct of the world that is drawn from *Chochma* and *Binah* as they are in themselves (including as they unite in the external *zivug*) is natural conduct, while the drawing down of miracles (the changing of nature) occurs because a raising and elevation takes place in *Chochma* and *Binah*, as they unite in an internal *zivug*.

3) The explanation of this matter (the connection of miracles to the internal *zivug* of *Chochma* and *Binah*) in greater detail can be understood by prefacing what was explained earlier (in the maamar *Pasach Eliyahu*): that natural conduct—where Hashem leads every creature according to its nature, which indicates that in this conduct the creatures "take up space"—is in the category of *da'as tachton* (lower knowledge), where "below there is [existence]" (and "above there is not"). Miraculous conduct—where Hashem leads the world according to His will and not according to the properties and nature of the creatures, which indicates that in this conduct the creatures do not "take up space"—is in the category of *da'as elyon* (higher knowledge), where "above there is [existence]" (and "below there is not"). These two types of knowledge regarding the *Sefiros* are *Chochma* and *Binah*. In *Chochma*, it is felt that the *Ohr Ein Sof* is the true existence and "all is considered as nothing before Him"—this is *da'as elyon*, where above there is existence and below there is not. In *Binah*, which is *hasagah* (intellectual grasp)—and *hasagah* is a state of existence—it is *da'as tachton*. For even though in *Binah* one also grasps that "all is considered as nothing before Him," this is not *felt* within it. Since *hasagah* (even the *hasagah* that "all is considered as nothing before Him") is a state of existence, that existence (of the grasp) is the opposite of the feeling of "nothingness."

Furthermore, the fact that *Binah* is *da'as tachton*—where above there is not and below there is—also applies to the drawing down from *Chochma* to *Binah*. *Chochma* and *Binah* are *Ayin* (Nothingness) and *Yesh* (Existence), and the formation of *Binah* from *Chochma* is analogous to the formation of *Yesh* from *Ayin*. Just as in the formation of *Yesh* from *Ayin*, the *Ayin* is hidden from the *Yesh*—which is why it is called *Ayin*, because it is not grasped within the *Yesh* [and this fact, that the *Ayin* is not grasped within the *Yesh*, is not only because of the *Yesh* (which is incomparable to the *Ayin* and therefore incapable of grasping it), but also because of the *Ayin* itself, which conceals itself from the *Yesh*. As is known, the formation of *Yesh* from *Ayin* occurs because the Divine power (*Ayin*) that brings the creature (*Yesh*) into being conceals itself from the creature]—so too is it in the formation of *Binah* from *Chochma*. The fact that *Binah* is *Yesh* is because the *Ayin* of *Chochma* conceals itself from *Binah*. And even though the *hasagah* of *Binah* is in the "point" of *Chochma*—meaning *Binah* grasps the point of the intellectual concept—the essence of *Chochma* is the "vision" of *Chochma* which is above intellect, and this aspect is not drawn down into *Binah* at all. Furthermore, the intellectual grasp of *Chochma* (that is drawn into *Binah*) is primarily the *hanachas hanefesh* (the soul's settling/conviction), and the intellect within it is only the externality. What is grasped in *Binah* is only the intellect within the point of the concept (the externality), but the *hanachah* within the point of the concept (its internality and essence) is in the category of *Ayin* relative to *Binah*, and is not grasped within it.

Based on this, we can add an explanation as to why *da'as elyon* is specifically in *Chochma*, whereas in *Binah* (even though the concepts of *Chochma* are grasped there) it is *da'as tachton*. The reason *Chochma* is *da'as elyon*—where "below there is not" (the feeling that "all is considered as nothing before Him")—is not because of its intellect (for intellect is a state of existence, the opposite of the feeling of nothingness), but because of its internality and essence (which is analogous to the "vision" of *Chochma*) which is above intellect. Since only the intellect of *Chochma* is drawn into *Binah*, it remains *da'as tachton* in *Binah*.

Now, the fact that only the intellect of *Chochma* is drawn into *Binah* [which is why *Binah* is *da'as tachton*] applies to the externality of *Binah*. But in the internality of *Binah*, the essence of *Chochma* also shines, and through this, *Binah* also becomes a state of *da'as elyon*, because the feeling of "nothingness" that is in *Chochma* shines also in *Binah*. Based on this, we can explain why miracles are drawn from the internal *zivug* of *Chochma* and *Binah*: because miraculous conduct is in the category of *da'as elyon*, and since in the internal *zivug* (the drawing of *Chochma* into the internality of *Binah*), the higher knowledge that "below there is not"—that "all is considered as nothing before Him"—is felt even in *Binah*, therefore, the conduct drawn from the internal *zivug* of *Chochma* and *Binah* is miraculous conduct.

4) One must understand that according to the above, the primary superiority of the internal *zivug* (the root of miraculous conduct) over the external *zivug* (the root of natural conduct) is in *Binah*, where in the internal *zivug*, the feeling of "nothingness" (that is in *Chochma*) shines also in *Binah*. From the fact that we say regarding miracles (in their literal sense), "Blessed is He who performed miracles for our forefathers," and "our forefathers" are *Chochma* and *Binah*, it is understood that for miracles to occur in their literal sense, there must be a raising and elevation in *Chochma* as well.

The explanation for this is that the superiority of the internal *zivug* over the external *zivug* consists of two things. Regarding the aspect of revelation: in the externality of *Binah*, *Chochma* is in concealment—it is an *Ayin* that is not grasped—whereas in the internality of *Binah*, it is in revelation. There is a further superiority in the internal *zivug*: that the drawing of *Chochma* (in this *zivug*) is from a higher level—the internality of *Chochma*—which is above the level where *Chochma* is called *Ayin*.

The matter is that the fact that *Chochma* is *Ayin* is as it becomes a source for *Binah*. Since the formation of *Binah* from *Chochma* occurs because *Chochma* conceals itself from *Binah*, it is called *Ayin* (which is not grasped) at that point. But *Chochma* itself is a state of *Yesh*, and the fact that *Chochma* is a source for *Binah* (and certainly *Binah* itself) is *Ayin* relative to the essence of *Chochma*—the *Ayin* of "all is considered as nothing." Based on this, the level of *Chochma* that becomes a source for *Binah* is analogous to the knowledge of *da'as tachton* (where above there is not and below there is), and the level of the essence of *Chochma* that is above being a source for *Binah* is analogous to the knowledge of *da'as elyon* (where above there is and below there is not). [It can be said that there are two levels even in the *Chochma* that is above being a source for *Binah*: the essence of *Chochma* and the illumination of *Chochma*. For this aspect in *Chochma* (the feeling that the source for *Binah* is the *Ayin* of "all is considered as nothing") is the illumination of *Chochma* and not its essence, but this illumination is attached to the essence, and therefore it is felt within it that all matters that are not the essence of *Chochma* are "as nothing." The illumination of *Chochma* that becomes a source for *Binah* is a separate illumination, and therefore it is a source for *Binah*—analogous to the *Ayin* of the created *Yesh*, while the illumination of *Chochma* that is attached to the essence of *Chochma* is felt as the *Ayin* of "all is considered as nothing" relative to the essence of *Chochma*—analogous to the *Ayin* of the True *Yesh*.] One of the differences between the internal *zivug* and the external *zivug* is that in the external *zivug*, the drawing is from the externality of *Chochma*—that is, from the level of *Chochma* as it becomes a source for *Binah* (and this level of *Chochma* conceals itself from *Binah*, an *Ayin* that is not grasped)—whereas in the internal *zivug*, the drawing is from the internality of *Chochma*, and this drawing is *in revelation* within *Binah*. Since relative to the internality of *Chochma*, the source for *Binah*—and certainly *Binah* itself—are "as nothing," therefore, because the internality of *Chochma* is revealed in *Binah*, it is included in *Chochma*.

5) The fact explained above—that the essence of *Chochma* is *Yesh* and the illumination of *Chochma* is *Ayin* of "all is considered as nothing" relative to the essence of *Chochma*—are the two aspects of *Yesh* and *Ayin* within *Chochma* itself. But in general, the *Yesh* aspect of *Chochma* is that the *Ohr Ein Sof*, the True *Yesh*, dwells within *Chochma*, and the *Ayin* of *Chochma* is that the entirety of *Chochma* (even the essence of *Chochma*) is in *bitul* (nullification) to the *Ohr Ein Sof*, where it is felt that above there is existence and below there is not, which is the *Ayin* of the True *Yesh*. However, the fact that *Chochma* becomes a source for *Binah* is the *Ayin* of the created *Yesh*. Based on this, the fact that the external *zivug* of *Chochma* and *Binah* is *da'as tachton* is also from the perspective of *Chochma*. The explanation given above (section 3) that *Chochma* is *da'as elyon* (and only *Binah* is *da'as tachton* because only the intellect of *Chochma* is drawn into *Binah*) is in a general sense. But specifically, the aspect of *da'as tachton* in the external *zivug* is also from the perspective of *Chochma*, since *Chochma* (in the external *zivug*) is the *Ayin* of the created *Yesh*. The primary aspect of *da'as elyon* is in the internal *zivug*, where *Chochma* (in this *zivug*) is the *Ayin* of the True *Yesh*, and *Binah* is also included in *Chochma*.

Based on this, we can explain the phrase "Blessed is He who performed miracles for our forefathers" (in the plural), that the raising and elevation (from which miracles are drawn) is also in *Chochma*. For *Chochma* in its own right (as it is in the external *zivug*) is a source for *Binah*—the *Ayin* of the created *Yesh*—and the drawing of miracles is from the level of the essence of *Chochma* which is above being a source for *Binah*—the *Ayin* of the True *Yesh*.

6) Based on what was explained above (section 4) that the fact that *Chochma* becomes a source for *Binah* is *Ayin* relative to the essence of *Chochma* (the *Ayin* of "all is considered as nothing"), it can be said that the raising and elevation in the level of *Chochma* that becomes a source for *Binah* is included in the raising and elevation of *Binah*. The reason "miracles for our forefathers" is in the plural—that the raising and elevation brought about by miracles is in both *mochin* (intellects), *Binah* and *Chochma*—means that through miracles, a raising and elevation is also effected in the essence of *Chochma* which is above being a source for *Binah*.

This can be understood by prefacing what was brought earlier (in the maamar *Pasach Eliyahu*), that both types of knowledge (*da'as elyon* and *da'as tachton*) are true, because both have a root in the *Atzmus* (Essence) of the *Ohr Ein Sof*. The fact that the *Ohr Ein Sof* is (in revelation) down below to the point of no end is the root of *da'as elyon* where "below there is not," and the fact that the *Ohr Ein Sof* is (in concealment) up above to the point of no end is the root of *da'as tachton* where "below there is [existence]." It was explained there that the reason *da'as tachton* is also true because it has a root in the *Atzmus* of the *Ohr Ein Sof* is that the knowledge that "below there is existence" is (*not* that for this level the "below" is a reality in its own right, but) in order that there be the revelation of "up above to the point of no end." The perfection of *da'as tachton* is through the revelation of *da'as elyon* shining within it, for on the surface it appears that the knowledge that "below there is existence" means the "below" is a reality in its own right, but through its connection with *da'as elyon*, it is revealed that its essence (internally) is the revelation of "up above to the point of no end."

The same applies to the concept of *da'as tachton* in natural conduct. The fact that Hashem leads every creature according to its nature (according to its temperament and properties), which indicates that the creatures "take up space," is (not that the creatures take up space in their own right before Him, but) because it arose in His will, may He be blessed, that the creatures should take up space. The difference between natural conduct and miraculous conduct is that the will in natural conduct is to lead the world in a way that the world is a reality, and the will in miraculous conduct is to lead the world in a way that the world is "as nothing." The perfection of natural conduct is through the revelation of that which is above nature being drawn into it, for specifically through this is the internality of natural conduct revealed—that it is because it arose in His will, may He be blessed.

It can be said that the same applies to *da'as elyon*, which has two modes. On the surface, it appears that the fact that "below there is not" is because the "below" *in its own right* is the *Ayin* of "all is considered as nothing," while internally, it is that "below there is not" is in order to reveal that the *Ohr Ein Sof* is down below to the point of no end. Through *da'as elyon* being drawn into *da'as tachton* and connecting with it, its internality is revealed. The same applies to miracles and nature: through the drawing of miracles into the world (nature), their internality is revealed—that the fact that in conduct above nature the creatures do not take up space is because it arose in His will, may He be blessed. This is the meaning of "Blessed is He who performed miracles for our forefathers"—"for our forefathers" in the plural—that the raising and elevation through the performance of miracles is in both *Binah* (the root of nature) and *Chochma* (the root of that which is above nature), and both matters are not for their own sake, but to reveal that the *Ohr Ein Sof* is up above to the point of no end and down below to the point of no end.

7) Now, in order for there to be a connection between the two matters—that in the nature of the world [where it is emphasized that the world is a reality, for the fact that Hashem leads every creature according to its nature indicates that in this conduct the creatures take up space] there should be the revelation of that which is above nature [where it is emphasized that the world is nullified, for the fact that Hashem leads the world according to His will and not according to the properties and nature of the creatures indicates that in this conduct the creatures do not take up space]—it must be through there being an aspect of *bitul* within nature itself. As explained earlier (in the maamar *Pasach Eliyahu*), the fact that the form of the creatures was not specified in the Ten Utterances, and the formation of the form in every creature is from the aspect of the Will, indicates the *bitul* of the world. For the formation from the Will is (not that creatures are formed, but) that through the formation, the Will is fulfilled. Because the formation of the form in every creature—and the intention in the form of the creature is (both its physical shape and) also its nature and properties—was from the aspect of the Will, this emphasizes that the essence of nature (internally) is that through it, the Will of Hashem is fulfilled.

It was explained earlier (in the maamar *Pasach Eliyahu*) that according to the interpretation that *teva* (nature) comes from the word *matbeia* (coin/imprint), that Hashem imprinted every person with the seal of Adam HaRishon, *teva* there refers to the *chiyus* (vitality) of the world. According to the interpretation that *teva* is a term borrowed for anything that is not according to reason and intellect, *teva* there refers to the formation of the world from *Ayin* to *Yesh*, for creation *Yesh* from *Ayin* (unlike the matter of *chiyus*) is above intellect. It can be said that the *bitul* within nature relates to both matters. The *bitul* that belongs to the *chiyus* of the world is revealed in the *bitul* of the form of the creatures, for the form of the creatures is a vessel for the *chiyus* and the soul within them. The *bitul* that belongs to the formation of the world is revealed in the fact that the formation of *Yesh* from *Ayin* has no place in intellect. The fact that there is no place in intellect for the formation of *Yesh* from *Ayin* indicates that from the perspective of the definition of the world, there is no place for its formation, and the fact that the world was formed is only from the Will of Hashem.

This is why there are two modes in miracles: a change in the form and properties of the creatures (like the splitting of the Red Sea, where the waters stood like a wall) and a change in the essence of their existence in their material (like the miracle where water turned into blood and the staff turned into a snake). Through the *bitul* in the form of the creatures (that the formation of the form is from the Will), they are like vessels for the miracle of the change of form and nature, and through the *bitul* in the essence of their existence (that the formation of their existence has no place in intellect), they are like vessels for the miracle of the change of the essence of their existence.

8) It can be said that the same applies to the reverse: that for that which is above nature to be drawn into the nature of the world, it is through there being an aspect of nature within that which is above nature. This is "Blessed is He who performed miracles" in the plural—a multitude of *types* of miracles. It can be said that one of the reasons for the multitude of miracles, even though the concept of multiplicity (division) is in *Hishtalshelus* and the root of that which is above nature is above *Hishtalshelus*, is so that through this it would be possible for the drawing of that which is above nature into the nature of the world.

The Rebbe (Rashab) n"e explains in the maamar that, in general, they are divided into two types: miracles that are above nature, and miracles that are clothed in the ways of nature. Specifically, even within miracles clothed in nature, there are two types. There are those where the garments of nature are such that it is recognizable that they are only a garment for the miracle clothed within them, like the miracles of Chanukah and Purim (and similarly the miracles of the redemption of 19 Kislev and 12 Tammuz), where even though they were clothed in the ways of nature, the miracle within them was clearly recognizable (to the point that all the ends of the earth saw it). And there are those where the garments of nature conceal and hide the miracle clothed within them, to the point that sometimes they conceal and hide it completely, such that it is not recognizable at all that it is a miracle, and the beneficiary of the miracle does not recognize his miracle.

To add: even within miracles that are above nature, there are two types. Even though the miracles are not clothed in nature, there is still room to err that they are nature. Like the descent of the *Man* for forty years; even though the miracle was not clothed in nature [and as the Rambam writes, that the stay of Israel in the desert for forty years and the existence of the *Man* there is one of the great wonders, for the nature of the desert is not a place for seed, etc., and it is impossible for there to be bread there (neither bread from the earth nor bread from heaven). And certainly the fact that there was no waste, and all the more so the fact that he who gathered little did not lack—which from the perspective of the nature of the world has no place], nevertheless, because the miracle lasted for forty years, there was room to err that it was nature. And there is the miracle that is so revealed that there is no room at all to err that it is nature, like the miracle of the splitting of the Red Sea.

9) The maamar continues that regarding the difference between the two types (miracles above nature and miracles clothed in nature) in their root, there are two explanations. The root of miracles above nature is from the Name *Havayah*, and the root of miracles clothed in nature is from the Name *Shaddai*, "Who is sufficient (*shaddai*) in My G-dliness for every creature." [The difference between the two modes in miracles clothed in nature itself is that the Name *Shaddai* is in *Yesod*, and its drawing down below is through *Malchus*. In this there are two modes: that their drawing down through *Malchus* is by way of passing through, or by way of clothing. These are the root of the two modes in miracles.] According to this explanation, the difference between the two types of miracles is (also) in their root. Another explanation is that miracles clothed in nature are from the aspect of *Ani* (I) as it is clothed in the *Ayin* of *Malchus*. (This is similar to what is explained elsewhere, that the root of miracles clothed in nature is from the Name *Havayah* as it is clothed in the Name *Adonai*.)

Based on what was explained above (section 8) that the multitude of types of miracles is above *Hishtalshelus* (that in order for them to be drawn into *Hishtalshelus* there is multiplicity above *Hishtalshelus*), it can be said that the four types of miracles have their root in the four letters of the Name *Havayah*. The miracles drawn from the letters *Vav-Hei*, which relate to the worlds (*Vav-Hei* are the revealed), are clothed in nature. Just as in the letters *Vav-Hei*, the *Vav* is a single line drawn from above to below—meaning that even when it is drawn below, it is connected with above—and the *Hei* is in the form of length and width (in which only the surface of the receiver is recognizable), so too is it with the miracles drawn from the letters *Vav-Hei*. The miracles drawn from the *Vav*, even after they are drawn and clothed in the garments of nature, it is clearly recognizable that they are a miracle. The miracles drawn from the final *Hei*, after they are drawn and clothed in the garments of nature, only the nature in which the miracles are clothed (the surface of the receiver) is clearly recognizable. The miracles drawn from the letters *Yud-Hei*, which are above relation to the worlds (*Yud-Hei* are the hidden), are above clothing in nature, and within them themselves there are two types. The letter *Hei*, which has the form of length and width, through which it is possible for there to be a "grasp"—therefore, in the level of miracles drawn from it, there is room for nature. Even though the miracles themselves are not clothed in nature, nevertheless, relative to this level, nature is not excluded, and therefore there is room to err that they are nature. Relative to the level of miracles drawn from the letter *Yud*, which is only a point that has no "grasp," there is no place for nature, and therefore in these miracles it is not possible to err that they are nature.

10) The maamar explains (at its conclusion and seal) that the phrase "Blessed is He who performed miracles... at this time" is a request that there be miracles also at this time. Based on what the Alter Rebbe (the master of the celebration and redemption of 19 Kislev) writes in *Tanya* regarding the request "Forgive us," that since we bless (after the request) "Blessed are You, Hashem, the gracious One who forgives abundantly," there is no doubt about it—it is understood that the same applies to the request that Hashem perform miracles also at this time. Since *we bless* "Blessed... who performed miracles... at this time," there is no doubt that at this time, in the near future, Hashem will perform miracles in the plural—all four types mentioned above, including the miracles of "I will show him wonders" that will occur with the coming of Moshiach Tzidkeinu—that these miracles too will be at this time, in the very near future, with the coming of Moshiach Tzidkeinu speedily in our days, truly.

## 19 Kislev, 5726

"He redeemed my soul in peace from those who draw near to me, for there were many with me." Our Rebbe (the master of the redemption and the joy) writes: "When I read in the Book of Psalms the verse, 'He redeemed my soul in peace,' before I began the following verse, I emerged in peace from the 'Hashem of peace.'" It is understood from this that the content of the verse relates to the redemption of our Rebbe. Now, the redemption of our Rebbe was the matter of spreading the wellsprings (of the teachings of Chassidus) to the "outside." As is known, the cause of the accusation and imprisonment below was due to the debate above regarding the dissemination of the secrets of Torah in a revealed manner, to the point that it would reach everyone. When the verdict was issued above to disseminate the secrets of Torah (and, on the contrary, with greater intensity and strength than before the imprisonment), the judgment below was nullified of its own accord, and he went out to freedom. From this it is understood that the content of the verse, "He redeemed my soul in peace," relates to the dissemination of the teachings of Chassidus. Now, our Sages of blessed memory said, "'He redeemed my soul in peace from those who draw near to me'—the Holy One, blessed be He, says: Whoever engages in Torah, performs acts of loving-kindness, and prays with the congregation, I account it to him as if he redeemed Me and My children from among the nations of the world." Based on this, it can be said that in addition to the connection of disseminating the teachings of Chassidus to the content of the verse as it is in its literal sense (for "a verse does not depart from its literal meaning")—redemption in peace—it (the dissemination of Chassidus) also relates to the matter of one who engages in Torah, performs acts of loving-kindness, and prays with the congregation.

2) The simple interpretation of this verse is that "for there were many with me" provides the reason for "He redeemed my soul in peace." That is to say, it is possible for a redemption to occur in a manner of war, and the virtue of this redemption is that it was "redeemed in peace." The reason for this—that He redeemed my soul in peace—is "for there were many with me." We must understand what the concept of "redeemed in peace" specifically entails, and the connection of "for there were many with me" to "redeemed in peace." Now, in the exposition of our Sages of blessed memory on this verse—"Whoever engages in Torah, performs acts of loving-kindness, and prays with the congregation, etc."—Rashi explains: Torah, as it is written, "All her paths are peace"; likewise, acts of loving-kindness are also peace; and "prays with the congregation" is the matter of "for there were many with me" (as Rashi explains there, "for there were many with me," meaning they prayed with me). This means that these three matters—Torah, acts of loving-kindness, and prayer with the congregation—are divided into two categories: Torah and acts of loving-kindness are the matter of "in peace," and prayer with the congregation is the matter of "for there were many with me." We must understand why these three matters are divided into two categories. We must also understand the expression "Whoever engages in...," which speaks here of the person who engages in Torah, etc., and not of the matters of Torah, acts of loving-kindness, and prayer with the congregation themselves. As is the common phrasing in many places, such as "The world stands on three things: on Torah, on service, and on acts of loving-kindness" (and not "whoever engages in Torah, service, and acts of loving-kindness sustains the world"), whereas the phrasing here is "Whoever engages in..."

3) This will be understood by prefacing what is written, "All Your works shall praise You, Hashem, and Your pious ones shall bless You." It is stated in the Zohar that the interpretation of "shall bless You" (*yevarchucha*) is "they shall bless *Koh*." The Rebbe (Rashab), of blessed memory, explains (in his maamar entitled *Padah B'Shalom* of the year 5667) that *Koh* is Malchus, and "bless *Koh*" is to draw forth a revelation of light into Malchus. And it is said, "Your pious ones shall bless You," meaning this drawing forth is specifically through the power of Your pious ones. The matter is that this—that "Your pious ones shall bless You"—is in proximity and continuation to "All Your works shall praise You, Hashem," because the drawing forth into Malchus (which is through Your pious ones) is from a very lofty place (the Ohr Ein Sof that is above the Seder Hishtalshelus) where there is no possibility of apprehension, only acknowledgment (*hoda'ah*) alone—"shall praise You." And the drawing forth from there (in the manner of a blessing) is specifically through the power of Your pious ones.

In order to understand the greatness of the elevation of drawing forth in the manner of a blessing from the place where there is acknowledgment, the maamar prefaces the distinction between a blessing and prayer, where each has a virtue over the other. The matter is that prayer is from below to above—meaning the one who prays is below and requests that they bestow upon him from above—whereas a blessing is from above to below, where the one who blesses is above the root from which the blessing is drawn and draws it forth from above to below. This is why a blessing is in the way of a command; since the one who blesses is above the root from which the blessing is drawn, it is within his power to command. This is why a blessing is specifically for one who has the power to bless (like Avraham Avinu, to whom the Holy One, blessed be He, said, "The blessings are given to you"), [and unlike prayer, which everyone can pray, and furthermore, everyone is *commanded* to pray], because in order to bless, the one who blesses must be above the root from which the blessing is drawn. From this, the virtue of a blessing over prayer is understood: in prayer, which is a *request* that the influence be drawn forth, there is no certainty that the request will be fulfilled. But in a blessing, which is in the way of a command, the influence *will certainly* be drawn forth, since the one who blesses has the power to command. The virtue of prayer over a blessing is in the drawing forth itself, for the drawing forth through prayer is more elevated. The matter of a blessing is to draw forth that which is already present in the source, not something new, whereas the matter of prayer is a request from the Holy One, blessed be He, that even if the influence is not changed in the source, G-d forbid—and furthermore, even if it is *decreed* upon him that he be ill, G-d forbid, or the like—nevertheless, a new influence will be drawn forth for him from the Ohr Ein Sof that is above the Seder Hishtalshelus. This is what we say in many prayers, *Yehi Ratzon* ("May it be Your will"); the interpretation of *Yehi* Ratzon is that there should be a *new* will. The reason for this—that the drawing forth of a new light is specifically through prayer—is because "Your hands yearn for the work of Your hands," and therefore, through prayer—the service of the lower realm—one reaches the Ohr Ein Sof that is above the Seder Hishtalshelus.

Based on this, the greatness of the elevation of the blessing (of "Your pious ones") is understood, for its drawing forth is from the place where there is acknowledgment; in this blessing, there are both virtues. The drawing forth is from the Ohr Ein Sof that is above the Seder Hishtalshelus—meaning that through this blessing, a new will is created—which is the virtue of prayer. And yet, it is (not prayer, a request, but) a *blessing*, in the way of a command. This is similar to what is stated in the Jerusalem Talmud regarding the verse, "You shall decree a word and it shall be established for you": even if He (the Holy One, blessed be He) says one thing and you say another, "yours shall stand and Mine shall not stand." The fact that the Holy One, blessed be He, nullifies His decree because of the decree of the Tzaddik is because the Tzaddik draws forth a new will—the virtue of prayer—and the manner of the nullification of the decree is (not through a request, but) through the *decree* of the Tzaddik—the virtue of a blessing, which is in the way of a command.

4) The maamar continues that the drawing forth through the blessing of "Your pious ones" is similar to the drawing forth through Torah. Through Torah, a *change* and renewal is made in the world [meaning that a new thing is created that did not exist even in the root and source of the world], and the change and renewal is (not in the way of a request, as in prayer, but) in the way of "of its own accord." Like Rashbi, who caused rain to fall through the utterance of Torah; for in order for there to be rain after it was *decreed* that there be a drought, it is through the drawing forth of a new will, and yet, through the utterance of Torah, the drawing forth was in the way of "of its own accord," without service and toil. It can be said that what is stated in the maamar—that the drawing forth through the blessing of "Your pious ones" is like the drawing forth through Torah—is not only to provide an example of a drawing forth that possesses both virtues (of prayer and of blessing), but also because through this example, an explanation is added regarding the elevation of "Your pious ones shall bless You."

The matter is that it is explained in *Likutei Torah* regarding the immense miracles performed by several Tannaim and Amoraim, such as Rav Pappa bar Shmuel, who said, "River Ginai, divide your waters for me," that through their engagement in Torah, they drew forth a revelation of the Ohr Ein Sof that is above the Seder Hishtalshelus. Based on this, in the drawing forth through the engagement in Torah of all the Tannaim and Amoraim, there were also the two aforementioned matters (virtues). Through their engagement in Torah, a change and renewal was made in the world [a true renewal that did not exist even in the root and source of the world], and like the dividing of the river, which is a change and renewal even relative to the root and source of the river —the virtue of prayer—and that the change was made (not through a request, but) through the *command* of Rav Pappa bar Shmuel—the virtue of a blessing, which is in the way of a command. It can be said that the innovation in this—that Rashbi caused rain to fall through the utterance of Torah, relative to the immense miracles performed by several Tannaim and Amoraim through their engagement in Torah—is that the change in the world made by all the Tannaim and Amoraim was through their *command and decree*, such as Rav Pappa bar Shmuel *who commanded and decreed* upon the river to divide its waters, whereas the innovation of the rain falling through the utterance of Torah by Rashbi is that even though there was no command at all in the discourse he said (not even by allusion), nevertheless, through his utterance of Torah, the rain fell. Based on this, it can be said that the fact that the maamar brings the example of the rain falling through the utterance of Torah is to hint that the drawing forth through the blessing of "Your pious ones shall bless You" is similar to this (as will be explained in section 12).

5) The maamar explains the connection of "shall bless *Koh*" [the drawing forth into Malchus (*Koh*) from the Ohr Ein Sof that is above the Seder Hishtalshelus] to "Your pious ones," based on what the Rambam writes regarding the distinction between *chesed* (kindness) and *tzedakah* (charity): *tzedakah* is the matter of equity (*yosher*—*tzedakah* comes from the word *tzedek* and *yosher*) to give to each person according to what is fitting for him, whereas *chesed* is the excess of goodness, to benefit more than what is fitting. The maamar brings that what the Rambam says—that *tzedakah* is the influence to each person according to what is fitting for him—is like what is written (regarding the mitzvah of *tzedakah*) "sufficient for his need, which he lacks," and our Sages of blessed memory said, "sufficient for his need—and you are not commanded to make him rich." And *chesed* [which is the excess of goodness to benefit more than what is fitting] is influence in abundance (above the filling of the lack), wealth. And similarly, it is above in the drawing forth into the Sefirah of Malchus: the matter of *tzedakah* is the drawing forth of the revelation that belongs to Malchus, through which her lack is filled. [And as is known regarding the matter of "You have made the heavens, etc.," *At* (You) is missing a *Heh*, for the fact that Malchus becomes a source to bring the worlds into being ("You have made the heavens, etc.") is because the five Partzufim (Keter, Chochma, Binah, Ze'er Anpin, and Malchus) were lacking from her, and what remained is only the externality of Malchus, the Malchus of Malchus.] And the matter of *chesed* is the drawing forth of a revelation of light that is above belonging to Malchus, which is drawn into Malchus. This is what our Sages of blessed memory said, "Who is a *Chassid*? He who acts kindly with his *Kono*," and it is explained in *Tikkunei Zohar* that "with his *Kono*" means "with his *Ken* (nest) of his." *Kono* is Ze'er Anpin (for Atzilus is called *Kinyan*, and Ze'er Anpin is the end of Atzilus), and "his *Ken*" is Malchus (as is written in *Pardes* that Malchus is called a *Ken*), and the matter of "acting kindly with his *Ken* of his" is that he draws into Malchus a revelation of a new light (that is above the light that departed from her), wealth. And in the matter of the Sefiros, it is the internality of Keter, *Atik*. For the drawing forth of the externality of Keter, *Arich*, is in the category of filling the lack. Since *Arich* is the root and source of the emanated beings, but it surrounds them (*sovev*), it is in the category of the light that belongs to the worlds (Malchus), and therefore, when this light is not revealed in Malchus, Malchus is lacking, and through it being drawn forth and revealed, the lack is filled. And *Atik* is the lower aspect of the Emanator, the Ohr Ein Sof that is above belonging to the worlds (above even belonging in the manner of *sovev* and surrounding), wealth. 

6) The maamar continues that it is stated in the Gemara that *tzedakah* is for the poor, and acts of loving-kindness are for both the poor and the rich. From this (that acts of loving-kindness are also for the rich) it is understood that the influence that comes from *chesed* is above the level of "rich." It should be noted that, seemingly, one could say that the influence of *chesed* to the rich is that they bestow upon him wealth greater than the wealth he had before. What the maamar says—that the influence that comes from *chesed* is *above* the level of "rich"—is because when the influence they bestow upon him now is a matter that is within his capacity, then, since he previously lacked the matter they are bestowing upon him (even though the matter is within his capacity), he is not (the true matter of) "rich." Therefore, the maamar explains that acts of loving-kindness *for the rich* is that the abundance that comes from *chesed* is above the level of "rich," meaning that it is in a completely different category (and the fact that he has no matter that is not within his capacity at all is not a lack in his wealth).

7) In order to explain the matter of *chesed* that is above wealth, the maamar prefaces an explanation of the matter of the wealth that existed in the days of Shlomo (the most elevated level of wealth). It explains that the fact that in the days of Shlomo "the moon stood in its fullness" is because, in addition to the fact that the five Partzufim that were lacking were drawn into Malchus (the moon, *sihra*), the revelation of the internality of Keter, *Atik*, was also drawn into her. And from the wealth of Malchus (Knesses Yisrael), immense wealth was drawn to Yisrael below as well—immense wealth in physicality, to the point that "silver was not considered as anything in the days of Shlomo," and immense wealth in spirituality, for it was specifically in the days of Shlomo that the Beis Hamikdash was built, for the revelation that was in the Beis Hamikdash was a more elevated revelation than the revelation that was in the Mishkan that existed before. To add: the fact that in the days of Shlomo there was a most elevated revelation is also before the Beis Hamikdash was built. For the fact that the building of the Beis Hamikdash was specifically through Shlomo [even though the plan of the Temple was made by David] is as it is written, "He (Shlomo) shall be a man of rest, etc., and I will give peace and quiet upon Yisrael in his days (and therefore) he shall build a house for My name, etc." It is known that the fact that all the nations were subservient to Shlomo [which is why there was rest and peace in his days] is that they were subservient to the revelation of the Ohr Ein Sof that shone through him. And similarly regarding the fact that they came to him from all the nations to hear his wisdom; the true reason for this—that they came to him—is because they were drawn to Elokus (similar to what is written regarding Mashiach, "And all the nations shall flow unto him," for the fact that they will all be drawn to him is that they will be drawn to Elokus). For the revelation of light that shone then drew to itself the sparks that were in the nations, like a torch that draws to itself all the sparks. And since the revelation that shone then was a most elevated revelation, therefore, even the sparks in the distant places were drawn to the revelation that shone through Shlomo, as it is written, "And the Queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Shlomo *for the name of Hashem*, etc., and she came to Yerushalayim, etc."

8) Now, the fact that Shlomo was a man of rest—that the clarification in his days was in the way of rest [that there was no need for war in order to clarify the sparks, but rather the sparks were drawn to him of their own accord, to be clarified]—was primarily through the revelation that shone in the Beis Hamikdash. For the clarification through the revelation that shone in the Mishkan, including also the revelation that shone in the Ark that was in the Mishkan, was in the way of war, and the clarification through the revelation that shone in the Beis Hamikdash was in the way of rest. As it is stated in the Zohar, "There were two who said 'Arise, Hashem': Moshe and David. Moshe said, 'Arise, Hashem, and Your enemies shall be scattered,' and David said, 'Arise, Hashem, to Your resting place.'" The maamar explains that the fact that the Mishkan and the Ark would travel in the desert in order to nullify the Kelipos that were in the desert (snakes, fiery serpents, and scorpions) and to clarify and elevate the sparks of holiness that fell there, is a clarification in the way of war. Like a king who leaves his place and goes to the enemy's land to fight him and conquer him. Therefore, Moshe said [regarding the traveling of the Ark in the desert—"And it came to pass when the Ark traveled"] "Arise, Hashem, and Your enemies shall be scattered," for there are enemies that one must fight. But the clarification through the revelation that shone in the Beis Hamikdash was in the way of rest, for Shlomo was in his place and the sparks were drawn to him of their own accord. Therefore, David said, "Arise, Hashem, *to Your resting place*." It should be noted that in many places it is explained that the clarification that was through the traveling of the Ark in the desert is a clarification in the way of rest. For the matter of rest explained there is that the clarification is in the way of "of its own accord." And since the nullification of the Kelipos and the clarification of the sparks that fell into them through the revelation that shone in the Mishkan was in the way of "of its own accord," therefore, the clarification through the Ark of the Mishkan is also called a clarification in the way of rest. But the true matter of rest is that the one who clarifies does not descend to the place of that which is being clarified, but is found in his own place, and through the revelation of light (of the one who clarifies) that shines upon that which is being clarified, it is drawn by itself to the one who clarifies. And rest in this manner existed specifically in the days of Shlomo. [Similar to what is known regarding the matter of the clarification through Torah, that in general, even the clarification through the revealed Torah is in the way of rest. For since the clarification is in the way of "of its own accord" (for through learning in Torah that this thing is permitted and this thing is forbidden, they are clarified of their own accord—and not like the clarification through prayer, which is through the Nefesh Elokis being enclothed in the Nefesh Habahamis), therefore, the clarification through the revealed Torah is also called a clarification in the way of rest. But specifically, since the clarification through the revealed Torah is through it descending and being enclothed in physical matters, this is a matter of war [similar to the king leaving his place and going to the place of the enemy], and the true matter of clarification in the way of rest is in the clarification through the internality of Torah.]

9) It can be said that the difference between the "Arise, Hashem" that Moshe said and the "Arise, Hashem" that David said is in two matters: in the *manner* of the clarification [for the clarification through the Ark of the Mishkan was in the way of war, which is why it is said, "Your *enemies* shall be scattered" and "Your *haters* shall flee," for there are opponents (your enemies and your haters) that one must fight, whereas the clarification in the days of Shlomo, and specifically through the revelation of the Beis Hamikdash, was in the way of rest, "Arise, Hashem, to Your resting place," as explained at length above], and also in the clarification *itself*. For the fact that in the "Arise, Hashem" that Moshe said it is stated "*Your enemies* shall be scattered" and "*Your haters* shall flee" is because even after the clarification through the traveling of the Ark in the desert, opponents still remained—your enemies and your haters—only they were in a manner that they would be scattered and flee. The matter is that in a clarification in the way of from above to below, where the higher (the one who clarifies) is not enclothed in the lower, and the clarification in the lower is through the revelation of the light of the higher, the fact that the lower is not an opponent is not from its own reality (will, intellect, etc.), but on the contrary, because through the revelation of light that is not in its capacity shining into it, it *is nullified* from its reality. Now, when the clarification is in a manner where the higher (the one who clarifies) comes to the place of the lower (that which is being clarified), this proves that the lower occupies a place relative to it, and therefore it is not possible that through its revelation the lower will be nullified completely. And since this clarification is also in the way of from above to below and not in the way of enclothement (as in section 8), and the nullification through this revelation of light is not a complete nullification, therefore, even after the clarification, the reality of the opponent (your enemies and your haters) still remains (in concealment), only that nevertheless, since the lower is not in the capacity even relative to this revelation that it has a place relative to it, therefore, even through this revelation the strength of the lower is nullified, and its opposition (your enemies and your haters) is in a manner that they are scattered and flee. This is unlike the clarification in the way of rest, for the fact that the higher (the one who clarifies) is in his place and does not descend to the place of the lower is because the lower does not occupy a place relative to it—through this revelation of light, the nullification of the lower is a complete nullification.

It can be said more deeply that even when the clarification is in a manner where the one who clarifies is in his place, but he is at a level such that that which is being clarified has a place relative to him, the nullification of the opponent is not a complete nullification. Based on this, one can add an explanation to the fact that it is implied in the maamar that the fact that Shlomo was a man of rest is because in his days "the moon stood in its fullness," wealth that is above the filling of the lack, for the *true* matter of rest [and peace] is when the opponent is nullified completely, and in order for the opponent to be nullified completely, it is through the drawing forth of the matter of wealth, the Ohr Ein Sof that is above belonging to the worlds, *Atik*. For in the light that belongs to the worlds, including also the light of *Sovev*, Keter, since this light gives a place to the worlds—*olam* comes from the word *elem* (concealment) and *hester* (hiding) —the nullification of the opponents that are in the world through the revelation of this light is not a complete nullification. And the fact that Shlomo was a man of rest and peace was in his days is because in his days there was the drawing forth of the aspect of *Atik*, wealth.

10) The maamar continues that the matter of *chesed* that is above even wealth [which is why acts of loving-kindness are also for the rich] is the revelation of the time to come, which is more elevated even than the revelation that was in the days of Shlomo. For the drawing forth that was in the days of Shlomo was from the aspect of *Atik* (as above), and in the time to come the drawing forth will be from the aspect of the internality of *Atik*, meaning the *Ein Sof* that is in *Radla*, which is the *essence* of the matter of *Atik*. It should be noted what is written in the Zohar (in *Idra Zuta*): "This *Atik* is found in three heads," and R. Chaim Vital explains (in the explanation of *Idra Zuta*) that the essence of *Atik* is the *Ein Sof*, and because of its concealment in the three heads, the three heads are also called by the name *Atik*.
