It should be added that the fact that even the miracles of the military victory and the finding of the cruse of oil are from the *Havaya* of above, which is beyond *Seder Hishtalshelus* (only that they were clothed in the levels of *Seder Hishtalshelus*, *Yichuda Tata’ah* and *Yichuda Ila’ah*), is (primarily) because they were a preparation for the miracle of the oil lasting for eight days, a miracle that is not clothed in nature. These miracles, in and of themselves, are (primarily) the matters of *Yichuda Tata’ah* and *Yichuda Ila’ah*. And through the fact that the miracle of the military victory was the cause of the miracle of finding the cruse of oil (as mentioned at the beginning of the maamar), after a miracle occurred with it (the cruse they found) and they lit from it for eight days, through this, the revelation of the *Havaya* of above, which is beyond *Seder Hishtalshelus*, was drawn down both into the miracle of finding the cruse of oil and into the miracle of the military victory.

7) This is why they established the beginning of the days of Chanukah on the 25th of Kislev, the day of the miracle of the lights, because the perfection of the miracle of the military victory that occurred on the 24th of Kislev (*Yichuda Tata’ah*) is that it is revealed through the miracle of the oil that its root, too, is beyond *Seder Hishtalshelus*. And although there is a superiority in *Yichuda Tata’ah* over *Yichuda Ila’ah* [and it can be said that this is why the miracle of the military victory was the cause of the miracle of the oil, for the cause is higher than the effect], nevertheless, the perfection of *Yichuda Tata’ah* is that it should contain the light and revelation of *Yichuda Ila’ah*, as explained in the maamar. The reason why they did not innovate anything on the 26th of Kislev is, as explained in the maamar, because the primary revelation of the *Havaya* of above will be in the time to come, like the harp of the days of Moshiach with eight strings, whereas in the Beis Hamikdash, only the revelation of the *Havaya* of below shone, which is why the harp of the Beis Hamikdash had seven strings, and the revelation of the *Havaya* of above that occurred in the miracle of the oil was only for a time (in the year the miracle occurred), and therefore they did not establish that anything should be innovated on the 26th of Kislev.

8) May it be His will that this matter of "You stood by them in the time of their distress" be fulfilled in us, which refers to the general state of the exile, which is the greatest distress. [For although all the persecutions have already passed and will not occur again, as the Mitteler Rebbe stated, "it shall not rise a second time," since what we have already endured is sufficient, and now everything is for the good; and on the contrary, even during the time of exile, it is fulfilled in every Jew: "And kings shall be your foster fathers, and their queens your nursing mothers," as the Gemara states that this matter already occurred in practice during the time of the Talmud, and especially when the government helps the Jews in the study of Torah and performance of mitzvos. And even when it seems there is some confusion, we walk in the path of Avraham Avinu, the first Jew, who, upon seeing a river hindering him from fulfilling the command of Hashem, stood firmly and was not affected, and then it was revealed that it had no existence at all, but was only an illusion. Nevertheless, the state of exile (in whatever form) is a distress.] The request is that very soon, "You will stand by them in the time of their distress." And since "in all their distress, *He* is distressed," surely Hashem will very soon take the Jews out of the exile, as it is written, "And Hashem your G-d will return your captivity," and it does not say "He will return" (*v'heshiv*) but "He will return" (*v'shav*), until we come to give thanks and praise to Your great Name, and "they lit candles in the courtyards of Your holiness," in the Third Beis Hamikdash, "the Sanctuary, O L-rd, which Your hands have established."

## Motzaei Shabbos Parshas Mikeitz, the night of Zos Chanukah, 5746

1. "On the eighth day, the prince of the children of Menashe..." "This is the dedication of the altar..." It is well known that one of the reasons we read the offerings of the princes during Chanukah is because the dedication of the altar took place during Chanukah, similar to the dedication of the altar (the first time) through the offerings of the princes. From this, it is understood that the eight days of Chanukah are analogous to the eight days of the *miluim* (the inauguration of the Mishkan). Just as the eight days of the *miluim* are divided into two—the seven days of the *miluim* and the eighth day—so too is it with the eight days of Chanukah, where the eighth day of Chanukah is called (according to Jewish custom) by a name of its own: *Zos Chanukah*. [It may be said that this is also why the Torah reading on the eighth day of Chanukah is much more extensive than the seven days preceding it; for on the first seven days, we read only two princes each day, while on the eighth day of Chanukah, we read five princes, as well as *Zos Chanukas HaMizbeach* and the beginning of the portion of *Beha'aloscha* regarding the lamps. This addition to the Torah reading indicates the superiority of the day.] It is explained in the *maamar* "Baruch She'asah Nissim" by the Alter Rebbe, with the glosses and notes of the Tzemach Tzedek, that the eighth day [of the *miluim*, and likewise the eighth day] of Chanukah is *Malchus*. Therefore, it is called *Zos Chanukah*, because *Zos* is *Malchus* [ftnref_1712_9, 1713_10].

2. It is known that there are two aspects to the "eighth." The eighth is above the seven that precede it [analogous to the interpretation of the letters of the word *az* (then), where the *Aleph* rides upon the *Zayin*], for the seven are the seven *middos*, and the eighth is *Binah*. And the eighth is *Malchus*, which is below the seven *middos*. It is the same regarding the eighth day of the *miluim*, regarding which it is stated in *Likutei Torah* that there are two interpretations: that the eighth day of the *miluim* is above the seven days of the *miluim* (for the seven days of the *miluim* are in *Binah*, and the eighth day of the *miluim* is *Chochmah*), and that the eighth day of the *miluim* is *Malchus*. The bridge between these two interpretations is that for the *hemshech* (drawing down of light) to occur in *Malchus* (the eighth below the seven), it must be specifically through the *hemshech* coming from a higher place (that which is above the seven).

Seemingly, one must say that this is also the case regarding the eighth day of Chanukah, which contains both interpretations. The fact that on the eighth day the *hemshech* is in *Malchus* (which is why it is called *Zos* Chanukah)—the eighth below the seven—is because its root is in the eighth above the seven. However, from the fact that the aforementioned *maamar* (which explains the concept of the eighth day of Chanukah) cites [at the beginning of the subject] only the interpretation that the eighth is *Malchus*, unlike the *drushim* that explain the concept of the eight days of the *miluim* (where both interpretations of the eighth are cited), it implies that the primary essence of the eighth day of Chanukah is the *hemshech* downward.

3. This can be understood based on the well-known principle regarding the superiority of action [for the power of action in man, the lowest power, is analogous to the *sefirah* of *Malchus*]. There are several aspects to this. It is through action specifically that an elevation is achieved in all the powers of the soul. As the well-known parable illustrates regarding the lifting tool called a lever: by placing it under the entire object one wishes to lift, only then is the entire object lifted. For when one lifts an object from its middle, the lower part remains below. Like the raising of a house: for the entire house to be lifted upward, it must be done by lifting the bottom beam of the house; when one lifts the walls from their middle, the bottom beams remain below. It can be said that since all the beams belong to one house, when the bottom beam remains below, the elevation and ascent of the upper beams is also not complete. This is all the more so in the analogy of the powers of the soul, where each power influences the others ; for the ascent of the higher powers (intellect, etc.) to be complete, it must be through the ascent of the power of action, the lowest power. Another aspect of this is that through action, the ascent and elevation of the higher powers is to a higher place. Like the analogy of a balance scale: when one weighs in one pan and places a load in the other, the further the pan in which one weighs descends, the higher the load in the other pan is lifted.

4. The fact that the completeness of the ascent of all powers is achieved specifically through action is because the root of action is above all the powers. There are two aspects to this. The *hemshech* into action (the fact that intellect and *middos* are drawn into action) has its root in the soul, which is above the powers. For intellect and *middos* in themselves are limited and cannot descend so far as to be clothed in action; their ability to descend and be clothed in action is because they are drawn from the soul, which is present in all powers and limbs equally. Furthermore, the root of the power of action itself is above the root of all the powers. The fact that all the powers are in a state of proximity to the soul, while the power of action is a distinct power, is because the root of all the powers is in the *giluyim* (manifestations) of the soul, whereas the root of the power of action is in the *etzem* (essence) of the soul.

5. Just as in the powers of the soul the root of the power of action is above the root of all the powers, so too is it in the *avodah* of Hashem, where the primary superiority is specifically in action. The fact that "study is greater" applies only now, but in the time to come, "action will be greater". Even now, when "study is greater," the reason for this is that study leads to action, which proves that the main thing is action. The reason that in the time to come action will be greater is because there are two general aspects to the superiority of action over study [analogous to the two aspects in section 4 regarding the superiority of the power of action over other powers]. One is that action awakens and draws down the *Ohr HaBli Gvul* (Infinite Light) that is above being clothed in *Chochmah* (study), which is why the *hemshech* through action is lower. The second is that action reaches the *He'elem HaAtzmi* (Essential Concealment), which is above even the *Ohr HaBli Gvul*. It can be said that the superiority of action now (that even now the main thing is action, and the greatness of study is that it leads to action, as above) is that through action there is a *hemshech* of *Ohr HaBli Gvul*, a completeness in the matter of *giluyim*. This is the meaning of "study is greater because it leads to action"—that this superiority of action is included in the greatness of study. The fact that the *hemshech* of study is in a manner that leads to action—that the *hemshech* comes downward—is the greatness of study, that the *Ohr HaBli Gvul* is drawn into it, through which it is drawn downward and leads to action. The fact that in the time to come action will be greater is the elevation of action itself, which reaches the *He'elem HaAtzmi* that is above the *giluyim*.

6. It is known that on Chanukah, after the overcoming of the darkness of the Greeks, there was a *hemshech* more exalted than the *hemshech* that had been in the Beis HaMikdash before that. For regarding the *hemshech* that existed previously, it was possible for the darkness of the Greeks to overcome, whereas the *hemshech* of Chanukah is from the *Havayah* of *Le'eila* (the Higher Havayah) which is above *hishtalshelus* (the chain of progression). This light illuminates even the darkness, and *Havayah* (with the addition of the *vav*) "will illuminate my darkness", that the darkness itself shines, *analogous* to the *giluy* of the time to come. Based on this, one can explain the difference between the eighth day of Chanukah and the eighth day of the *miluim*. The fact that the eighth day of the *miluim* is in *Malchus* (the eighth below the seven) means that the *hemshech* *from above* was drawn down until *Malchus*. The fact that the eighth day of Chanukah is in *Malchus* means that through the *hemshech* of Chanukah, an ascent is achieved in *Malchus* itself. This is why in the *drushim* that explain the concept of the eight days of the *miluim*, both interpretations of the eighth are brought (that it is above the seven and that it is below the seven), while in the aforementioned *drush* that explains the concept of the eighth day of Chanukah, only the interpretation that the eighth is below the seven is brought. For the fact that the *hemshech* from above is drawn also below (the eighth day of the *miluim*) is due to the superiority of the root of the *hemshech* (similar to the superiority of action now), the eighth above the seven. And the power that there will be refinement and ascent in the "below" itself (the eighth day of Chanukah) is from the *He'elem HaAtzmi* that is above the root of the *giluy* (similar to the "action will be greater" that will be in the time to come). Therefore, the emphasis of the eighth day of Chanukah is that it is in *Malchus* [which is why it is called *Zos* Chanukah], the matter of the refinement of the "below".

7. This matter—that in the time to come action will be greater—was revealed (like all matters of Chassidic teaching) specifically in the final generations. The reason they revealed this to us (even though now the *halachah* is that study is greater) is because all the *giluyim* of the future depend on our actions and our *avodah* now. The fact that in the time to come action will be greater is through the effort and alacrity in the matter of action now. Therefore, in the generation of the footsteps of Moshiach, they revealed that in the time to come action will be greater, in order to awaken thereby even more effort and alacrity in the matter of action, and through this, there will be "action will be greater" in the time to come.

Since in the time to come action will be greater, it is understood that all matters of the future Redemption, and certainly the Redemption itself, must be in actual action. As long as the Redemption is not yet in actuality, the main thing is missing. Like the well-known story of the Tzemach Tzedek and the Rebbe Maharash: when the Tzemach Tzedek spoke regarding the *ketz* (end-time) that was for the year 5608, that this was fulfilled through the *Likutei Torah*, the Rebbe Maharash replied, "One needs to have Moshiach below ten *tefachim*." It can be said that the fact that the Tzemach Tzedek said that the *Likutei Torah* was printed is because through "Your springs shall spread outward," *Aty Mar Da Malka Meshicha* (the Master, this is King Moshiach, is coming). And since the inner dimension of Torah (your springs) is above the limitation of time, it is understood that when there is the spreading of the springs—the cause for *Aty Mar*—the effect, *Aty Mar*, is also present immediately. For in matters that are within the category of time, the cause and effect are in a manner of precedence and delay, where the effect *evolves* from the cause. In this *hemshech* (where the effect is drawn and evolves from the cause), there can be several modes: that the *hemshech* will be in a manner of "I will hasten it" or in a manner of "in its time". But in matters that are above the category of time, it is not possible for there to be a gap between the cause and the effect; when the cause is present, the effect is also present immediately. Rather, this matter is such that the cause (spreading the springs) and the effect (the coming of Moshiach) are above time, and the completeness of the spreading of the springs and the coming of Moshiach is that they be drawn into time and place, and into actual action below ten *tefachim*. Simply put, through our actions and our *avodah* in spreading the springs outward in actual action, through this *Aty Mar Da Malka Meshicha* comes in actual action.

Since the fact that one must wait for the coming of Moshiach (in addition to believing in the coming of Moshiach—to wait for it) is a *halachah* —and the nature of *halachah* is (first and foremost) the fulfillment of the matter as it is in its simplicity, and in actual action—therefore, with the agreement of the entire community, "We want Moshiach now", speedily and in our days, literally.

## B’s”d. Shabbos Parshas Vayigash, 5725

### B’s”d. Shabbos Parshas Vayigash, 5725

"Then Judah approached him," etc.. In the Haftorah of Parshas Vayigash, it is stated: "Take for yourself one stick and write upon it, 'For Judah,' etc., and take one stick and write upon it, 'For Joseph,' etc., and join them one to the other into one stick, and they shall become one in your hand." Seemingly, the connection between the Haftorah and the Parsha lies in the fact that the content of both is the joining of Judah and Joseph. However, in terms of specifics, the approach of Judah to Joseph (in the Parsha) emphasizes that Joseph was superior to Judah, to the extent that Joseph was king over all his brothers, including Judah. In the Haftorah, it is stated: "And My servant David shall be king over them." [As explained in Torah Or, Joseph is the *Yesod* of *Ze’er Anpin*, and Judah is *Malchus*. Therefore, currently, Joseph is above Judah, because in the *Seder Hishtalshelus*, *Ze’er Anpin* is above *Malchus* [and furthermore, *Ze’er Anpin* bestows upon *Malchus*. This is the meaning of "Judah approached him and said, 'Bi Adoni' (Please, my lord)," for the meaning of *Bi Adoni* is that through Judah (*Bi*) the *shefa* is drawn down from Joseph the *Tzaddik* (*Adoni*)]. But in the time to come, when the root of *Malchus* will be revealed—which is higher than the root of *Ze’er Anpin*—therefore, "My servant David shall be king over them."] However, from the fact that the verse "And My servant David shall be king over them" is also in the Haftorah of Parshas Vayigash, it implies that in "Judah approached him," there is also the superiority of Judah over Joseph. It is just that what is emphasized in the revelation of "Judah approached him" is the superiority of Joseph over Judah, while in the Haftorah, it is the superiority of Judah *over Joseph*.

2) This can be connected to what is explained in the *maamarim* of the Tzemach Tzedek, that the final letters of *Vayiga**sh** Elai**v** Yehud**ah*** (ויגש אליו יהודה) spell *shaveh* (שוה - equal), because the matter of "Judah approached him" is that *Malchus* (Judah) and *Ze’er Anpin* (Joseph) are both equal. He explains that the concept of *shaveh* is similar to what is written in *Likutei Torah* regarding the interpretation of the verse, "To do what is upright (*yashar*) in the eyes of Hashem." The concept of *yashar* is the connection and unification of *Da’as Elyon* (Higher Knowledge) and *Da’as Tachton* (Lower Knowledge), *Yichuda Ila’ah* and *Yichuda Tata’ah* (where both are equal), to be "one in one". For Joseph and Judah, as they are from their own perspective, are (like) two opposites. In Joseph (*Ze’er Anpin*), which is from above to below, it is *Da’as Elyon*, where "above exists and below does not." In Judah (*Malchus*), which is from below to above, it is *Da’as Tachton*, where "below exists and above does not." The matter of "Judah approached him" is the connection of *Da’as Elyon* and *Da’as Tachton*. [And this is what our Sages said on the verse "Judah approached him": "There is no 'approaching' except for peace," for the matter of peace is the connection of *Da’as Elyon* and *Da’as Tachton*]. This is similar to what is written, "And the children of Judah and the children of Israel shall be gathered together, and they shall appoint for themselves one head." For currently, the *avodah* of the children of Judah is from below to above (*Da’as Tachton*), and the *avodah* of the children of Israel [the ten tribes who belong to Joseph] is from above to below (*Da’as Elyon*), and in the time to come they will be joined together, to be "one in one."

From this, it is understood that in the approach of Judah to Joseph, there are two matters. In the revelation, the matter of Judah’s approach to Joseph is because Joseph (*Yesod* of *Ze’er Anpin*) is the *mashpia* (bestower) and Judah (*Malchus*) is the *mekabel* (recipient); therefore, Judah approached Joseph and requested *Bi Adoni*, so that the *shefa* from Joseph the *Tzaddik* would be drawn into him. And there is another matter in Judah’s approach to Joseph, *which is hinted at* in the final letters of *Vayigash Eliav Yehudah*, which is the connection and unification of Joseph (*Ze’er Anpin*, *Da’as Elyon*) and Judah (*Malchus*, *Da’as Tachton*) that will be in the time to come, where *Malchus* will be like *Ze’er Anpin*. Based on this, it can be said that in "Judah approached him" is also hinted the elevation of *Malchus* that will be in the time to come—that *Malchus* will be higher than *Ze’er Anpin*, "a woman of valor is the crown of her husband"—which is even higher than the matter of both being equal.

It can be said that this is what is found in the *Zohar*, that the approach of Judah to Joseph is the matter of "connecting *Geulah* (Redemption) to *Tefillah* (Prayer)" [for *Geulah* is the level of *Yesod*, Joseph, and *Tefillah* is the level of *Malchus*, Judah]. From the language of our Sages, "connecting *Geulah* to *Tefillah*" (and not "connecting *Tefillah* to *Geulah*"), meaning that *Tefillah* is in its place and *Geulah* is connected (approached) to *Tefillah*, it is understood that the main thing is the *Tefillah*. And from the fact that the *Zohar* says that the approach of Judah to Joseph is the matter of connecting *Geulah* to *Tefillah*, it implies that even in the approach of Judah to Joseph, there exists (in concealment) the superiority of Judah over Joseph.

3) This will be understood by prefacing what the Rebbe (Rashab) *n.e.* said in the *maamar* on this subject: that the fact that Judah preceded with *Bi Adoni* (when seemingly he should have begun with "Let your servant speak," etc.) is like the matter of the precedence of the verse "Hashem, open my lips" before *Tefillah* [which is not an interruption between *Geulah* and *Tefillah*, as stated in the *Gemara*, because since the Sages ordained to say "Hashem, open my lips," it is considered as one long *Tefillah*]. Now, in the *Targum* on the verse "Hashem, open my lips," it explains: "Open my mouth in Torah." And it is explained in several *maamarim* that discuss the matter of Torah and *Tefillah* (and also in the aforementioned *maamar*), that the request in this verse at the beginning of *Tefillah* is that the *Tefillah* ("and my mouth shall declare Your praise") should be like Torah ("Hashem, open my lips in Torah"). For just as in Torah, the Torah that a person learns is the word of Hashem, "My words which I have placed in your mouth", and the person is like one who answers after the reader, so too, the *Tefillah* should be in a manner of "and my mouth shall declare *Your* praise," that the person's *Tefillah* should be such that through it, *Your* praise—the *Tefillah* of the Holy One, Blessed be He—is drawn down (*yagid* from the word *himshech*).

4) Now, it is explained in the aforementioned *maamarim* that Torah and *Tefillah* are like the two matters of "above, above to no end" and "below, below to no limit." Torah, which is a drawing down from above to below, is like the matter of "below to no limit," and *Tefillah*, which is an elevation from below to above, is like the matter of "above to no end." And according to what is known—that the fact that *Ohr Ein Sof* is "below to no limit" (the root of Torah) is the matter of expansion and revelation, that it expands and is revealed below, below to no limit, and the fact that *Ohr Ein Sof* is "above to no end" is as it is elevated and included in His Essence above, above to no end—it is understood that *Tefillah* is higher than Torah. The request "Hashem, open my lips and my mouth shall declare Your praise" is that in *Tefillah*, which is from below to above, there should *also* be the superiority of Torah, from above to below.

The matter is that *Tefillah* is *that the person* requests and prays, and also the drawing down that comes through *Tefillah*—that the Holy One, Blessed be He, fulfills his request—is from the perspective of the superiority of the *avodah* of the lower realm, "For the work of Your hands You shall yearn". It can be said that one of the reasons why one must stand in *Tefillah* like a servant before his master in awe, fear, and trembling is because the fact that the *avodah* of the lower realm (*Tefillah*) awakens the will above to fulfill his request is (primarily) because in the *avodah* of the lower realm there is the superiority of *bitul ha-yesh* (nullification of the ego). Therefore, the more the *Tefillah* is in greater *bitul*, the more it awakens the Above to fulfill his request. This is why *Tefillah* is an elevation from below to above, because the request of *Tefillah* is in *bitul* (in the manner of a "servant" who requests), and the matter of *bitul* is that he exits his own existence—an elevation from below to above. Torah, however, is the word of Hashem [which exists even before the person’s engagement in Torah], and the effect that is achieved through the person’s engagement in Torah is that the Torah and the *Ohr Ein Sof* clothed within it are drawn to the person. And furthermore, even the drawing down of the Torah and the *Ohr Ein Sof* clothed within it *into the person* who engages in Torah is (not by the person nullifying his intellect, but on the contrary) that the Torah is drawn down and clothed in the intellect of the person as he is in his own existence—a drawing down from above to below.

It can be said that just as in Torah, its connection to the matter of "below to no limit" is because *Ohr Ein Sof* is "below to no limit," that the Torah, which is the wisdom and will of the Holy One, Blessed be He, is drawn down below, below; so too is it in *Tefillah*, that the connection of *Tefillah* to the matter of "above to no end" is (not only because *Tefillah* is an elevation *of the person*, but also) because *Ohr Ein Sof* is "above to no end." For the fact that through *Tefillah* a new will is awakened to fulfill the request of the one praying [even when, according to the wisdom of Torah, he is not worthy of it] is because through *Tefillah*, an elevation is made in the Light above, that it is elevated above the *Hishtalshelus*, and above even the Torah, up to "above, above to no end," and from there the new will is drawn to fulfill his request. Nevertheless, there is an advantage in the drawing down of Torah over the drawing down through *Tefillah*. For the drawing down through *Tefillah*, since *Tefillah* is the *avodah* of the lower realm, therefore even the drawing down from above through *Tefillah* is regarding the matters of the world (fulfilling the request of the one praying), but the *root* of the drawing down is from "above, above to no end." The drawing down of Torah, which is a drawing down from above by its own nature, is a drawing down of *Elokus*. Even after the Torah has descended below and been clothed in human intellect, it is the wisdom of the Holy One, Blessed be He, which is above the intellect of created beings.

5) The explanation of the matter in more detail will be understood based on what the Tzemach Tzedek says in the *maamar* on the verse "To understand the matter that our Sages said that *Ohr Ein Sof* is 'above to no end' and 'below to no limit'" [this notebook (in which the aforementioned *maamar* is found) was in captivity for several years, and recently it was redeemed from captivity and arrived here. It should be noted that in this *drush* there are several matters that are not in the aforementioned *drushim*] regarding the interpretation of the verse, "Train a youth according to his way; even when he grows old, he will not depart from it." Seemingly, when a person grows old, he should be elevated to a higher level; what is the superiority that even when he grows old, he will not depart from the way he was trained when he was a youth? He explains in the *maamar* that in human intellect there is a great and vast difference between the intellect a person attains in his youth and the intellect he attains in his adulthood, and certainly in his old age. But in Torah, which is *Elokus*, the difference between his youth and his old age is only in the attainment of the intellect of the person in which the Torah is clothed. But in the Torah itself, which is the wisdom of the Holy One, Blessed be He, which is above the intellect of created beings, there is no difference between the intellect of the youth and the intellect of the old man; therefore, even when he grows old, he will not depart from the way he was taught when he was a youth. He adds in the *maamar* that it is written, "I was a youth, and also I have grown old"—"youth" is *Metatron* in *Yetzirah*, and "also I have grown old" is his elevation in *Atzilus*; the wisdom of Torah in *Atzilus* is much higher than the wisdom of Torah in *Yetzirah*. Nevertheless, even when he grows old and is elevated in *Atzilus*, he will not depart from the way he was taught when he was a youth in *Yetzirah*, because the essence of Torah is the essential *taanug* of the *Atzmus* ("And I was to Him, etc., a delight", specifically *to Him*), which is higher even than the wisdom of the Holy One, Blessed be He—the wisdom of *Atzilus*. And regarding this matter in Torah (which is the essence of Torah), there is no difference between the attainment of *Yetzirah* (and also the attainment of human intellect) and the attainment of *Atzilus*.

Based on this, the advantage of the drawing down of Torah over the drawing down through *Tefillah* will be understood even more. For the drawing down below through *Tefillah* is (not that *Elokus* itself is drawn down below, but) that through the drawing down, a change is made in the matters of the world. And the drawing down of Torah is a drawing down of *Elokus*, which, even after its descent below, is the wisdom of the Holy One, Blessed be He. Furthermore, the *Elokus* in Torah that is drawn down below and clothed in the intellect of the person learning is not in the category of *olamim* (worlds); therefore, the attainment of human intellect and the attainment of *Atzilus* are exactly equal in relation to it.

6) It can be said that just as the advantage in the drawing down of Torah over the drawing down of *Tefillah* is regarding the "below," that a revelation of *Elokus* is drawn into it, so too is it in the advantage that is in the drawing down through *Tefillah* over the drawing down of Torah. For in addition to the fact that the *root* of the drawing down through *Tefillah* is from above the *Hishtalshelus*, the advantage in the drawing down through *Tefillah* is also regarding the "below," that through *Tefillah* a change is made in the created beings below, to heal the sick and bless the years. And to add, this matter [that through *Tefillah* a change is made below] is in every *Tefillah*. The matter is as explained in the aforementioned *drush* of the Tzemach Tzedek: that the fact that it is stated in the *Gemara* that in the *Tefillah* of the community (specifically) it is written, "Behold, the Almighty is mighty and does not despise", that it is desired and accepted forever, the intention is that the fact that the Holy One, Blessed be He, fulfills the request of the *Tefillah* of the community is also in revelation. That is, even in the *Tefillah* of an individual, the Holy One, Blessed be He, fulfills his request [as is proven from the fact that the Men of the Great Assembly ordained to pray three times every day, and certainly they did not ordain that it should be, *chas v'shalom*, a blessing in vain]. However, in the *Tefillah* of an individual, it is possible that the influence (the fulfillment of the request) will be in concealment, and the superiority of the *Tefillah* of the community is that the influence is in revelation. It should be noted that in several *maamarim* it is explained that in the *Tefillah* of an individual, it is possible that the influence will remain above and not be drawn down below, while in the aforementioned *drush* of the Tzemach Tzedek it is explained that the influence (even in the *Tefillah* of an individual) is drawn down below, only that it is possible that it will be in concealment. It can be said that since the request of *Tefillah* is that the influence be drawn down below (to heal the sick and bless the years), therefore, even when they draw down for him the influence he requested, if the influence is not drawn down below, his *Tefillah* is a blessing in vain. From this it is proven that in every *Tefillah*, even in the *Tefillah* of an individual, the influence is drawn down below, only that it is possible that it will be in concealment.

He continues in the *maamar* that the fact that sometimes the drawing down of *shefa* is in concealment is because of the multitude of screens that separate. That is, the fact that the *shefa* is in concealment is not because of the manner of the drawing down (for the drawing down is at a level of concealment that is above revelation), but rather, the concealment is because of the screens that conceal. It is like giving a gift in a closed vessel, where the gift is in its entirety in the vessel, but because of the screen of the vessel, it is impossible to take it in revelation. He adds in the *maamar* that the influences that were drawn down through all the *Tefillos* not only exist in their entirety in concealment, but that eventually they will also be in revelation. For when all of Israel will awaken in complete *teshuvah* and will nullify all the screens, all the influences of all the *Tefillos* throughout all the generations will then be revealed.

7) It must be understood that the reason why through *Tefillah* a light is drawn down that is above *Hishtalshelus*, through which a change is made in the created beings (to heal the sick and bless the years), is from the perspective of the *ha'ala'as man* (raising of feminine waters) in *Tefillah*. The matter of *ha'ala'as man* in *Tefillah* is seemingly when the *Tefillah* is with *kavanah* (intent) and *bitul*, yet in the *maamar* it is explained that through *every* *Tefillah* a change is made in the created beings (only that it is in concealment). It can be said that the explanation for this is that at the time that a Jew prays (even when he does not have *kavanah* in his *Tefillah*), in his *pnimiyus* (inner essence) he is like one standing before the King, only that this is in concealment. Therefore, every *Tefillah* of a Jew is an *ha'ala'as man* that awakens and draws down the Light that is above *Hishtalshelus* to change the created beings. Only that the drawing down and the change below (the fulfillment of the request) through this *ha'ala'as man* is in concealment. And the more the *kavanah* and *bitul* in the *Tefillah* are in revelation, the fulfillment of the request is in greater revelation.

8) He continues in the *maamar* that based on this, it will be understood what is found in the *Gemara* that when the Rebbe heard that Rabbi Chiya and his sons were like the Patriarchs, he placed them before the *teivah* (ark), he said "He causes the wind to blow" and the wind blew, "He causes the rain to descend" and the rain came, when he reached "He revives the dead," the world trembled. It must be understood that Rabbi Chiya and his sons were praying every day, and nevertheless, the drawing down through their *Tefillah* (the wind blew, the rain came, the world trembled) was specifically when the Rebbe placed them before the *teivah*. Seemingly, it could be said that because they prayed before the *teivah* and were *shluchei tzibur* (communal prayer leaders), there was in their *Tefillah* the power of the community, and the *Tefillah* of the community is desired and accepted forever, that even when there are screens, the drawing down is in revelation. But this explanation is not sufficient, because even the *Tefillah* of the community, even when the *shaliach tzibur* is a very great person, does not have the power to act *in revelation* to make a great change in creation, and especially the change of the revival of the dead, when there are screens that conceal. He explains in the *maamar* that through the fact that the Rebbe placed them before the *teivah* and they became *shluchei tzibur* of all of Israel, they broke (through their *Tefillah*) the screens. For in addition to the fact that the community who were present at the time of their *Tefillah* were all greatly awakened, the screens from the perspective of those who were not there and were not awakened were also broken by Rabbi Chiya and his sons through their *Tefillah*, since their *Tefillah* was as *shluchei tzibur* of all of Israel, even of those who were not awakened.

It can be said that the explanation for this is that through the fact that the *Rebbe* placed them before the *teivah* and made them *shluchei tzibur* of all of Israel, this acted upon all of Israel (even upon those who did not know at all about the whole matter) that they should be drawn after the community who were present at the time of the *Tefillah* of Rabbi Chiya and his sons, and certainly after Rabbi Chiya and his sons themselves (the *shluchei tzibur*). Therefore, the awakening of the community who were present at the time of the *Tefillah*, and the *Tefillah* of Rabbi Chiya and his sons, acted in the *pnimiyus* upon all of Israel, and thereby they nullified the screens.

9) Now, what was explained above (section 4)—that the drawing down of the Light that is above *Hishtalshelus* through *Tefillah* is that through it a change is made in the matters of the world (and not that the Light itself is drawn down below)—is in *Tefillah* as it is from its own perspective. But when the *Tefillah* is in a manner that it also has the matter of Torah, it can be said that in the drawing down through this *Tefillah*, there are two superiorities: that the root of the drawing down is "above, above to no end," and that the Light itself is drawn down below. That is, in the change that is made in the world (that the sick person is healed, etc.), it is felt in revelation not only the change in the world (the result of the drawing down) but also the Divine Light through which the change is made.

It can be connected to what is explained above (section 3) that the request "Hashem, open my lips and my mouth shall declare Your praise" at the beginning of the *Amidah* *Tefillah* is that the person’s *Tefillah* should be in a manner that through it *Your* praise—the *Tefillah* of the Holy One, Blessed be He—is drawn down. When the *Tefillah* is in this manner, there are in it two superiorities: the superiority of *Tefillah*—the *avodah* of the lower realm [which reaches "above, above to no end," "For the work of Your hands You shall yearn"], and the superiority of Torah—a drawing down *from above*. And through the connection of the two matters *in the avodah* of *Tefillah*, that even *the person’s request* is in the manner of "and my mouth shall declare *Your* praise," thereby, even *in the drawing down* through *Tefillah* there are (like) two matters: that through it a change is made *in the below*, and that the change in the below is in a manner that the *Divine Light* through which the change is made is felt in it.

10) Based on this, the connection of "Judah approached him" to the connection of the two opinions, *Da’as Elyon* and *Da’as Tachton*, can be explained, for the two matters of Torah and *Tefillah* are like *Da’as Elyon* and *Da’as Tachton*. For in addition to the fact that Torah is from above to below and *Tefillah* is from below to above (for *Da’as Elyon* is from above to below and *Da’as Tachton* is from below to above), the fact that the Torah is drawn down into the intellect of the person as he is in his own existence (for even though the intellect of the person is incomparable to the Torah, nevertheless, in order for the intellect of the person to understand and attain the Torah he is learning, he does not need to exit his own existence) and even then it is the word of Hashem—meaning that the drawing down of Torah below is not according to the boundaries of the below but from the perspective of the matter of Torah (that because it is without limit, it is drawn down everywhere)—shows that in the drawing down of Torah, the created beings do not take up space—*Da’as Elyon*, where "above exists and below does not." And the fact that the drawing down of *Tefillah* is from the perspective of the superiority of the *avodah* of the lower realm ("For the work of Your hands You shall yearn"), and also the fact that the matter of this drawing down is to change the created beings, shows that in this drawing down, the below *does* take up space—*Da’as Tachton*, where "below exists." And through Judah’s request *Bi Adoni*, which is like the matter of the precedence of the verse "Hashem, open my lips and my mouth shall declare Your praise" before *Tefillah* (as above, section 3), that his *Tefillah* (*the person’s* *Tefillah*) was in a manner that through it *Your* praise—the *Tefillah* of the Holy One, Blessed be He—is drawn down, the drawing down through his *Tefillah*, whose matter is to make a change in the below, was also in a manner that the drawing down *from above* (which is above *Hishtalshelus*) was felt in it in revelation—a connection of the two opinions.

And according to what is explained above (section 4) that *Tefillah* is higher than Torah [for Torah is like the matter of "below, below, etc." and *Tefillah* is like the matter of "above, above, etc."], and the advantage of Torah over *Tefillah* is that the drawing down of Torah is a drawing down of *Elokus*, it can be said that when *Tefillah* is in a manner that it also has the superiority of Torah, *Tefillah* is higher than Torah. And so too it is in *Da’as Elyon* and *Da’as Tachton*, that when *Da’as Tachton* is in a manner that the revelation of *Da’as Elyon* also shines in it, then it is higher than *Da’as Elyon*. And so too it is in *Ze’er Anpin* and *Malchus*, which are *Da’as Elyon* and *Da’as Tachton* (as above, section 2), that through *Malchus* becoming like *Ze’er Anpin* (both are equal), it is a preparation for the revelation of the root of *Malchus* which is higher than the root of *Ze’er Anpin*. Therefore, in the connection of the two opinions in "Judah approached him," there is also, in concealment, the elevation of *Malchus* that will be in the time to come, that *Malchus* will be higher than *Ze’er Anpin*, "a woman of valor is the crown of her husband."

**********

## B’s”D. Shabbos Parshas Vayigash, 5732

### B’s”D. Shabbos Parshas Vayigash, 5732

"And David My servant shall be a prince to them forever"—this verse is found in the Haftorah of Parshas Vayigash. We must understand: on the face of it, the connection between the Haftorah and the Parsha lies in the fact that the content of both is the unification of Yehudah and Yosef. The Parsha begins, "Then Yehudah approached him," and in the Haftorah it is said, "Take for yourself one stick and write upon it, 'For Yehudah'... and take one stick and write upon it, 'For Yosef'... and join them one to another for yourself into one stick... and I will make them one nation... and one king shall be king to them all." 

However, in terms of the particulars, the approach of Yehudah to Yosef (in the Parsha) emphasizes that Yosef was superior to Yehudah [both in the approach itself—for the fact that Yehudah approached Yosef was because he was in need of him (to release Binyamin)—and in Yehudah’s words to Yosef, where he said, "Please, my lord, let your servant speak..."], to the point that Yosef was king over all his brothers, including Yehudah. Yet in the Haftorah it is said, "And My servant David shall be king over them... and David My servant shall be a prince to them forever." From the fact that these verses are in the Haftorah of Parshas Vayigash, it is implied that this matter (the superiority of Yehudah over Yosef) exists (in hint) also in "Then Yehudah approached him," except that in "Then Yehudah approached him," the superiority of Yehudah over Yosef is in hint and in concealment (whereas what is emphasized in the open in "Then Yehudah approached him" is the superiority of Yosef over Yehudah), while in the Haftorah it is in the open.

2) Now, this matter [that in "Then Yehudah approached him" there is also (in concealment) the superiority of Yehudah over Yosef] is understood from what is explained in *Torah Or*, in the *maamar* titled "Vayigash Eilav Yehudah": The fact that the Beis Hamikdash was made of *dumam* (inanimate matter) [unlike the Mishkan, where only the floor of the Mishkan was of earth—*dumam*—but the walls of the Mishkan were of *tzomei'ach* (vegetative), and the covering of the Mishkan (the curtains) was of *chai* (animate)] is because the fact that *dumam* is lower than *tzomei'ach* is according to the *Seder Hishtalshelus*. But in their root, the root of *dumam* is higher than the root of *tzomei'ach* and *chai* [similar to heaven and earth, where in the actual creation, heaven preceded earth, but in their root in thought, earth preceded heaven. For the very fact that the earth was created last indicates that in thought it preceded heaven, for the end of an action is in the initial thought]. 

It is the same regarding *Ze'er Anpin* and *Malchus* [which are analogous to *tzomei'ach* and *dumam*]: in the *Seder Hishtalshelus*, *Ze'er Anpin* is higher than *Malchus*, but in their root, the root of *Malchus* is higher than the root of *Ze'er Anpin*. Therefore, in the time to come, *Malchus* will be higher than *Ze'er Anpin*—"a woman of valor is the crown of her husband"—for then the root of *Malchus* (which is above the root of *Ze'er Anpin*) will be revealed. This is why in the Mishkan the walls and roof were of *tzomei'ach* and *chai*, while in the Beis Hamikdash all of this was of *dumam*: because the Mishkan, which was only a temporary dwelling for Hashem, was according to the *Seder Hishtalshelus*, and therefore the floor of the Mishkan was *dumam*, the walls were *tzomei'ach*, and the roof was *chai*, because in the *Seder Hishtalshelus*, *tzomei'ach* is higher than *dumam*, and *chai* is higher even than *tzomei'ach*. But the Beis Hamikdash, which was the permanent dwelling for Hashem, was a microcosm of the World to Come, when *Malchus* (*dumam*) will be higher than all the *Sefiros*. Therefore, even the roof was *dumam*.

Similarly, this applies to Yosef and Yehudah: Yosef is (the *Yesod* of) *Ze'er Anpin*, and Yehudah is *Malchus*. The fact that now Yosef is higher than Yehudah is because in the *Seder Hishtalshelus*, *Ze'er Anpin* is higher than *Malchus*. [And this is "Then Yehudah approached him and said, 'Please, my lord,'" for the meaning of "Please, my lord" is that in Yehudah (in me) the *shefa* (bounty) should be drawn from Yosef the *Tzaddik* (my lord), because *Malchus* receives *shefa* from *Ze'er Anpin*]. But in the time to come, when the root of *Malchus* will be revealed—which is higher than the root of *Ze'er Anpin*—it will be, "And My servant David shall be king over them." 

We need an explanation: since the matter of "Then Yehudah approached him" is that Yosef (*Ze'er Anpin*, *tzomei'ach*) is higher than Yehudah (*Malchus*, *dumam*), the *maamar* that begins with "Then Yehudah approached him" does not (seemingly) have the purpose of explaining the reason why the Beis Hamikdash was *dumam*. From the fact that in the *maamar* "Vayigash Eilav Yehudah" it is explained that the reason the Beis Hamikdash was *dumam* is because the Beis Hamikdash was a microcosm of the World to Come, when *Malchus* (*dumam*) will be higher than all the *Sefiros*, it is proven that even though in the open the matter of "Then Yehudah approached him" is that *Ze'er Anpin* is higher than *Malchus*, there is within it (in "Then Yehudah approached him"), in the interior and in concealment, the superiority of *Malchus* over *Ze'er Anpin*, analogous to the revelation of the time to come.

3) This can be understood by prefacing that when Yehudah wanted to arouse Yosef to draw down the *shefa* to him, he said to him, "For you are like Pharaoh." The explanation for this is: since the drawing from *Ze'er Anpin* (Yosef) to *Malchus* (Yehudah) is a descent, Yehudah explained to Yosef that, on the contrary, through this there would be an ascent in *Ze'er Anpin*, that "you are like Pharaoh." The matter is that Pharaoh is the category of *Keter*, which is above the *Hishtalshelus*. This is what is meant by the verse, "And you shall give a fifth to Pharaoh, and the four parts shall be yours"—the four parts are *Chochmah*, *Binah*, and *Ze'er Anpin* and *Nukvah*, and the fifth is the *Keter* that is above the *Hishtalshelus*. It can be said that the fact that "the four parts shall be yours" comes in continuation to "a fifth to Pharaoh" is because through the "fifth to Pharaoh"—the drawing of the *Keter* that is above the *Hishtalshelus*—the four parts [which shall be yours, in the *Hishtalshelus*] are also in a more elevated manner. 

As it is stated in the *Zohar*, that Pharaoh is "he from whom all lights (*nehorin*) are opened and revealed (*d'atfiri'u*)." It can be said that the meaning of "all lights are *atfiri'u*" is that through the drawing of the *Keter* (Pharaoh), even the revelations (*nehorin*) of the *Hishtalshelus* are in a manner of *atfiri'u*, above limitation. And even though the matters in the *Hishtalshelus* (the four parts) are always drawn from the *Keter* that is above the *Hishtalshelus*, nevertheless, their drawing from the *Keter* (in the usual way) is in a manner where the *Keter* is distinct from them, and therefore they are in limitation. The matter of "a fifth to Pharaoh and the four parts shall be yours" is that even as they are drawn in revelation (in the *Hishtalshelus*), it is felt in them that their drawing is from the *Keter* that is above the *Hishtalshelus*, and then they are in a manner of *atfiri'u*. 

This drawing in this manner is through the *Sefirah* of *Yesod*. For the matter of *Yesod* is that it bestows upon *Malchus*, and in order for the bestowal to be below, it is through the drawing from the *Keter*. This is what he said, "Pharaoh (*Keter*) said to Yosef (*Yesod*), 'You shall be over my house, and by your word shall all my people be kissed... and without you no man shall lift his hand or his foot.'" The drawing from the *Keter* (meaning the additional light drawn from the *Keter*) in all the levels of the *Hishtalshelus* (all my people) is through the *Sefirah* of *Yesod*, "by your word" (of Yosef). And yet, "only in the throne will I be greater than you"—the fact that *Yesod* draws from the *Keter* is from the aspect of the *Keter* as it relates to the *Hishtalshelus* [which is why the revelation of this drawing is that *the matters of the Hishtalshelus* are in a manner of *atfiri'u*], but the "throne," the exaltation and transcendence—meaning the *Keter* itself, which is exalted and distinct from the *Hishtalshelus*—is not drawn through *Yesod*. This is the superiority of *Malchus* over *Yesod*: the root of *Malchus* is in the *Keter* itself. However, in *Malchus*, its root is in concealment, and its revelation is through *Ze'er Anpin*. And by *Ze'er Anpin* revealing in *Malchus* its root (the essence of the *Keter*), *Ze'er Anpin* also ascends to this level. This is what Yehudah said to Yosef, "Please, my lord... for you are like Pharaoh," for by drawing into *Malchus* (in me, in Yehudah) the *shefa* from *Yesod* (my lord, Yosef), through which the root of *Malchus*—the essence of the *Keter*—will be revealed in it, through this it will be "(like you) like Pharaoh," that the essence of the *Keter* will be drawn into it.

4) We need an explanation: since even the fact that *Yesod* draws from the *Keter* is because the matter of *Yesod* is that it bestows upon *Malchus* (as mentioned in section 3), and yet the fact that *Yesod* draws from the *Keter* is only from the aspect of the *Keter* as it relates to the *Hishtalshelus*, what is added by the matter of "Please, my lord" [that in *Malchus* the *shefa* is drawn from *Yesod*] that specifically through this is the drawing of the essence of the *Keter*? 

It can be said that the explanation for this is that the matter of "Please, my lord" is (not *that Yesod* bestows upon *Malchus*, but) *that in Malchus* the *shefa* is drawn from *Yesod*—"Please, my lord." And the revelation of the *Keter* that is drawn through *Malchus* receiving the *shefa* from *Yesod* is a higher revelation than the revelation of the *Keter* that is drawn through *Yesod* bestowing upon *Malchus*. The matter is that the analogy for the *Sefirah* of *Malchus* in the powers of the soul is the power of speech (*Malchus* is the mouth). One of the reasons for this is that the difference between the power of speech and all other powers is that all powers in a person are for his own sake, while the power of speech is (not for his own sake, but) for the sake of the other. 

Furthermore, the fact that speech is heard by another is because it goes *outside* and becomes *separate* from the person speaking. From this, it is also understood regarding the *power* of speech (before he actually speaks): even though the power of speech is within the person himself, its matter is that it has the power *to exit* from his own reality and speak to another. It is the same regarding *Malchus*: even while it is in *Atzilus* (before it descends to vivify and bring the created beings into existence), it is the root and source of the created beings. It can be said that one of the explanations for why the root of the power of speech is in the essence of the soul (higher than the root of all other powers) is that the powers that are from the side of the revelations of the soul have the matter of being a revelation of the soul (for a revelation is in proximity to the matter it reveals), and since the matter of the power of speech is that it has the power *to exit* from its own reality (the opposite of the matter of proximity, of revelation), it is because its root is in the essence of the soul, which is not limited by the matter of revelation, and it is in its ability to be also in concealment, and even in total concealment—an exit from its own reality.

5) Now, it is explained in *Torah Or* there that the reason why specifically in the earth there is the matter of *bitul* (nullification) [that the earth is a trampling place under the feet of all] is because in thought, earth preceded heaven. It is the same regarding the *bitul* in *dumam*: the fact that *dumam* is in *bitul* [for the matter of *tzomei'ach* is that it grows in height and exaltation, and the matter of *bitul* is specifically in *dumam*] is because the root of *dumam* is higher than the root of *tzomei'ach*. It is the same regarding *Ze'er Anpin* and *Malchus* [which are analogous to heaven and earth, *dumam* and *tzomei'ach*]: the fact that *Malchus* is in *bitul* is because its root is in the *Keter* (higher than the root of *Ze'er Anpin*).

It can be said that the fact that the analogy for the *Sefirah* of *Malchus* in the powers of the soul is the power of speech is also regarding the *bitul* of *Malchus*. It can be said that in the *bitul* of speech, there are two matters. That speech is not a matter of "highness." For the matter of all powers is that they reveal the soul—a matter of highness and perfection—and the power of speech, which is not a revelation of the soul, is not a matter of highness. And another matter in the *bitul* of speech is that the matter of speech is that the person speaking *exits* from his own reality (nullifies himself) for the sake of the other. It is the same in the *bitul* of *Malchus*: in addition to the fact that *Malchus* is not a matter of revelation, it *descends* to vivify and bring the created beings into existence. It can be said that this *bitul* in *Malchus* is hinted at in what is said in *Torah Or* that Yehudah (*Malchus*) is a term of *hoda'ah* (acknowledgment/thanks) and *bitul*, for the *bitul* of *hoda'ah* is that one nullifies himself to the one to whom he is acknowledging.

6) Now, it is known that by a person bringing the intellect (which he grasped in his thought) into speech, more is added to the intellect than it had initially (in thought). It is the same in the *midos* (emotions): by speaking words of love, his love is increased. It is the same with other *midos*. The reason for this is that the root of speech is in the essence of the soul, and therefore, through the drawing and enclothement of the intellect and *midos* in speech, their drawing from the soul is from a higher place. It can be said that even the level of the soul from which the intellect and *midos* are drawn through their enclothement in speech is lower than the level of the soul from which speech itself is drawn. For the fact that the intellect and *midos* (*the revelations* of the soul) are drawn and enclothed in speech is because the matter of the *Bli Gvul* (infinite) of the soul, which has the ability to descend lower and lower, is drawn into them. Even though this matter in the soul is also in the level of the soul that is higher than the root of the intellect and *midos* [which is why, through the drawing of this level, the intellect and *midos* are increased], nevertheless, this matter in the soul (that it has the ability to descend lower and lower) is the aspect of its revelation. But the root of the power of speech itself (which is not a matter of revelation) has its root in the essence of the soul.

7) Based on this, we can explain what was brought above from *Torah Or* that the meaning of "Please, my lord" is *that in him* (in Yehudah) the *shefa* should be drawn from Yosef the *Tzaddik*. Seemingly, since Yehudah requested this from Yosef, he should have requested that he (*Yosef*) draw the *shefa* to him; why did he request from Yosef that in him (*in Yehudah*) the *shefa* should be drawn? The explanation for this can be said according to what is known regarding *Oros* (lights) and *Keilim* (vessels) (for *Ze'er Anpin* and *Malchus* (Yosef and Yehudah) are analogous to *Oros* and *Keilim*): the fact that the *Oros* are drawn to be enclothed in the *Keilim* is because the root of the *Keilim* is higher than the root of the *Oros*, and since in the *Oros* the superiority of the *Keilim* that is from their root is felt, therefore they are drawn to be enclothed in the *Keilim*. 

It can be said that since *Oros* are *revelation*, therefore, even the superiority of the *Keilim* (that is from their root) as it is felt in the *Oros* is that through the *Keilim* the *Bli Gvul* that is in the matter of *revelation* is revealed [for through the *Oros* being enclothed in the *Keilim*, they are drawn also into the worlds of *Beriah*, *Yetzirah*, and *Asiyah*, and even into this physical world]. It is the same regarding *Ze'er Anpin* and *Malchus*: the fact that *Ze'er Anpin* wants to bestow upon *Malchus* [for all bestowal is with desire] is because it feels the superiority of *Malchus* that is from its root. The main feeling is that through the bestowal in *Malchus*, *the drawing of Ze'er Anpin* will be in perfection [for through *Malchus*, it will be drawn also into *Beriah*, *Yetzirah*, and *Asiyah*, and even into this world]. Therefore, Yehudah said to Yosef, "Please, my lord," that the drawing of the *shefa* from *Ze'er Anpin* to *Malchus* should be (not in order *that the drawing of Ze'er Anpin* should be in perfection, but) in order *that in Malchus* the *shefa* should be drawn, "Please, my lord." And specifically through this will be "like you, like Pharaoh," the ascent of *Ze'er Anpin* to the essence of the *Keter*. For the aspect of the *Keter* that is revealed in *Ze'er Anpin* through *the drawing of Ze'er Anpin* being drawn lower and lower—the matter of the *Bli Gvul* (of the *Keter*) that has the ability to be drawn lower and lower—is the revelation of the *Keter*. And by Yosef (*Ze'er Anpin*) drawing the *shefa* to Yehudah (*Malchus*) in order *that in Malchus* the *shefa* should be drawn ("Please, my lord"), through this there will be in him the revelation of the essence of the *Keter*.

8) We need an explanation: since the matter of *Ze'er Anpin* (Yosef) is revelation, seemingly it is not possible that there should be in it a matter that is not revelation. How did Yehudah accomplish with Yosef the matter of "Please, my lord"—that he should draw *shefa* to *Malchus* for the sake of *Malchus*—a drawing that (seemingly) does not belong to the matter of revelations? This can be understood by prefacing what is explained in *Likutei Torah*, Parshas Beha'aloscha, "When you light the lamps," and also in the *drushim* of Parshas Vayigash regarding the matter of "For your servant pledged the lad," that the fact that Israel are responsible for one another is because they are mixed with one another (*arevim*—pledged/responsible—is a term for mixed). This is one of the reasons why the Congregation of Israel is compared to the Menorah ("the Menorah of pure gold"): just as in the Menorah it is said, "It shall be made of hammered work," for through the hammering, what is above descends below and what is below ascends above, and everything is mixed together, so too all of Israel are mixed (and included) in one another, to the point that no beginning or end can be found among them. And this is "You are called man," that all of Israel are (like) one man.

It can be said that the fact that all of Israel are like one man is higher than the fact that all of Israel are mixed with one another. For the term "mixed with one another" belongs to separate things that have been mixed together. It is the same regarding the matter of "It shall be made of hammered work," that the inclusion of the hammered work is in a manner *that there are* two matters of above and below, only that they are mixed with one another, that what is above descends below and what is below ascends above. But the fact that all of Israel are like one man is above division. It can be said that the fact that "You are called man" is because even in the unification of the limbs of the human body there are two matters: all the limbs complete one another, for they all need one another. Just as the head bestows upon the foot, so the foot bestows upon the head. And regarding this matter (that the foot bestows upon the head), the foot is called by the name "head." In this inclusion, there is the division of head and foot, but since every limb is a "head" [for every limb has a special superiority, from which it bestows upon all the limbs], therefore, their inclusion is in a manner that no beginning or end can be found among them. It is the same regarding Israel: in every one of Israel there is a special superiority, and this superiority he bestows upon all the rest of Israel. Thus, all of Israel complete one another, in a manner that no beginning or end can be found among them. [Meaning, there is the matter of beginning and end, but it is impossible to determine (it will not be found) who is the beginning and who is the end.] And another matter in the unity of the limbs of the human body is that its main matter is that they are part of the human body. This matter is equal in all the limbs. It is the same regarding Israel: every one of Israel is a part of the totality of Israel, and from the aspect of this matter, all of Israel are one essence.

It can be said that the pledge of Yehudah, that he bound himself with a strong bond to be, etc., in two worlds, comes from the aspect that all of Israel are truly one essence (which is higher than being mixed with one another). Therefore, this pledge was specifically with Yehudah, for Yehudah is the category of *Malchus*, and the root of *Malchus* is in the *Keter* (and in the *Keter* itself, in the interior of the *Keter*), which is above division. It should be added that by Yehudah saying to Yosef, "For your servant pledged the lad," that this pledge was from the aspect that all of Israel are truly one essence, this matter was drawn (at least in a manner of *makif*) also into Yosef, and therefore Yosef could not restrain himself, and he made himself known to his brothers.

9) Based on this, it can be said that the meaning of "like you, like Pharaoh" is that even the true root of *Ze'er Anpin* (Yosef, "like you") is in the essence of the *Keter* (which is higher than the level of *Keter* that is the root of *Ze'er Anpin*)—Pharaoh. That is, the intention in the emanation of *Ze'er Anpin*, and also in the level of *Keter* that is the root of *Ze'er Anpin*, is in order to complete the intention of the interior of the *Keter*. And by Yehudah [the category of *Malchus*, *whose root* is in the interior of the *Keter*, meaning that even after it was drawn below, its root in the interior of the *Keter* is felt in it] saying to Yosef, "like you, like Pharaoh," this matter was established in Yosef, and this activated in him the matter of "Please, my lord"—that he should draw *shefa* to *Malchus* for the sake of *Malchus*, in order to complete the intention of the interior of the *Keter*.

10) Now, according to this explanation, the connection of "Please, my lord" to "like you, like Pharaoh" is that "like you, like Pharaoh" is the cause for the matter of "Please, my lord." For by it being established in Yosef that the true root of *Ze'er Anpin* (the intention in the emanation of *Ze'er Anpin*) is in the interior of the *Keter*, this brought him to draw the *shefa* to *Malchus* for the sake of *Malchus*, in order to complete the intention of the interior of the *Keter*. And another explanation for the connection of the two matters: by Yosef drawing the *shefa* to *Malchus* for the sake of *Malchus* ("Please, my lord"), through this there will be in him the revelation of the interior of the *Keter*—"like you, like Pharaoh" (as mentioned in section 7). For the fact that the matter of "like you, like Pharaoh" was established in him before he bestowed upon *Malchus* was (not in a manner of revelation, but) that he prepared himself to bestow upon *Malchus* in order to complete the intention. And by bestowing upon *Malchus*, the *revelation* of the interior of the *Keter* was drawn to him.

This is the connection of "Then Yehudah approached him" with the Haftorah of Parshas Vayigash, whose content is that in the time to come Yehudah will be higher than Yosef, for even the approach of Yehudah to Yosef was in a manner that the superiority of Yehudah was established in Yosef, and furthermore, that afterwards he (as it were) received from Yehudah, for by bestowing upon Yehudah, the revelation of the interior of the *Keter* shone in him, analogous to what will be in the time to come, that *Ze'er Anpin* will receive from *Malchus*.

11) Now, the explanation of the Rebbe Rayatz in his *maamar* "And My servant David shall be king over them, etc." is known: the reason why King Moshiach is called by the name David is because the matter of David is humility and *bitul*. For even though he was a king, he called himself "poor and needy." It is the same with King Moshiach: even though he will be in the ultimate greatness, as it is written, "And the spirit of Hashem shall rest upon him, etc.," and he will learn Torah with the Patriarchs and Moshe Rabbeinu, nevertheless, he will be in the ultimate humility and *bitul* to learn also with simple people. And according to what is known that all the revelations of the time to come depend on our deeds and our *avodah* now, it is understood that among the matters that bring closer and hasten the coming of Moshiach is the study and dissemination of Torah—the revealed part of Torah and the inner part of Torah—to everyone, even to simple people. And that this should be in a manner of humility and *bitul*. Meaning, the study and dissemination should not be for the sake of the elevation that is achieved through this in the teacher and disseminator ("from my students more than all"), but for the sake of the recipients. [Analogous to what was explained above regarding "Please, my lord"]. And through this, we bring closer and hasten even more the fulfillment of the prophecy, "And My servant David shall be king over them... and David My servant shall be a prince to them forever," with the coming of Moshiach Tzidkeinu, immediately.

**********

## Shabbos Parshas Vayigash, 5736

"Then Judah approached him," etc. The Zohar states that Judah’s approach to Joseph corresponds to the concept of *semichas geulah l’tefillah* (connecting the Redemption to the Prayer). It is explained in the *maamarim* of our Rebbes that *geulah* (Redemption) represents the level of *Yesod*, as it is written, "If he who has the right of redemption will redeem you, he shall redeem," and "good" (*tov*) is the level of *Yesod*. *Tefillah* (Prayer) represents the level of *Malchus*. This is the connection between *geulah* and *tefillah* to Joseph and Judah: Joseph, "a youth of seventeen years," is in *gematria* "good" (*tov*), which is *Yesod*, and Judah is *Malchus*. The precise distinction in this matter is well-known: Judah’s approach to Joseph signifies that Joseph was in his place and Judah approached him, whereas *semichas geulah l’tefillah* means that *tefillah* remains in its place and *geulah* is connected (approached) to *tefillah*. Furthermore, Judah’s approach to Joseph emphasizes that Joseph was on a higher level than Judah, which is why Judah needed to approach him, whereas in *semichas geulah l’tefillah*, the primary focus is *tefillah*.

2) The core explanation for this in the *maamarim*, based on what is explained in *Torah Or* in the *maamar* beginning "And behold, we were binding sheaves," is that the *nitzotzos* (sparks) that were refined by the *shevatim* (tribes) required a second refinement by Joseph. The refinement performed by the *shevatim* was a refinement from below to above (*birur d’Ban*), which only achieves the *bitul* of the *yesh* (the ego of existence). Therefore, they required a second refinement by Joseph, a refinement from above to below (*birur d’Mah*), through which they are included in *Elokus*. It is explained there in *Torah Or* that for the *himshech* (drawing down) of the name *Mah* to occur, it must be through the *ha'alaas MAN* (raising of feminine waters) of the name *Ban*. This is the meaning of "And behold, my sheaf arose," which refers to the *ha'alaas MAN* of the *shevatim* in a state of *bitul* (prostration), which awakened the *himshech* of the name *Mah* to perform the second refinement.

It is known that the very act of awakening the *ha'alaas MAN* of *Ban* is itself accomplished through a *himshech* from the name *Mah*. This is the meaning of the Zohar’s statement that Judah’s approach to Joseph is the concept of *semichas geulah l’tefillah*: Judah’s approach to Joseph is the *ha'alaas MAN* of the *shevatim* (Judah being the primary tribe) to Joseph, to awaken the *himshech* of *MAD* (masculine waters) from the name *Mah* so that the second refinement from above to below may take place. [This is what our Sages meant regarding the verse "Then Judah approached him": "There is no 'approach' other than for peace," because the virtue of the refinement that proceeds from above to below (Joseph’s refinement) is that it is a path of peace. As is known, there is a difference between the refinement of *tefillah* and the refinement of *Torah*: the refinement of *tefillah*, which is from below to above, is a path of war, while the refinement of *Torah*, which is from above to below, is a path of rest and peace. And within *Torah* itself, there is a difference between the refinement through the revealed aspects of *Torah* and the refinement through the inner dimensions of *Torah*.] The empowerment for the *ha'alaas MAN* of *Malchus* to *Ze'er Anpin*—Judah’s approach to Joseph—is through the *himshech* from *Ze'er Anpin* to *Malchus*, which is *semichas geulah l’tefillah*.

The phrase *semichas geulah l’tefillah*, which indicates (as mentioned above) that *tefillah* is the primary element, is because the essential intent of *dirah b’tachtonim* (a dwelling place in the lower worlds) is fulfilled through the refinement that proceeds from below to above, the refinement of *Ban*. Since the refinement from above to below, the refinement of *Mah*, is a result of the revelation of light (and not from the lower being itself), it does not relate as deeply to the lower being itself. The intent of *dirah b’tachtonim* is fulfilled specifically through the refinement from below to above, whereby the lower being itself becomes a vessel for *Elokus*. Therefore, in the time to come, Judah will be higher than Joseph, as it is written in the *Haftorah* of Parshas Vayigash: "And My servant David shall be king over them... and David My servant shall be their prince forever."

3) One must understand: if the refinement of *Ban* being higher than *Mah* will be in the time to come, then currently *Mah* is higher than *Ban*, and the concept of *semichas geulah l’tefillah* means that currently *tefillah* is higher than *geulah*. Also, one must understand that from the Zohar’s statement that Judah’s approach to Joseph is the concept of *semichas geulah l’tefillah*, it implies that even in Judah’s approach to Joseph, the superiority of *Malchus* relative to *Ze'er Anpin* is evident.

4) It is explained in the *maamarim* that these two matters—the *himshech* of *Mah* and the refinement of *Ban*—are analogous to *talmud* (study) and *maaseh* (action). The matter is that the reason for the debate regarding whether *talmud* is greater or *maaseh* is greater is because each possesses a unique virtue. The virtue of *talmud* is in the realm of revelations, and the virtue of *maaseh* is that specifically through the performance of *mitzvos* is the *himshech* of the *Atzmus* (the Essence). They concluded that *talmud* is greater because *talmud* leads to *maaseh*, meaning that the virtue of *maaseh* is also attained through *talmud*. There are two aspects to this: through the study of *Torah*, the *himshech* of the *Atzmus* within the performance of *mitzvos* is *revealed*, for the revelation of all things is through *Torah*—"Torah is light." Furthermore, even the *himshech* of the performance of *mitzvos* is through *talmud*. For the fact that the performance of *mitzvos* draws down the *Atzmus* occurs when the action is performed in a state of *bitul*, and this is achieved through the study of *Torah*, which is called *tushiyah* (counsel/strength) because it weakens the power of the *nefesh habehamis* and effects *bitul* within it. This is the meaning of "great is *talmud* because it leads to *maaseh*," that the action itself comes about through *talmud*. Similarly, this applies to the *himshech* of *Mah* and the refinement of *Ban*: the *himshech* of the *Atzmus* is through the refinement of *Ban*, and as mentioned, the intent of *dirah b’tachtonim* is fulfilled specifically through the refinement of *Ban*, but in order to arrive at this, it is through the *himshech* of *Mah*. This is like the concept of *talmud* leading to *maaseh*.

One must understand that according to this, the aforementioned precision [that the concept of *semichas geulah l’tefillah* means that even now *tefillah* is higher than *geulah*] is even more pronounced, because in the matter of *talmud* and *maaseh*, currently *talmud* is greater and only in the time to come will *maaseh* be greater, yet in the matter of *geulah* and *tefillah*, even now *tefillah* is higher than *geulah*.

5) One can explain this by saying that the reason *talmud* is currently greater because it leads to *maaseh*—which proves that *maaseh* is the primary element—is caused by the fact that in the time to come, *maaseh* will be greater. However, currently, since the primary virtue lies in revelations, the greatness of *maaseh* (through which specifically the *himshech* of the *Atzmus* is achieved) as it is felt now is as it relates to revelations. Since the revelation of the *himshech* of the *Atzmus* in the performance of *mitzvos* is through the study of *Torah*, therefore *talmud* is currently greater because it leads to *maaseh*. Based on this, one can say that what is stated in the *maamarim*—that the *himshech* of *Mah* to awaken the *ha'alaas MAN* of *Ban* is like the concept of *talmud* leading to *maaseh*—is (also) an explanation for why the concept of *semichas geulah l’tefillah*, which points to the virtue of *tefillah*, is analogous to the fact that in the time to come, Judah will be higher than Joseph. Even though Judah being higher than Joseph will be primarily in the time to come, nevertheless, since even now the virtue of *Malchus* shines (to some extent) [the reason *talmud* is greater now is because it leads to *maaseh*], it is therefore relevant that in several matters, the virtue of *Malchus* itself will be revealed—the virtue of *tefillah* relative to *geulah*. Based on this, one can also explain the statement in the Zohar that Judah’s approach to Joseph is the concept of *semichas geulah l’tefillah*, even though Judah’s approach to Joseph (generally) points to the virtue of *Ze'er Anpin*, because there are aspects in Judah’s approach to Joseph in which the virtue of *Malchus* is emphasized, as follows.

6) This can be understood by prefacing that Judah’s approach to Joseph and his statement, "How shall I go up to my father," etc., led to Joseph being unable to restrain himself and revealing himself to his brothers. Seemingly, after they all came to him with Benjamin his brother, he should have revealed himself to them immediately; why did he hide the goblet in Benjamin’s sack so that they would have to come to him again? And even then, he did not reveal himself to them immediately, but only after Judah approached him and presented his arguments did he reveal himself to his brothers.

The core explanation for this is that Benjamin is the *tzaddik tachton* (lower righteous one) who raises the receiver from below to above and connects him to the *mashpia* (the one who bestows). The primary *ha'alaas MAN* of *Malchus* is through Benjamin. Therefore, the primary *ha'alaas MAN* of the *shevatim* occurred when they came to Joseph the second time for the sake of Benjamin. The reason Joseph hid the goblet in Benjamin’s sack was because the awakening of the *ha'alaas MAN* of *Malchus* is through the *himshech* from *Ze'er Anpin* (as mentioned in section 2). It can be said that the reason Joseph commanded the goblet to be hidden in Benjamin’s sack (and Benjamin and the *shevatim* did not know of it) is because for the *ha'alaas MAN* of the receiver to be from the side of the receiver, the *himshech* from the *mashpia* that awakens the receiver must be hidden.
