The decision in *Toras HaChassidus* is in accordance with the opinion of the Ramban.

[**To note:** In general, the advantage of clothing is regarding the lower entity, but regarding the drawing down itself, the drawing down in a way of "passing through" is more elevated than the drawing down in a way of clothing. When the drawing down is clothed in the lower entity, it is constricted and changed according to the vessel of the lower entity, whereas when it is in a way of "passing through," it is not constricted as much. As is also understood from the aforementioned analogies, that the advantage of the intellect being clothed in the brain is regarding the brain, that the brain grasps the intellect, but regarding the intellect, through the intellect being clothed in the physical brain, a constriction and materialization occurs; whereas in writing intellect through the fingers of the hand, the intellect is not changed by the fingers. [As explained in the *drushim* that discuss the matter of "passing through" and clothing, that when the drawing down is in a way of clothing, it relates to the garment, whereas when the drawing down is only in a way of "passing through," it does not. As explained there the difference between Eretz Yisrael and outside the land: outside the land, the drawing down of the *shefa* (bounty) from *Malchus* of *Atzilus* as it is clothed in *Malchus* of *Beriah* and *Yetzirah* is through it *clothing* itself in *Malchus* of *Asiyah*, and therefore the *shefa* relates to *Malchus* of *Asiyah*; in Eretz Yisrael, the drawing down of the *shefa* (from *Malchus* of *Atzilus* clothed in *Malchus* of *Beriah* and *Yetzirah*) through *Malchus* of *Asiyah* is only in a way of "passing through," and therefore the *shefa* relates to *Malchus* of *Yetzirah*]. But the fact that the *hashra'as haShechinah* in the Beis HaMikdash was in a way of clothing—the intent of this is (only) *the advantage* of clothing, that the revelation that shone in the Beis HaMikdash (even after its clothing in the Beis HaMikdash, which was in a physical place) was above the category of place, and the matter of clothing is that its revelation in the physical Beis HaMikdash was in a state of *pnimiyus*. To add: the fact that the *hashra'as haShechinah* in the Beis HaMikdash was in a way of clothing also indicates the greatness of the *revelation* that shone within it. The fact that the revelation was drawn (even) into a physical place and in a manner of clothing is because the revelation that shone in the Beis HaMikdash was an unlimited revelation.]

After explaining at length the matter of the *hashra'as haShechinah* in the Beis HaMikdash, the maamar continues that the interpretation of "true attribute" is like the matter of *tikkun* and truth that is "free of hairs"—without limit. This is why specifically the name Havayah is the "true attribute," because the name Shaddai at the creation of the world, based on "I said to My world, 'Dai'," shines within limitation, and also the name Shaddai that was revealed to the Avos, based on "I am sufficient in My Elokus for every creature," shines within limitation, because the miracles that stem from this name are clothed in nature. Therefore, specifically the name Havayah is the "true attribute," as it is without limit. The maamar continues that all the names are true, but Havayah is the "truth of truth."

9) The explanation of the connection between the two opinions regarding the manner of *hashra'as haShechinah* in the Beis HaMikdash and the explanation that specifically the name Havayah is the "true attribute" can be said to be that the point of difference between the two opinions is the matter of clothing. Clothing (in general) is a descent, where the one who is clothed is constricted and changed according to the manner of the garment (as mentioned in section 8). Therefore, the opinion of the Rambam is that the *hashra'as haShechinah* in the Beis HaMikdash was in a way of "passing through," because it is not appropriate to say regarding Elokus that it was constricted and changed to be within the category of a physical place. The opinion of the Ramban (and this is the decision of *Toras HaChassidus*) is that the fact that the *hashra'as haShechinah* in the Beis HaMikdash was in a way of clothing is not a descent, but on the contrary, through the *oar* being drawn and clothed in a physical place, it is revealed that the *oar* is unlimited (as mentioned in section 8). It can be said that the reason the maamar also brings the opinion of the Rambam [and adds that this is also the opinion of the Rav Saadia Gaon, the Radak, and others] is because in the clothing in the Beis HaMikdash, both matters were present. Externally, the clothing (even the clothing in the Beis HaMikdash) is a descent and constriction—the foundation of the Rambam's view. Internally, the clothing in the Beis HaMikdash is a revelation of the truth of *bli gevul*, which has the ability to be clothed even in a physical place—the foundation of the Ramban's view. To note: this matter (that clothing is a revelation of the truth of *bli gevul*) is explained specifically in *Toras HaChassidus*, because the *pnimiyus* of the Torah reveals the *pnimiyus* within every matter.

Based on this, it can be said that the reason the maamar brings the two opinions regarding the manner of *hashra'as haShechinah* in the Beis HaMikdash is to hint that the same applies to the clothing of miracles in nature, which also contains these two matters. The matter externally is that the root of the miracles clothed in nature is from the *oar* that relates to the worlds; from the perspective of this revelation, it is impossible for there to be a change or nullification of nature. The matter internally is that through the clothing of miracles in nature, the truth of *bli gevul* is revealed, that the fact that the *Ohr Ein Sof* of *bli gevul* (which transcends clothing) rules and governs nature is in such a way that even nature itself becomes according to His will. However, this matter is revealed specifically through the miracles that transcend nature. After seeing miracles that transcend nature, it is understood that the clothing of miracles in nature is (not because they lack the power to overturn nature, but) in order to reveal that the rule of the *Ohr Ein Sof* over nature is even over nature itself. Based on this, it can be said that the fact that the Avos drew down the revelation of the name Shaddai based on "I am sufficient in My Elokus for every creature"—a new revelation that did not exist previously, even in concealment (as mentioned in section 2 and section 5)—was a preparation for the revelation of the name Havayah that was revealed at the giving of the Torah. And more than that, this drawing down itself (the drawing down of the revelation of Kel Shaddai that the Avos drew down) is the drawing down of the revelation of the name Havayah, for internally, even the miracles clothed in nature have their root drawn from the *Ohr Ein Sof* that transcends relation to the worlds—Havayah. However, the drawing down of the name Havayah then was clothed in the name Kel Shaddai, and at the giving of the Torah, when the revelation of the name Havayah was drawn down, it was then revealed that this is also the *pnimiyus* of the revelation of Kel Shaddai that was drawn down by the Avos (similar to what was explained above, that after seeing miracles that transcend nature, it is revealed that this is also the *pnimiyus* of the miracles clothed in nature). This is "I appeared to the Avos as Kel Shaddai, but by My name Havayah I was not *known* to them"—I was not *recognized* by them in My true attribute—meaning that even to the Avos the revelation of the name Havayah was drawn down, but at that time it was in concealment; I was not *known*, I was not *recognized*.

10) More deeply, it can be said that the same applies to the name Shaddai at the creation of the world, based on "I said to My world, 'Dai'." Since the intent and purpose of the measurement and limitation of the world ("I said to My world, 'Dai'") is that through the *avodah* of *Torah* and *mitzvos*, the revelation of *bli gevul* will be drawn into the limitation (of the name Shaddai based on "I am sufficient in My Elokus for every creature," and through this, the *bli gevul* of the name Havayah), therefore, the matter of "I said to My world, 'Dai'" in its *pnimiyus* is that there should be a revelation of *bli gevul* within the limitation. It can be said that this is why all the names are true, because the purpose of all the names (even the name Shaddai of "I said to My world, 'Dai'," and even the name *Elokim* which has the same *gematria* as *hateva* (nature)) is that there should be the revelation of the truth of Havayah. However, the connection of the name Shaddai at the creation of the world to the revelation of Havayah is that *the intent* in "I said to My world, 'Dai'" is so that there should be a revelation of *bli gevul* within it, but in actuality, the matter of "I said to My world, 'Dai'" is *to limit, to conceal* the *bli gevul*. And the connection of the name Shaddai that was revealed to the Avos to the revelation of the name Havayah is that then the revelation of the *bli gevul* of Havayah began *in actuality*, and it was only that then (as yet) I was not *recognized* in My true attribute.

11) The revelation of the *pnimiyus* of the miracles clothed in nature (that the clothing is because through it the truth of *bli gevul* is revealed, as mentioned in section 9) will be primarily in the future. For now, the limitation drawn from the name Shaddai based on "I said to My world, 'Dai'" and the *bli gevul* drawn from the name Havayah are two opposing matters. Therefore, there is room to say that the fact that there are miracles clothed in nature is because in these miracles, the limitation (drawn from "I said to My world, 'Dai'") still remains, *which conceals* the *bli gevul* of Havayah. But in the future, when the limitation will not conceal the *bli gevul*, it will be felt in the revelation that the clothing of miracles in nature is because through it the truth of *bli gevul* is revealed. This is "I was not recognized by them in My true attribute"—that the revelation of the name Havayah now is a "true *attribute*," *middah* derived from measurement and limitation, where the *bli gevul* of Havayah is limited in the matter of *bli gevul*, and the revelation of Havayah that will be in the future will transcend *middah* (as mentioned in section 1), for then, even within the limitation there will be a revelation of *bli gevul*, as it is written: "And the glory of Havayah shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see," that even the physical flesh will see the revelation of the glory of Havayah, with the coming of Moshiach Tzidkeinu, immediately.

## By the Grace of Hashem. Friday, Erev Shabbos Kodesh, Parshas Bo, 10 Shevat, after midday, 5730

### By the Grace of Hashem. Friday, Erev Shabbos Kodesh, Parshas Bo, 10 Shevat, after midday, 5730

**– The completion of the writing of the Sefer Torah for the welcoming of Mashiach Tzidkeinu –**
**– Which was initiated and begun by my revered father-in-law, the Rebbe, Nasi of our generation –**

**To understand** the matter of writing a Sefer Torah, as stated in the Rambam that it is a positive commandment for every single person of Israel to write a Sefer Torah for himself, as it is said, "Now, therefore, write for yourselves this song". And the King is commanded to write one Sefer Torah for himself, for the sake of the King, in addition to the Sefer that he must have when he is a commoner, as it is said, "And it shall be, when he sits upon the throne of his kingdom, that he shall write for himself a copy of this Torah... and it shall be with him, and he shall read therein all the days of his life," etc.. The first to write a Sefer Torah was Moshe Rabbeinu, who is the *Ra'aya Mehimna* (Faithful Shepherd) of all Israel, and the *atpashtuta* (extension) of Moshe in every generation, and he finished it on Erev Shabbos Kodesh, as explained in many places. The Tzemach Tzedek writes that the matter of writing a Sefer Torah is understood based on what the Alter Rebbe explains regarding the verse, "And Moshe wrote," as follows.

2) It is written, "These are the journeys of the Children of Israel who went out from the land of Egypt." The Alter Rebbe notes in his *drushim* on *Eileh Masei* that one must understand why it uses the plural "These are the journeys," and concludes with "who went out from the land of Egypt," when "from the land of Egypt" refers only to the first departure and journey (when they traveled from Rameses to Sukkos). He explains that all forty-two journeys until their encampment across the Jordan of Jericho, in the Holy Land—may it be built and established speedily in our days by Mashiach Tzidkeinu—are all called "who went out from the land of Egypt." Regarding this, it says, "And Moshe wrote their goings-forth according to their journeys... and these are their journeys according to their goings-forth." The Alter Rebbe notes that one must understand why, when it says "And Moshe wrote," it places their "goings-forth" before their "journeys" ("And Moshe wrote their goings-forth according to their journeys"), and afterwards reverses the order and says, "and these are their journeys according to their goings-forth." He explains this based on the known principle that the ultimate intent of the soul’s descent into the body—that the soul descended from a high mountain to a deep pit, which is the "Egypt" of the world, or at the very least, the "Egypt" of *kedushah*—is for the sake of an ascent. And the ascent is to a level higher than the place from which it descended, as is explained in many places, that specifically through this, one understands why the descent occurred: because through the descent, it reaches a level higher than the place of its descent. And this is "and these are their journeys according to their goings-forth," the ascent from below to above, to the level of their "goings-forth". The preface and the empowerment for this is "And Moshe wrote their goings-forth according to their journeys," meaning that Moshe [the *Ra'aya Mehimna*, the faithful shepherd of all Israel in all generations] wrote (and writing signifies *hemshech*—drawing down) and drew down from the level of their "goings-forth," the source of the souls of Israel, to their "journeys," the level of the soul as it is clothed in the body. And it says "goings-forth" (and not "their departure" or the like), because its meaning is like a *motza* (a source/outlet), where the matter drawn from it is as if it happens automatically. For "goings-forth" is a level such that one cannot say that something is influenced by it and undergoes a change, but rather it is a *motza*, in an automatic way. Nevertheless, specifically from there, the matter is drawn downward, as it is written, "And He breathed into his nostrils the soul of life," and "he who breathes, breathes from his inner being". Furthermore, in the matter of "And (Moshe) wrote," the concept of "sealing" is also included, as it is in the Gemara regarding the interpretation of "And he shall write for her a scroll," etc., that according to Rabbi Meir [who is so called because he illuminates the eyes of the Sages in *halacha*], "wrote" means "sealed". Meaning, Moshe effected the act of writing and sealing of the level of "goings-forth" into "journeys." And through this, it became possible afterwards for there to be in practice "and these are their journeys according to their goings-forth," the ascent from below to above, as mentioned above.

3) The explanation of this matter is (as explained in the aforementioned *drushim*), that it is stated in the *Zohar* that the seventh *tikkun* of the thirteen *tikkunei dikna* (rectifications of the beard) is the *tikkun* of *Emes* (Truth). Even though all thirteen *tikkunei dikna* are drawn from above, from the *Seder Hishtalshelus* entirely—which is why the thirteen attributes of mercy rectify all flaws, as explained in *Iggeres HaTeshuvah*—nevertheless, they are called *tikkunei dikna*, the hairs of the beard. The concept of hair is that although its nourishment is from the *mochin* (intellects) within the skull, the manner of drawing down the vitality into the hair is through the barrier of the skull itself, which is a matter of extreme *tzimtzum*, to the point that if a person were to cut the hair of his head, he would feel no pain at all. A parallel to this is understood above: although the *hemshech* in the level of the hairs of the beard is far above the *Seder Hishtalshelus*, nevertheless, the manner of the *hemshech* is only in the aspect of an *oar* (illumination) that comes through a great *tzimtzum* called "hair." This is not the case with the seventh *tikkun* of the thirteen *tikkunei dikna*, which is the *tikkun* of *Emes*; in it, the light is drawn down and revealed not through the *tzimtzum* of the aspect of hair. A parallel to this in a person below is the part of the face that is free of hair, as explained in the *Zohar*. Furthermore, it is written, "A man’s wisdom illuminates his face", for in the illumination of a person’s face, the innovation of the new *hemshech* from the level of *Chochma* is recognizable, like Rabbi Abahu, whose face shone because he found a new *Tosefta*. And the change in his face is in a way that is visible to all, as it is in the Gemara regarding Rabbi Yehuda, that even a non-Jew recognized that his face was shining because "a man’s wisdom illuminates his face." This is one of the differences between the *hemshech* in the hair and the *hemshech* in the face: although the hairs are drawn from the *mochin* in the head, and even after that they receive vitality from the *mochin* in the head, to the point that through this they grow and increase, nevertheless, what is seen and revealed are only the hairs alone, and the vitality within them is not recognizable in them, to the point that even the fact that they receive vitality is not recognizable in them. This is not the case with the *hemshech* in the face, "a man’s wisdom illuminates his face," for in his face, the *Chochma* is recognizable. And this is why the seventh *tikkun* is called *Emes*, for the concept of *Emes* is that it has no changes, because the innovation (the elevation) of this *tikkun* is that it does not change (become obscured) through the *hemshech*, for even while being drawn down, it shines openly.

Perhaps it can be said that the fact that the seventh *tikkun* is called *Emes* (for the concept of *Emes* is that it does not change) is also from its root, for the level from which the *hemshech* of the seventh *tikkun* comes is "I, Hashem, have not changed".

4) Based on the known principle that everything that is highest of the high is drawn down to the lowest of the low, it is understood that the seventh *tikkun*, the *tikkun* of *Emes*, is drawn down primarily below. The matter is that the true essence of "I, Hashem, have not changed" is in His *Atzmus* and *Mahus* (Essence and Being), may He be blessed, whose existence is from His *Atzmus* and is not caused by any prior cause, Heaven forbid. For in the *giluyim* (revelations), which are preceded by a cause and reason, one cannot say the true concept of "I have not changed," and this itself—that they are preceded by a cause and reason—is a change, for there is a difference between before it was drawn from the cause and reason, at the time it was drawn from the cause and reason, and after it was drawn from the cause and reason. This is not the case in His *Atzmus* and *Mahus*, may He be blessed, which is not caused by any prior cause, Heaven forbid; there are no changes in Him, Heaven forbid—the true concept of "I, Hashem, have not changed." And this matter is drawn down primarily below, as the Mitteler Rebbe explains, that the fact that the *yesh* (existence) below feels that its existence is from its own essence, and in its feeling it is not preceded by a cause and reason, is because its root is from His *Atzmus* and *Mahus*, may He be blessed, whose existence is from His *Atzmus* and is not caused by any prior cause, Heaven forbid; for the true existence of the created *yesh* is the True *Yesh*. As explained at length in the *maamar* "Mi Kamocha" by the Rebbe Maharash
[which, as the Rebbe, my father-in-law, revealed—each of the Rebbes had fixed *maamarim* which they would repeat in public once every two or three years, and one of the *maamarim* of the Rebbe Maharash was this *maamar* "Mi Kamocha"],
and similarly it is explained in his *Hemshech Mayim Rabim* regarding the concept of "There is nothing else besides Him" [in addition to what is explained there regarding the concept of "The Great, the Mighty," etc. (and our Sages said, "Greatness" refers to the work of Creation) as explained in *Shaar HaYichud VeHaEmunah*, that this comes as a continuation of what is explained there at the beginning of the *Hemshech*, that even though the soul descends to a place where there are many waters and rivers that cause confusion, nevertheless, "Many waters cannot extinguish the love, nor can the rivers wash it away", this is explained there in addition], that even though the world is an existence, nevertheless, its true essence is *Elokus*, and specifically below is this matter seen, as explained there at length. And similarly, it is explained in the *maamarim* of the Rebbe (Rashab) *n.e.* that specifically below in this world is it seen and revealed how "There is no comparison to You," and through this we know that even the higher worlds are in a state of "no comparison" relative to the *Atzmus*. And similarly, it is also regarding the concept of "There is nothing else besides Him."

5) The true concept that the created *yesh*—its true existence is the True *Yesh*, His *Atzmus*, may He be blessed—applies to the souls of Israel as they are below, for specifically regarding them can one say the title of *yesh* (for they are in a state of existence), unlike the nations of the world, etc., who are not in a state of existence, for their entire matter is only that they are secondary to the souls of Israel (as explained in many places). And what is said that the created *yesh* is the True *Yesh* refers to the souls of Israel below, whose root is in His *Atzmus*, may He be blessed. And although all existing things were found only from the truth of His existence (and this is the foundation of foundations and the pillar of wisdom, etc.), nevertheless, the souls are "a part of G-d from above, truly", and the teaching of the Baal Shem Tov is known, that when you grasp a part of the essence, you grasp the whole. And with this, one can also understand that while the soul is below, a *Yid* cannot—neither does he want to, nor can he—be torn away from G-dliness, Heaven forbid, because even as he is found below, in the truth of his existence, there is felt in him, as it were, the true existence of His *Atzmus* and *Mahus*, may He be blessed, whose existence is from His *Atzmus* and is not caused by any prior cause, Heaven forbid.

6) And this is "And Moshe wrote their goings-forth according to their journeys and these are their journeys according to their goings-forth," that Moshe Rabbeinu drew down the level of His *Atzmus* and *Mahus*, may He be blessed ("goings-forth") into the soul below ("journeys"), and through this, the ascent of the soul was subsequently effected, "and these are their journeys according to their goings-forth." And as explained at length in the *maamarim* of the *Yom HaHillula* of 10 Shevat (and specifically in the twentieth chapter thereof) regarding the descent of the soul: because the soul descended to the lowest of the low, into the body and the *nefesh habahamit*, therefore, in order that it be able to withstand all the trials and fulfill its mission and role, it needs the attribute of *Netzach* (victory) specifically. And the attribute of *Netzach* brings the drawing down and revelation of the "Supreme Treasury," which is the revelation of the *pnimiyus* (inner essence) and *Atzmus* of *Ein Sof*, may He be blessed. Furthermore, through the *avodah* of man, an elevation is effected, as it were, even above. And as the teaching of the Maggid of Mezeritch is known in the interpretation of the Mishnah, "Know what is above you"—meaning, know that everything that is above, in the *partzufim* and the higher *Sefiros*, everything is "from you," that it depends on the *avodah* of man. And more specifically, it is explained in his teachings on the verse, "Make for yourself two silver trumpets" [which was said to Moshe Rabbeinu, the *Ra'aya Mehimna*], "trumpets" (*chatzotzros*) are two "half-forms" (*chatzi tzurah*), that through the *avodah* of man below, he effects a perfection above, as it were, so that there will be a complete form.

7) The drawing down of the level of *Emes*, the seventh *tikkun*, through the *tikkunei dikna*, and afterwards through the ten *Sefiros* of *Atzilus* and the entire *Seder Hishtalshelus* until this lowest world, than which there is no lower, is through the Torah, as our Sages said, "There is no *Emes* but Torah." And regarding the Torah it is said, "Is not My word like fire?" And our Sages expounded, "Just as fire does not receive impurity, so too the words of Torah do not receive impurity"—meaning that the Torah is drawn down everywhere, even to the lowest of the low. And not only is the Torah drawn down even to the lowest of the low, but furthermore, even while it is below, it is as it is above, in the manner of "My word is like fire." This is the level of *Emes*, whose concept is as it is in the *Yerushalmi* [regarding "The seal of the Holy One, blessed be He, is *Emes*"]: that *Aleph* is the beginning of all letters, and *Tav* is the end of all letters, and *Mem* is their middle, and there are no changes in this at all, which is the concept of "I am the first and I am the last, and besides Me (also in the middle) there is no G-d". Therefore, through the Torah (which is *Emes*), the level of *Emes*, the seventh *tikkun*, is drawn down to this lowest world, than which there is no lower.

8) And this is "And Moshe wrote," etc., which refers to the matter of Torah, for the matter of Moshe is (as explained in *Likkutei Torah*) the matter of Torah; "Moshe received Torah," etc., and it is called by his name, as it is written, "Remember the Torah of Moshe My servant." Through the Torah, he drew down this level, *Emes*, from the thirteen *tikkunei dikna* (the thirteen attributes of mercy), from the Supreme Will, may He be blessed, until the lowest of the low, and even to the manner of writing ("And he wrote") with ink on parchment. Meaning, that although the writing of an intellectual matter in general, and certainly the wisdom of the Holy One, blessed be He, is a great descent, and there must be many *tzimtzumim* until it is drawn with ink on the scroll in the twenty-two letters of the Torah, as explained in *Tanya*, nevertheless, it did not change here through its being drawn down, and this level is drawn down until this lowest world, than which there is no lower. And this is also why in "And (Moshe) wrote," the matter of "sealing" is included (according to Rabbi Meir), as mentioned above.

Regarding the "seal," there are two matters. The first matter is that it is sealed on the object so that it will not be changed, the level of "I, Hashem, have not changed" [and this matter exists also in every single Jew, for this is what is written, "I, Hashem, have not changed, and you, O sons of Yaakov, have not been consumed," and one of the interpretations of this is that since "I, Hashem, have not changed," therefore "you, O sons of Yaakov, have not been consumed"]. The second matter in a seal is that not only is it not the essence of the person, but it is only a name and a sign for the person (for the name has no value relative to the essence of the owner of the name), but furthermore, when one seals with a seal on wax, it is separated and distinct from him. The parallel to this in the worlds are the worlds of *Beriah, Yetzirah, Asiyah*, for "He, His life, and His essence are not one in them", and especially the world of *Asiyah*, than which there is no lower. Meaning, that the level of "I, Hashem, have not changed" (goings-forth), the first matter of the seal, is drawn down and sealed to the lowest of the low (journeys), and likewise the second matter of the seal. And all this is effected through "And Moshe wrote," through Moshe Rabbeinu, the *Ra'aya Mehimna*.

9) And this is the concept of the mitzvah of writing a Sefer Torah, regarding which the Rebbe, the Tzemach Tzedek, writes that it is like the matter of "And Moshe wrote," etc., as mentioned above, for in every single person of Israel there is the level of Moshe within him [and as it is written, "And now, Israel, what does Hashem your G-d ask of you but to fear," and the Gemara asks on this, "Is fear of Heaven a small thing?" (and especially since afterwards the verse details many details, and even to the exposition of our Sages, "Do not read *mah* (what), but *me'ah* (a hundred)," which is the ultimate perfection, ten included in ten, and also as explained in many places that there are one hundred letters in this verse), and answers: "Yes, for Moshe it is a small thing," for since in every single person of Israel there is the level of Moshe within him, therefore fear is a small thing for everyone]. And since in every single person of Israel there is the level of Moshe within him, therefore, it is in his power to draw down, through the writing of a Sefer Torah, the level of *Emes* as it is in the truth of existence [as stated in the Rambam that He alone is the Truth and there is no other truth like His truth], and to draw it down until the lowest of the low, and in the manner of "a man’s wisdom illuminates his face," that the entire world will recognize in him that he found a new *Tosefta*. And it is drawn down also in the writing, with ink on the parchment, for the parchment is the parchment of an animal (and a living creature) and the ink is made from vegetation and minerals, and from all this a complete Sefer Torah is made, and it is "before the eyes of all Israel".

It can be said, by way of possibility, that this is why the Rebbe, my father-in-law, Nasi of our generation, began the writing of the Sefer Torah (and we merited to finish it), for our Sages said, "Moshe is the first redeemer and he is the final redeemer," and therefore *Shiloh*, which refers to Mashiach, is in *gematria* "Moshe", and when you add "one" (*echad*) to *Shiloh*, it is in *gematria* "Mashiach," as explained in the well-known *sicha* of the Rebbe, my father-in-law. Meaning, that when one draws down the level of "one," that the *Aleph*, the Master of the Universe, should illuminate the *Chet* and the *Dalet*, which are the seven heavens, the earth, and the four corners of the world, and in an open way (that "as I am written, so am I read"), this is the revelation of Mashiach. And therefore, just as the matter of Moshe is the matter of writing a Sefer Torah, as explained at length above, it can be said that similarly it is also the matter of Mashiach. And one should add to this: Mashiach Tzidkeinu is called "King Mashiach", as it is written, "And My servant David shall be king over them," and regarding the Sefer Torah of a king it is said, "And it shall be with him, and he shall read therein *all the days of his life*." And our Sages expounded regarding the verse, "In order that you remember the day of your going out from the land of Egypt *all the days of your life*," "the days of your life"—the days; "all the days of your life"—to include the nights. And the Sages say, "the days of your life"—this world; "all the days of your life"—*to include the days of Mashiach*, this world and the world to come. From which it is also understood regarding what is said about him, "and he shall read therein all the days of his life," that it refers also to the days of Mashiach.

10) In practice, we have already finished the Sefer Torah, which the Rebbe, my father-in-law, called the "Sefer Torah of Mashiach," and it is only a matter of time, and very soon we will merit the coming of Mashiach Tzidkeinu; he will come and redeem us and lead us upright to our land, and then there will be the revelation of the Torah of Mashiach. For although this was already given to Moshe from Sinai at the time of the Giving of the Torah, for the Giving of the Torah will not happen again, as explained in many places, at the Giving of the Torah it was in a state of concealment, and in the time to come, there will be the revelation of the Torah of Mashiach, "May He kiss me with the kisses of His mouth". And the preparation for this is, "those who taste life have merited" on Erev Shabbos Kodesh after midday of the six thousand years that the world exists, at which time was the Rebbe, my father-in-law, after the revelation of the Arizal, and afterwards the revelation of the Baal Shem Tov and the Maggid, and afterwards the revelation of the Rebbe, the Alter Rebbe, and his successors until the Rebbe, my father-in-law—all of this is the preparation and the preface and the vessel which, very soon, when everyone completes their matters, we will merit that the empowerment of the Rebbe, my father-in-law, Nasi of our generation, will be revealed in practice, and his hope and desire will be fulfilled, that with this Sefer Torah we will go out to welcome Mashiach Tzidkeinu, with joy and goodness of heart, "and everlasting joy upon their heads", and in the manner of "with our youth and with our elders," etc., "with our sons and with our daughters", very soon, and also in the language of the Targum, *b'agala didan* (in our immediate time).

## 10 Shevat, 5711

My revered father-in-law, the Rebbe, writes in his maamar for the day of his passing, 10 Shevat, 5711: "I have come into my garden, my sister, my bride." The Midrash Rabbah states (ad loc.): "It does not say 'to the garden,' but 'to my garden'—to my *ginuni*, to the place where My essence was initially; for the essence of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms." To understand the precision of the phrase "the essence of the *Shechinah*," we must look at what the Alter Rebbe explains: the term *Shechinah* is derived from the fact that it "dwells" (*shochen*) and clothes itself—as in the verse, "And I will dwell (*veshachanti*) among them"—which is the beginning of the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof*. Now, from the fact that he writes that the beginning of the revelation of the *Ohr Ein Sof* is called *Shechinah*, it is understood that the concept of *Shechinah* is far higher than *Atzilus* as well, even at the level of the light before the *Tzimtzum*. For the beginning of the revelation is in the *Ohr Ein Sof* before the *Tzimtzum*, and as the Mitteler Rebbe explains, the illumination of the *Kav* (the line) relative to the essence of the *Ohr Ein Sof* is called *Shechinah*. The concept of *Shechinah* is relative to its context: relative to *Atzilus*, the *Malchus* is called *Shechinah*. Regarding this, the Tzemach Tzedek explains that *Malchus* of *Atzilus* is called *Shechinah* only when it becomes a level of *Atik* for the world of *Beriah*; however, while it is within *Atzilus*, it is unified with the *Sefiros* of *Atzilus*, and the term *Shechinah* does not apply. Nevertheless, this does not contradict the statement above that the *Kav* is called *Shechinah*, because the *Shechinah* mentioned by our Sages refers to *Malchus* of *Atzilus*—and even then, only when it becomes *Atik* for *Beriah*. But at its root—that is, as it relates to the *Ohr Ein Sof*—the *Kav* itself is also called *Shechinah*. One of the reasons the *Kav* is called *Shechinah* relative to the *Ohr Ein Sof*, the Rebbe Maharash writes, is because the intent of the *Kav’s* extension is to be clothed in the worlds and the souls of Israel; therefore, even at its inception, it is called *Shechinah*. And at a higher level—the *Shechinah* in its primary root above the *Tzimtzum*—the Rebbe Rashab explains that the revelation of the light as it is before the *Tzimtzum* is called *Shechinah*. For generally, the light before the *Tzimtzum* has three levels: the essence of the light, the diffusion of the light, and within the diffusion of the light, two levels: the revelation of the light as it is for itself (which is the source of *Ohr Sovev*), and the revelation of the light as it relates to the worlds (which is the source of *Ohr Memaleh*). This latter revelation is called *Shechinah*. Even though it is before the *Tzimtzum* and cannot be a source for the worlds—which is why the *Tzimtzum* was necessary, and the first *Tzimtzum* is specifically a matter of withdrawal—it is still called *Shechinah*. This is the precision of the phrase, "the essence of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms": the revelation of the *Shechinah* in the lower realms is not the *Shechinah* as it relates to *Atzilus* (i.e., *Malchus*), nor as it relates to the *Ohr Ein Sof* (i.e., the *Kav*), but rather the essence and interiority of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms specifically. For the light that is clothed in the worlds comes in a sequence and gradation; above, it shines with greater revelation, and as it continues and descends, the light diminishes. This is the general rule regarding the light of the *Seder Hishtalshelus*. Even though before the sin the light was also revealed below, there was still a greater revelation of light above. As our Sages say: "He stretched out His right hand and created the heavens; He stretched out His left hand and created the earth." This refers to the light above the worlds—the essence of the *Shechinah*.

2) The statement that "the essence of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms" is explained by the Midrash to mean that "lower realms" refers to this physical world. As it explains, through the sin of the Tree of Knowledge, the *Shechinah* withdrew from the earth to the firmament, and through the Giving of the Torah at Mount Sinai, "I have come into my garden, my *ginuni*." The primary withdrawal caused by sin is specifically the sin of the Tree of Knowledge. Just as regarding sin in general, the primary sin was the Tree of Knowledge—which gave room for other sins and was the cause and factor for the sins of Cain, Enosh, etc.—so too regarding the effect of sin: the withdrawal caused by sin is primarily the withdrawal from this lowest world specifically. Just as the essence of the *Shechinah* in the lower realms is in this world specifically, so too with the withdrawal: the primary withdrawal is that it withdrew from the earth, which happened through the sin of the Tree of Knowledge, whereby the *Shechinah* withdrew from the earth to the firmament. This is also the reason why the maamar does not group the sin of the Tree of Knowledge with the sins of Cain and Enosh, but considers it separately: because with the sins of Cain and Enosh, the *Shechinah* withdrew from firmament to firmament, whereas with the sin of the Tree of Knowledge, it withdrew from earth to firmament. Aside from the fact that the withdrawal from the earth is what primarily concerns us, this is also the essence of the withdrawal.

(The maamar continues:) "And afterwards, seven *Tzaddikim* arose and brought the *Shechinah* down; Abraham merited and brought the *Shechinah* from the seventh firmament to the sixth, etc." (It summarizes this and concludes:) "Until Moses, who is the seventh (and all sevenths are beloved), brought it down to earth." The essence of the drawing down is through Moses, for Moses specifically brought it down to earth. Just as regarding the withdrawal from below to above, the essence is the sin of the Tree of Knowledge, where it withdrew from the earth, so too regarding the drawing down from above to below, the essence of the drawing down is to the earth specifically. Aside from the fact that the drawing down to the earth is what primarily concerns us, this is also the essence of the drawing down. This was accomplished by Moses specifically. The reason for this is explained in the maamar in brackets: because "all sevenths are beloved."

3) From the language of our Sages, "all sevenths are beloved" (and not "all beloved are sevenths"), it is proven that the primary virtue is that he is the seventh; because he is the seventh, that is the source of his belovedness. That is to say, his belovedness is not due to something dependent on his choice, will, or *avodah*, but rather in his being the seventh, which comes from birth. Nevertheless, all sevenths are beloved. Therefore, Moses merited that the Torah was given through him. My revered father-in-law, the Rebbe, explained (at the beginning of his arrival in America) that even in the matter of "the sevenths are beloved," the virtue of the first is evident, for the entire concept of "seventh" is that he is the seventh to the first. He then explained the virtue of the first—Abraham our forefather—due to his *avodah*, and that his *avodah* was with *mesiras nefesh*. He does not stop there, but adds (even though, seemingly, it is not relevant to the main point there) that his mode of *mesiras nefesh* was that he did not *seek* *mesiras nefesh*. This is the difference between the *mesiras nefesh* of Abraham and the *mesiras nefesh* of Rabbi Akiva: the *mesiras nefesh* of Rabbi Akiva was that he sought *mesiras nefesh*—"When will it come to my hand that I may fulfill it?"—whereas with Abraham, his *mesiras nefesh* was incidental. Abraham knew that the essence of *avodah* is as it is written: "And he called there in the name of Hashem, the Eternal G-d"—do not read "he called" (*vayikra*), but "he caused to call" (*vayikria*), that the other should also cry out. And if *mesiras nefesh* was needed incidentally, that too was present. So great was the virtue of his *avodah* and his *mesiras nefesh* that even Moses, who merited that the Torah be given through him, is because "the sevenths are beloved," for he is the seventh to the first. And the Holy One, Blessed be He, said to him (to Moses): "In the place of the great (Abraham), do not stand." And although the belovedness of the seventh is great and does not come through choice or *avodah*, but rather automatically by birth, there is no limitation to say it is miraculous and only for the uniquely righteous. Rather, as explained in *Tanna D'bei Eliyahu* (ch. 9 and 26) and cited in *Chassidus*, all of Israel—even a servant and even a maidservant—can reach the inspiration of the Holy Spirit, and every single Jew is obligated to say, "When will my deeds reach the deeds of my forefathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob?" However, one must not be fooled by oneself, and one must know that "in the place of the great, do not stand." The entire virtue of the seventh is that he is the seventh to the first, that he can carry out the *avodah* and mission of the first—that "do not read *vayikra*, but *vayikria*." This is the belovedness of the seventh: that he draws down the *Shechinah*, and not only that, but he draws down the *essence* of the *Shechinah*. And even more, that he draws it down into the lower realms.

This is demanded of each and every one of us, the seventh generation. For all sevenths are beloved, and even though our being in the seventh generation is not by our choice or our *avodah*, and in many matters it may be contrary to our will, nevertheless, all sevenths are beloved. We find ourselves in the footsteps of *Moshiach*, at the end of the footsteps, and the *avodah* is to complete the drawing down of the *Shechinah*—and not just *Shechinah*, but the *essence* of the *Shechinah*, and in the lower realms specifically.

4) After the maamar explains that the essence of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms, and that afterwards Moses (the seventh) drew it down to the earth specifically, it says: "And the essence of the revelation of *Elokus* was in the Holy Temple" (and it brings the verse): "And they shall make Me a sanctuary, and I will dwell among them." It does not say "in it" (*betocho*), but "among them" (*betocham*)—within each and every one of Israel. This is also what is meant by "The *Tzaddikim* shall inherit the land and dwell forever upon it"—that the *Tzaddikim* inherit the land, which is the Garden of Eden, because they cause the level of "He who dwells forever, exalted and holy" to dwell (i.e., they draw it down) so that it will be revealed below. This is "I have come into my garden, my *ginuni*, to the place where My essence was initially; for the essence of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms." The matter is (to explain why the essence of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms specifically) that the ultimate intent in the creation and descent of the worlds is that the Holy One, Blessed be He, desired to have a dwelling place in the lower realms.

Our Rebbe, the Alter Rebbe, explains that the ultimate purpose of the descent of the worlds is not for the sake of the higher worlds, since for them it is a descent from the light of His countenance, and it cannot be said that the intent is for the sake of the descent. It is known that creation is only by the power of the Essence, as written in *Iggeres HaKodesh* (in the *maamar* "Ihu V'chayohi"), that His essence and being—whose existence is from His essence and has no prior cause, G-d forbid—is the only one with the power and ability to create *yesh mei'ayin* (something from nothing) and absolute nothingness. That is, the creation is not from the revelations but from the Essence. If so, it cannot be said that the purpose of creation is for the sake of the higher worlds, for even the world of *Atzilus* is a "revelation of the concealment," and thus it is a descent from the light of His countenance. For when the lights of *Atzilus* were in their concealment, they were at a much higher level. Furthermore, since they are only revelations, it cannot be said that the Essence is for the sake of revelations; rather, the purpose is this lowest world. As the Mitteler Rebbe explains in this *parsha* (*Beshalach*) regarding the difference between the higher worlds and this world: in this world, it is felt that its existence is from its own essence (as explained in the *Hemshech* of *Rosh Hashanah* of this year, the difference between a created being and light: light is evidence of the Luminary—when we see light, the light itself shows and reveals that there is a Luminary. Whereas a created being, not only does it not reveal the Creator, but it conceals and hides Him, and on the contrary, it is felt that its existence is from its own essence—though through intellect it is known that this is not so). And although this is only in its feeling, the very fact that it appears as if its existence is from its own essence is because its root is from the Essence, whose existence is from His essence. It is thus understood that the intent in creation and the *hishtalshelus* is not for the sake of the higher worlds, whose nature is revelation, but the intent is this lowest world, which appears to it that it is not a revelation but an essence—that is, its existence is from its own essence—and through the *avodah* within it, through *iskafya* and *is'hapcha*, the Essence is revealed. For its sake was the creation and *hishtalshelus* of the worlds.

5) It goes without saying that according to the view brought by the Tzemach Tzedek that even the *Keilim* (vessels) of *Atzilus* are a "revelation of the concealment," the intent is certainly not for the sake of *Atzilus*, for that is a descent and only a revelation. But even according to the second view brought there, that the *Keilim* are a creation of *yesh mei'ayin*, it is explained in many places that when we say the *Keilim* are a creation of *yesh mei'ayin*, it does not mean *yesh mei'ayin* absolutely, but only relative to the light. Because the root of the *Keilim* is from the *Reshimu*, which is a state of concealment, their existence is such that their source is in concealment; therefore, relative to the light, they are like a creation of *yesh mei'ayin*, but relative to the *Reshimu*, they are a revelation of the concealment. Thus, it is understood that the intent is not the higher worlds, but the *avodah* in this world of *iskafya* and *is'hapcha*. And although now, through the performance of *mitzvos*, we add lights in *Atzilus*, and so how can we say that even now the primary intent is this world specifically? To this, the Rebbe Maharash explains that these lights in *Atzilus* are there "as if placed in a box," for they are not for the sake of *Atzilus*, but for the sake of the lower world, and they are not revealed in *Atzilus*. This is what the Rebbe Rashab explains regarding why it cannot be said that the intent is for the sake of the higher worlds, since for them it is a descent from the light of His countenance: the meaning is that the higher worlds are revelations, which is a descent, and also that the Essence is distinct from the matter of revelations. Rather, the purpose is this lowest world, for so it arose in His will to have pleasure before Him when the *Sitra Achra* is subdued and darkness is transformed into light. As explained in the maamar, the entire *avodah* is to transform the "folly" of the *Sitra Achra* into the "folly" of holiness, and through this, there is pleasure before Him, "that I have spoken and My will was done," and through this, we draw down that He, may He be blessed, should have a dwelling place in the lower realms. Just as in a dwelling, a person dwells with his entire essence and being, so too in the lower realms, which are a dwelling for Him, the intent is that we draw down not only revelations, but that the Essence of the *Ein Sof* is present within them. This is the ultimate purpose of the creation and *hishtalshelus* of the worlds.

6) At the conclusion of the maamar, it is explained that because the essence of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms, and the primary revelation of this was in the Holy Temple, this is the reason the Tabernacle was made specifically of *shittim* wood. For the intent is to transform the "folly" of the *Sitra Achra* and the "boiling" of the animal soul into the "folly" of holiness, as our Sages say: "His folly helped the elder"—*avodah* and *bitul* that is above reason. All the matters that my revered father-in-law, the Rebbe, demanded of us, and likewise the other *Nesi'im*, they fulfilled themselves. This is like the teaching of our Sages on the verse, "He tells His words to Jacob, His statutes and His judgments to Israel"—what He does, He tells Israel to do and observe; and likewise, what He commands Israel, He does. This is the same with the instructions of our *Nesi'im*: what they demanded of those connected to them, they themselves fulfilled and did. And the fact that they revealed to us that they fulfilled it is so that it should be easier for us to fulfill them. For example, regarding the matter of *ahavas Yisrael*, there are many stories from each of the *Nesi'im*. For example, the Alter Rebbe, who interrupted his prayer to go and chop wood, cook soup, and feed a woman who had just given birth, because there were no people there in the house. From the Mitteler Rebbe, how someone entered for *yechidus* and complained about the matters that young men complain about. The Mitteler Rebbe revealed his arm and said to him, "Do you not see that my skin has shriveled on my bones, etc., and all this is from your sins?" This shows the wonder and loftiness of the Mitteler Rebbe in general, and specifically regarding those who are connected to such matters; nevertheless, the connection with them was so great that because of their matters, which were not as they should be, it caused him extreme physical weakness, until his skin shriveled on his bones. From the Tzemach Tzedek, how he went before prayer to provide a loan to a simple person whose livelihood was affected. From the Rebbe Maharash, who once traveled specifically from a health resort to Paris and met a young man there and said to him: "Young man, *yayin nesech* dulls the mind and the heart; be a Jew." The young man went home and did not rest until he came to the Rebbe Maharash, repented, and a family of G-d-fearing people came from him. It is known that for the Rebbe Maharash, time was very precious, to the extent that even the saying of *Chassidus* was brief, and at certain times, at the eighth hour in the morning, he was already after prayer; yet he traveled a long distance and stayed there for a period of time for the sake of a young man, etc. From the Rebbe Rashab at the beginning of his *nesi'us*, when a new decree was issued and it was necessary to travel to Moscow regarding it. His older brother, the Rebbe RZ"A, said to him, "Time is precious to you, and you do not know the language of the country well (the Rebbe RZ"A was learned in languages), and you also need to find connections; therefore, I will travel regarding this according to your instructions." But the Rebbe Rashab did not agree to this and traveled himself and succeeded. And there are many stories from my revered father-in-law, the Rebbe, regarding his efforts to do a favor, even for a private individual, in spiritual or material matters. He gave of himself for this, not only his material self but also his spiritual self, even though the one he was benefiting was not only not in the category of "your fellow in Torah and *mitzvos*," but was not on his level at all.

7) Through *iskafya* and *is'hapcha* to the "folly" of holiness, we fulfill the intent of creation, which is that He, may He be blessed, should have a dwelling place in the lower realms. And this—that through *iskafya* and *is'hapcha* a dwelling is made in the lower realms, "I have come into my garden"—is at a higher level than before the sin. Just as when one tears down a previous building in order to build, it is obvious that the new building must be at a higher level than the previous one, so too it must be said that through *iskafya* and *is'hapcha*, a dwelling is made at a higher level. As explained in the maamar, through the subduing of the *Sitra Achra*, "the glory of the Holy One, Blessed be He, is exalted in all the worlds"—that is, the light that is in all the worlds equally. And although the language in the maamar is *Ohr Sovev*, it is impossible to say that the intent is that a light is drawn down that is within the category of the worlds but surrounds them; rather, the intent is that a lofty light is drawn down that is not in the category of worlds at all. Therefore, he calls this revelation of light "exalted" (*istalek*). Therefore, the passing of *Tzaddikim* is also called *histalkus* (passing/exaltation), for *histalkus* is the revelation of a most lofty light. There are two letters in *Iggeres HaKodesh* that explain the matter of *histalkus*. In the second letter, the matter of *histalkus* is explained regarding the Red Heifer. The matters done inside cannot purify from the impurity of death, only the matter done outside specifically—the heifer done outside. To this, the death of *Tzaddikim* is compared. Now, there is no Red Heifer, because due to our sins we had to be exiled from our land, but the passing of *Tzaddikim* occurred. Regarding the passing of *Tzaddikim*, there are two statements of our Sages: "The death of *Tzaddikim* is as weighty as the burning of the House of our G-d." "The passing of *Tzaddikim* is harder than the destruction of the Holy Temple." Through all this, the glory of the Holy One, Blessed be He, is exalted. The meaning of *histalkus* was explained by all the Rebbes—the Alter Rebbe, the Mitteler Rebbe, the Tzemach Tzedek, the Rebbe Maharash, the Rebbe Rashab, and my revered father-in-law, the Rebbe—that the intent of the word *histalkus* is not that it is an ascent above, G-d forbid, but that he is present below, only that he is in a state of transcendence. This is what is demanded of us, the seventh generation to the Alter Rebbe, and all sevenths are beloved: that even though we have not earned it and have not labored for it, nevertheless, all sevenths are beloved, and the *avodah* of the seventh generation is to draw down the *Shechinah* literally below. To transform the "folly" of the animal soul—which a person knows in his soul that he has, and the "boiling" of the animal soul that he has, and perhaps sometimes even lower—to make from it and transform it into the "folly" of holiness.

8) This is the matter of the *Tzaddik* who passes away: that even though there were already many concealments and hidden things and many difficulties and matters not understood, all this was not enough. And in order that "the glory of the Holy One, Blessed be He, be exalted in all the worlds," there was also the matter of the passing of *Tzaddikim*, which is harder not only than the destruction, but *more* than the destruction. The ultimate intent in this is that through it, the glory of the Holy One, Blessed be He, will be exalted. This is demanded of each one of us: to know that we find ourselves in the seventh generation, and the entire virtue of the seventh is that he is the seventh to the first, whose conduct was that he did not seek anything for himself, not even *mesiras nefesh*. For he knew that his entire matter is as it is written: "And he called there in the name of Hashem, the Eternal G-d." And like the conduct of Abraham our forefather, who came into such places where they did not know of G-dliness, did not know of Judaism, and did not even know of the *Aleph-Beis*, and while being there, one set oneself aside, and "do not read *vayikra* but *vayikria*." It is known that in the study of the rule of "do not read," both readings exist. And even in this, it is explicit in the Written Torah: *vayikra*. Nevertheless, one must know that if he wants the *vayikra* to be realized, there must be the *vayikria*. You must see to it that the other not only knows, but also cries out. Even though until now the other knew of nothing, now you must see to it that he cries out "to the Eternal G-d" (*El Olam*), not "to the world" (*El HaOlam*)—that is, that G-dliness is a matter unto itself and the world a matter unto itself, but that G-dliness rules and governs the world, or rather that the world and G-dliness are entirely one.

9) And although who is he whose heart encourages him to say, "I will perform the *avodah* of Abraham our forefather," nevertheless, a small part belongs to each one, and one is obligated in this, and the powers were given to him for this through the conduct shown by the first, and from him onwards until, and including, the conduct shown by my revered father-in-law, the Rebbe, who paved the way and gave us the powers for this. This itself is the belovedness of the seventh generation: that so many powers were given and revealed for our sake. Through *avodah* in such a manner, the essence of the *Shechinah* will be drawn down below in this physical and material world, and it will be at a level even higher than before the sin, as it is written regarding *Moshiach*: "And he shall be exalted and lifted up" more than the first man, and even as it was before the sin. And my revered father-in-law, the Rebbe, who "bore our illnesses and carried our pains," and "was wounded because of our transgressions, crushed because of our iniquities"—just as he saw our distress, so too, speedily in our days and in our time, may he redeem the flock of his pasture from the spiritual exile and the physical exile together, and set us in a pillar of light. But all this is still only revelations, and even more—that He will bind and unite us with the essence and being of the *Ein Sof*, Blessed be He. This is the inner intent of the descent and *hishtalshelus* of the worlds, the matter of sin and its rectification, and the matter of the passing of *Tzaddikim*, through which the glory of the Holy One, Blessed be He, will be exalted. And when He takes us out of exile with a high hand, and "all the children of Israel had light in their dwellings," then "Moses and the children of Israel shall sing... Hashem shall reign forever and ever" (as it is in the prayer text), and also in the language of the Targum: "Hashem's kingdom stands forever and ever." And we conclude, "And Hashem shall be King... Hashem is One and His Name is One," that there will be no difference between Hashem and His Name. All this is accomplished through the passing of *Tzaddikim*, which is harder even than the destruction of the Holy Temple. And since we have already passed through all these matters, now the matter depends only on us—the seventh generation. And we shall merit to see ourselves with the Rebbe here below in a body and below ten *tefachim*, and he will redeem us.

## B’s”d. Shabbos Parshas Beshalach, 13 Shevat, 5711

"You who dwell in the gardens, companions listen to your voice". In his maamar (a continuation of the maamar regarding his passing), the Rebbe Rayatz brings two interpretations that, on the face of it, are opposites. The first interpretation is that "gardens" refers to this world, and "you who dwell in the gardens" refers to the Congregation of Israel, which is scattered in exile, grazing in the gardens of others. Nevertheless, when they sit in synagogues and houses of study, the "companions"—the ministering angels, who are called companions because there is no jealousy, hatred, or competition among them—listen to your voice. The second interpretation is that "gardens" refers to Gan Eden. Although there are infinite levels within it, they are generally divided into two levels: the Lower Gan Eden and the Upper Gan Eden. "Companions listen" refers to the souls in Gan Eden, who are called companions, as is explained in a parenthetical note that sometimes one soul is "emitted" from another. It can be said that the precision in this is to teach us that the connection between souls is not merely a general connection, but that one soul is emitted from another. The meaning of "emanation" (atzilus) has been explained previously (in the maamar *Basi L’Gani*) as the revelation of what was hidden—meaning it is one thing; it is the very same thing, only it has descended and been revealed from its state of concealment. Just as hidden powers subsequently descend and are revealed from their concealment, it is impossible to say that the purpose of creation is for the sake of the world of Atzilus, for since it is a revelation of the hidden, it is a descent. Rather, the ultimate purpose is this lower world. It is the same regarding the connection of the souls mentioned above, which is a matter of emanation and the revelation of the hidden.

To understand the connection between these two interpretations, the maamar explains beforehand that when a soul descends below and is enclothed in a body, even though the body conceals and hides, nevertheless, through avodah, one removes the concealment and hiding. On the contrary, through this descent and enclothement, it is strengthened with an advantage of power. The precision of the expression "on the contrary" is as the Alter Rebbe explains (Iggeres HaKodesh, Section 25) regarding the words of the Baal Shem Tov concerning anger—for example, when an uncircumcised person stands before him, speaking and distracting him—that not only is he not affected by him, and he continues to daven as if the person had not distracted him, but even more: he experiences a strengthening and an addition of power when he contemplates the exile of the Shechinah, which is enclothed in a state of exile within this distracting speech. It turns out that through this distraction, the person is aroused even more. 

Similarly, the Mitteler Rebbe explains (in *Torah Ohr* the matter is brief, but in *Toras Chayim* on this parsha it is explained at length) regarding the verse, "And it was when Pharaoh sent out the people", that the intent is that not only did the kelipah of Pharaoh not conceal and hide, but on the contrary, he even assisted. As it is written, "And Pharaoh drew near", and it is stated in the Midrash that he "drew near" the hearts of Israel to their Father in Heaven. Furthermore, "Pharaoh sent out the people"—Pharaoh went along with the people—meaning that even the sparks that had fallen there ascended with them. This is also the precision of the expression "on the contrary": it is not just that the body does not conceal and hide the soul, leaving it in its state as if there were no concealment or hiding, but on the contrary, through the initial concealment, the person is strengthened with an advantage of power. This is the primary intent in the creation of the worlds: *iskafya* (subjugation) and *is’hapcha* (transformation) of the foolishness of the *sitra achra*—the "cooking" (passion) of the animal soul and the affairs of the world—into holiness. 

Although it is explained in many places that the performance of mitzvos is to add lights in Atzilus, it has already been explained above that the essential aspect of this is in this world. As the Tzemach Tzedek and the Rebbe Maharash explained, the lights as they are in Atzilus are like things placed in a box. Not only is the primary revelation—the primary dwelling of the Shechinah—in this world (as has been explained at length above by the Rebbe Rashab regarding the meaning of "the primary dwelling of the Shechinah"), but even more than this, the Alter Rebbe explains (and this is also from the Maggid), regarding the saying of our Sages, "Know what is above you," that you should know that "what is above"—all the matters of above—depends on you and is within you.

This is the concept of "You who dwell in the gardens": the souls that dwell in the Lower Gan Eden and the Upper Gan Eden come to listen to the voice of the souls of Israel below. The intent is that even the souls as they are above, before their descent into a body, and also those souls that have already been in a body—which possess the advantage that comes from the avodah of the body, because while they were in the body, "days were fashioned"[ftnref_2401_d] and "one of them was His", meaning they drew down the "One" and completed all their avodah, for which reason they are called "Israel, the hosts of Hashem" (as explained in the aforementioned maamar of the Rebbe Rayatz)—all of them come to listen to the voice of the souls enclothed in bodies. 

This applies even if their avodah is not yet complete—not in the first sense of "host" as an army, which implies *kabbalas ol* (acceptance of the yoke); not in the second sense of a fixed time, meaning that at the appointed time they complete all their avodah; and not in the third sense of *tzivyon* (beauty), which means not just will, but beauty, which comes from the interiority of Keter and causes *taanug* (delight), for which they are called "hosts of Hashem." Even so, they come to listen to the voice of the souls enclothed in bodies, even if they have not yet completed their avodah. For even though they are scattered in the exile and grazing in the gardens of others, nevertheless, they tear themselves away from the world, taking along the body and the animal soul, and they come into the synagogues and houses of study, recite the Shema, and occupy themselves with Torah. This is a matter of literal *mesiras nefesh* (self-sacrifice), and this is something one possesses only in this world. Therefore, the "companions"—even the souls in Gan Eden—listen to their voice, and the Holy One, Blessed be He, also says, "I and My entourage come to hear your voice." This is the meaning of "days were fashioned and *lo* (not)"—it is written *lo* (not) but read *lo* (to Him)—that in their avodah, the avodah of the soul in the body, they draw down from above the *hishtalshelus* (chain of worlds) to below the *hishtalshelus*, "One among them," so that "Hashem will be One and His Name One".

## 10 Shevat, 5712

**I have come into my garden**. It is stated in the Midrash Rabbah (in its place) that "into my garden" means "into my garden-bed," to the place where My essence was initially, for the essence of the *Shechinah* was in the lower realms. Due to sins—specifically the sin of the Tree of Knowledge—the *Shechinah* withdrew from the earth to the first heaven, and through subsequent sins, it withdrew from heaven to heaven, until the seventh heaven. Afterward, the *tzaddikim* arose and brought the *Shechinah* down from heaven to heaven, until the seventh came—and all sevenths are beloved—who is Moshe, and he brought it down below to the earth. This is the meaning of "The *tzaddikim* shall inherit the earth and dwell upon it forever," for the *tzaddikim* inherit the earth—which is *Gan Eden*—because they draw down and cause the *Shechinah* to dwell in the lower earth, which is the ultimate intent in the creation and formation of the worlds. The primary revelation of this drawing down of the *Shechinah* below is in the *Mikdash*, as it is written, "And they shall make Me a *Mikdash*, and I will dwell within them." Our Sages of blessed memory were precise in this: it does not say "within it" (*betocho*), but "within them" (*betocham*)—within each and every one of the Jewish people. This means that when each individual Jew performs within himself the same *avodah* that took place in the *Mikdash*, the essence of the *Shechinah* dwells within him, just as it was at the beginning of the creation of the worlds, and even more so than at the beginning, and an essential dwelling place is made in the lower realms**[ftnref_2413_ב])**.

Now, one of the primary *avodos* in the *Mikdash* was the *avodah* of the *korbanos*, which is a spiritual *avodah*. Even the *avodah* of the *korbanos* in the *Mikdash*, where they offered a physical animal, was a spiritual *avodah*. Proof of this is the participation of the *Kohanim* in their *avodah* and the *Levi'im* in their song and music at the time the *korban* was brought, which are spiritual matters. As it is written in the *Zohar***[ftnref_2414_ג])**, the *Kohanim* are in their silence and the *re'uta d'liba* (desire of the heart), which is an *hemshech* (drawing down), and the *Levi'im* are in song and music, which is a matter of *ha'alah* (raising up) from below to above. Thus, the *avodah* of *korbanos* in the *Mikdash* was also a spiritual *avodah*; how much more so is the *avodah* of *korbanos* as it exists in each and every Jew a spiritual *avodah*. This is what is written at the beginning of the *parsha* of the *korbanos*: "When a person (*adam*) among you brings a *korban* to *Hashem*." It is known**[ftnref_2424_ד])** how the *Alter Rebbe* explains this: it should have said, "When a person among you brings," so why does it say "When a person brings *from you*"? The explanation is: "When a person brings a *korban*," when a person comes to draw close to *Elokus*—for this is the essence of a *korban*, as why[ftnref_2425_ה]) is it called a *korban*? Only because it draws close (*mekarev*), for *korbanos* are a matter of drawing together the powers and the senses—"then *from you* shall be the *korban* to *Hashem*," meaning you must offer it from yourselves.

Just as the matter of *korbanos* in the physical sense involves taking a whole and perfect animal and slaughtering it—meaning one extracts its life-force—and afterward comes the offering of the *eimurim*, where the fat is burned on the fire upon the *mizbe'ach*, and even more so in the *korban olah*, the beginning of the *korbanos*, which ascends entirely to the *mizbe'ach*—so too is it in the *avodah* of *korbanos* in spirituality. One must be a "perfect animal," meaning one must examine oneself to ensure the "animal" within him is perfect—including the body and the *nefesh habehamis*. This examination must be done *ada'ata d'nafshi* (with personal intent), not as a "commandment of men learned by rote", for in that manner, the examination will not be good or sufficient. But when one knows that the matter touches upon the very life of his soul, then he examines himself in every corner of his soul—in the *mochin*, the *midos*, and especially in the three garments of thought, speech, and action—and then he will know all his matters that require rectification. And because the matter touches upon his very life, he will perform and fulfill in practice the necessary rectifications so that there will be no blemish in him**[ftnref_2426_ו])**.

After the examination, he then desires to be a *korban*, so he "slaughters" it—meaning the flesh and so on remains intact, but he extracts the life-force from it. He extracts the life-force from physical matters, meaning the body of the physical things remains, but he uses them only so that he will have additional strength in the *avodah* of *Hashem*, as the saying goes[ftnref_2427_ז]) regarding wine and fragrance that sharpen the mind, and the fact that he does not eat the meat of an ox—I have not delved into the reason for that. All this applies to matters that are optional, and especially to matters of *mitzvah*, such as eating on *Shabbos* and *Yom Tov*, or *tzitzis* made of physical wool and *tefillin* made of physical parchment—meaning physical things with which one performs a *mitzvah* itself, and not just the command of "In all your ways know Him"[ftnref_2429_ט]). This is the *avodah* of *korbanos*: to extract the life-force from physical matters so that one uses them only for the *avodah* of *Hashem*. Then it is "from you a *korban*," from yourself. And when a person offers "from you a *korban*," then it is a *korban* to *Hashem*, for he draws close and unites with *Hashem*.

b) However, when one contemplates his standing and situation *ada'ata d'nafshi* as mentioned above, he will often see and be convinced that even if he is not currently a sinner, nevertheless, "my sin is before me always". The sins he committed throughout the days of his life—including those he did before *Bar Mitzvah*, as the *Mitteler Rebbe* writes**[ftnref_2434_י])**—still exist, for he has not yet rectified them. A convincing proof that he has not rectified them is that if he had done *teshuvah* as required, then not only would he not have the issue of "one sin drags another", but on the contrary, because he became a *baal teshuvah*, his *avodah* from that point forward should have been with greater strength (*bechila yatir*) than it was until now. For the fact that he was previously in a "land of drought and shadow of death" should have aroused in him a greater longing for *Elokus*. But in his contemplation of his true standing and situation, without easing the burden on himself and without deceiving himself at all, he feels in his soul the exact opposite. Not only did his previous standing and situation not bring him to the "great love" where "willful sins become for him like merits"[ftnref_2435_יא])**, but on the contrary, it caused him and brought him to a state of coarseness and thickness, to the point where he is in the pattern of "one sin drags another." From all this, there is a convincing proof that for him, the matter of "my sin is before me always" is not that he has already done proper *teshuvah*, but rather that according to his current standing and situation, his previous *teshuvah* is insufficient and he now needs a most exalted *teshuvah***[ftnref_2436_יב])**; or rather, that from the outset he did not rectify his matters at all, and they are a screen separating him from his Father in Heaven**[ftnref_2437_יג])**, interrupting and preventing the *avodah* of *Hashem*.

When he contemplates all this, and also knows the greatness of the matter of *korbanos*—that it is the drawing together of the powers and the senses until he is included in the fire from above, and "the mystery of the *korban* ascends to the mystery of the *Ein Sof*" as will be explained—he asks himself: what connection do I have to the matter of *korbanos*? How can I desire to be a *korban* to *Hashem* when I am full of flaws? To resolve this difficulty, the *Rebbe Rayatz* adds to the *Alter Rebbe's* explanation of the word "from you" (*mechem*), saying: *mechem* means "from you and upon you the matter depends"—it is dependent upon you yourself. Regardless of his standing and situation and everything he has passed through until now, knowing the afflictions of his heart, nevertheless, "upon you the matter depends," to the point that each and every one can say, "When will my deeds reach the deeds of my ancestors, Avraham, Yitzchak, and Yaakov?". This is "from you a *korban* to *Hashem*": upon you the matter depends to be a *korban* to *Hashem*, to draw close and unite with *Hashem*. For *korbanos* are to *Hashem* specifically, not to *Elokim*, which has the same *gematria* as *hateva* (nature), but to *Hashem* specifically. As is explained in the *Zohar***[ftnref_2446_יד])** on the verse "He who sacrifices to *Elokim* shall be destroyed," the matter of *korbanos* is to *Hashem* specifically, above the name *Elokim*. In truth, it reaches even higher, "before *Hashem*," for "the mystery of the *korban* ascends to the mystery of the *Ein Sof***[ftnref_2447_טו])**," which is above even *Hashem*. For the name *Havayah*, even though it is above the name *Elokim*, nevertheless its four letters hint at *tzimtzum*, *hitpashtus*, *hemshech*, and *hitpashtus*, which is a *seder* of *hishtalshelus*. But *Ein Sof* is above *Havayah*, and it is there that the *avodah* of *korbanos* reaches. This is why it concludes with the matter of offering "to his will before *Hashem*." Before this, it says "a *korban* to *Hashem*," and at the conclusion of the matter, after saying "from the animal, from the cattle, and from the flock," it says "before *Hashem*." The *Tzemach Tzedek* explains[ftnref_2448_טז]) that "before *Hashem*" means above *Hashem*. This is the matter of *korbanos*: that the *avodah* is not only in the *nefesh elokis*, but "from the animal, from the cattle, and from the flock," which is the *nefesh habehamis*. Especially as it was in the *Mikdash*, where the *avodah* of *korbanos* could be with a physical animal, through which one reaches "before *Hashem*," the mystery of the *Ein Sof*.

c) Now, regarding *korbanos* and likewise regarding the drawing together of the powers and the senses, the physical *korban* was included in the fire from above. Similarly, in the *avodah* of *korbanos* in each and every Jew, the *Rebbe Rayatz* explains in the *maamar* that the *korbanos* must be included in the fire from above. This is the "flashes of fire" within the *nefesh elokis*, which is the fire from above, as it is written, "Its flashes are flashes of fire, a flame of *Yah*." The root of the love within the *nefesh elokis* is from the fire from above, the flame of *Yah*; therefore, the love within the *nefesh elokis* while it is below is also called the fire from above.

The explanation of why he calls the supernal love and that of the *nefesh elokis* "a flame of *Yah*" is found in *Sefer Yetzirah*: the ten *Sefiros* are like a flame bound to a coal. The *Rebbe Maharash* explains**[ftnref_2449_יז])** that there are two aspects to this: the flame within the coal and the flame above the coal. The flame above the coal can also exist on its own, yet it has no reality or substance; therefore, they said in the *Mishnah***[ftnref_2450_יח])** that one who carries out a flame is exempt, from which we know these two aspects: that the flame exists on its own—hence it is relevant to discuss the law of carrying out a flame—but the flame has no reality or substance, hence one who carries out a flame is exempt.
