Before the world was created—which includes the time before it was *ne'etzal* (emanated), as it is stated in *Sefer HaKabbalah*—there was "He and His Name alone". Afterward, the *Sefiros* were emanated. These two aspects—the ten *Sefiros* hidden in their Emanator and the ten *Sefiros* as they were emanated—are analogous to the two aspects of the flame. The flame within the coal, even though it is more intense**[ftnref_2456_יט])**, as it is within the coal, there is no reality of a flame there, and certainly no details of the flame. Its reality is not recognizable there, only the reality of the coal. Rather, there are proofs and evidence that a flame exists even within the coal—and on the contrary, it is more intense—but what is recognizable is only the coal. Similarly, and even more so, this is the case with the general matter of the ten *Sefiros* hidden in their Emanator: what exists there is only the Emanator, but there are proofs from the ten *Sefiros* that were emanated that the ten *Sefiros* hidden in the Emanator exist. Afterward, the ten *Sefiros* were emanated, but even after their emanation, they are bound to their source—the ten *Sefiros* hidden—like the flame above the coal that is bound to the flame within the coal. This is its entire existence; for if it were to become separated from its source, it would be nothing and zero. This is the "flame of *Yah*": just as the flame exists as it is in the coal, as it is above the coal and bound to it, and the flame above the coal can also exist on its own in separation, so too is it in the *seder hishtalshelus*. The revealed ten *Sefiros*, even though they are bound to their source—"He and His life are one, He and His attributes are one", analogous to the flame above the coal which is one thing with the flame within the coal, which is one with the coal—nevertheless, due to the multiplicity of the *hishtalshelus*, it is possible, ultimately, to give room for the error of separation, as our Sages said**[ftnref_2457_כ])**, "It is written, and whoever wishes to err may err"—that when the Almighty says "whoever wishes to err (by the writing) may err," it means that from the perspective of the flame, there can be room for the error of separation. The flame itself is the "flame of *Yah*," where the *Yud* also has the "tip of the *Yud*"—meaning the revealed *Sefiros* and the hidden *Sefiros*—both are the "flame of *Yah*," in which there is no separation, *chas v'shalom*.

The love within the *nefesh elokis* is also called "flame of *Yah*." For "In the beginning" (*Bereishis*) is for the sake of Israel, who are called "beginning" (*reishis*); since the entire *hishtalshelus* is for the sake of Israel, it is understood that all matters in the *hishtalshelus* have their counterparts and act within Israel. Similarly, it is also regarding the "flame of *Yah*" that acts within Israel so that they have the "flame of *Yah*," a love for *Elokus*. It is written, "For *Hashem* your *Elokim* loves you," and the explanation in *Chassidus***[ftnref_2458_כא])** is that He draws down to be within you love, and He loves you, and one is dependent on the other. "For *Hashem* your *Elokim* loves you" refers to the *Atzmus* of the *Ein Sof*, and from the "flame of *Yah*" above, this is also made within Israel. In this, there are, generally, two modes of love. Just as above there are the ten *Sefiros* as they are in the Emanator and as they are emanated, so too in love there are two such modes. The *midos* come from the *mochin*; when one contemplates the matters that lead to the love of Him, he gives birth to the love of *Hashem*. However, when he deepens himself in the intellectual grasp, as it should be, and he is "drowned" and unified with the intellectual grasp, then there is no reality to the *midah*, for the *midah* is included in its cause and is not on its own. Only when he "carries himself away" from the cause and lets himself down to the effect does the opposite occur: the reality of the *midah* is primarily felt, as it is in revelation, yet it is bound to the contemplation that gave birth to it—more specifically, to the intellectual arousal within the contemplation—and this gives life and intensity to the *midah*. Just as in the parable of the flame above the coal, which is bound to the flame within the coal—whereby there is constant existence, life, and intensity in the flame—so too is it with the love that must be bound with the love as it is included in the cause. This is the "flame of *Yah*" in which all the *korbanos* that one offers are included.

d) Now, it was explained above that *korbanos* are "before *Hashem*," meaning above *Hashem*. Consequently, it is understood that the fire from above in which the *korban* is included is also above *Hashem*. In *avodah*, this means that the *avodah* is above *taam v'daas* (reason and knowledge). Just as in the *avodah* of *korbanos* in the *Beis HaMikdash* they offered an animal specifically, so too is it now: "The prayers were instituted corresponding to the daily *korbanos*"; the *avodah* of the *nefesh elokis* must be with the *nefesh habehamis* specifically**[ftnref_2465_כב])**, and not the *avodah* of the *neshamah* on its own. And this *avodah* specifically reaches above *taam v'daas*, above the *seder hishtalshelus*. Even though the *avodah* of the *neshamah* on its own is also through the body and in the body—for the Giving of the Torah was specifically to souls in bodies, and the fulfillment of *mitzvos* is through the body, even the *mitzvos* of the duties of the heart, as explained in many places**[ftnref_2466_כג])**—nevertheless, it is possible that it might not be relevant to the body and will not affect it. For example, when one studies Torah and prays, while he is within the "four cubits of *Halacha*" and the "four cubits of prayer," he stands in a state of refinement, but when he is removed from this, when he finishes his study and prayer, he catches himself being in the same coarseness as before, and sometimes even more *yeshus* (self-centeredness) is added due to the satisfaction he has in his *avodah*.

It may be that this is analogous to the distinction between fulfilling *mitzvos* with a separate object versus fulfilling *mitzvos* with the body. Fulfilling *mitzvos* with a separate object, like *tzitzis* on physical wool and *tefillin* on physical parchment, there is no recognizable change[ftnref_2467_כד]) to the physical eye in the parchment and the wool; the changes that must be in the parchment and the wool so that they are suitable for the fulfillment of the *mitzvah* are through human labor, but the changes made by the designation and fulfillment of the *mitzvah* are not recognizable. In this way, it is possible for the fulfillment of *mitzvos* through the body and in the body to be like fulfilling them with a separate object—meaning the body is only a medium through which the *neshamah* studies Torah and fulfills *mitzvos*. This is not the ultimate purpose of the *avodah*; rather, the purpose is the *avodah* in the body and the *nefesh habehamis* to transform them from one extreme to the other, so that it is recognizable in the body that it is a body that studies Torah and fulfills *mitzvos*, and all the more so that this will be recognizable in the *nefesh habehamis*. Then, not only will he not be relevant to forbidden things, but he will also be removed from matters of luxury; even in optional matters, he will only desire and want what is necessary for the health of the body in order to serve the Almighty. But if his *avodah* is the *avodah* of the *neshamah* on its own, and the body is only a medium, this is not the ultimate intent and it is not a *korban* "to His will before *Hashem*." Therefore, the *avodah* in the body and the *nefesh habehamis* is mandatory; he must work and rework the body and the *nefesh habehamis*. As the *Rebbe Rashab* said**[ftnref_2470_כה])**, the *neshamah* was formed from the *oros* (lights)—the general category of *oros* is their revelation—and the body was formed from the *Atzmus* (Essence), whose existence is from His Essence, and there is no cause or reason preceding it, *chas v'shalom*. The *neshamah*, which recognizes the superiority of the body in this, wants to attain the *Atzmus*, and for this reason, it wants to connect with the body and the *nefesh habehamis* and rework them. Therefore, the will and intellect of the *neshamah* itself demands and requires connecting with the body—not to work through it as with a separate object, but to connect with the body and work together.

However, in truth, this is still not enough to be "to His will before *Hashem*," for all this is *avodah* that is from the perspective of *taam v'daas*, as the *neshamah* understands the superiority of the *Atzmus* and therefore wants the *Atzmus*, and thus works with the body. Even though it is *taam v'daas* of *kedushah*, it is still *taam v'daas*. But the ultimate *avodah* is that it should not be for the sake of the good and the superiority that he will receive through the *avodah*, but only to fulfill the supernal will. The supernal will is that He should have a dwelling place in the lower realms, specifically in this world. Therefore, the *neshamah*, which is a "part of *Elokah* from above"—"you are children"—feels the will of the Father, and for this reason, its *avodah* is in the body specifically. In this, there is also a superiority even over the level of a "servant". For a servant, even though his *avodah* is also not from the perspective of the taste and pleasure he has in it, nevertheless, the servant only fulfills the master's command—meaning he knows the master's command but does not feel his will and does not know why he wants it. Not so the *neshamah*: "You are children to *Hashem* your *Elokim*"; even though its *avodah* is also not from the perspective of taste, but to fulfill the Father's will, it feels the will and knows its reason. This is why, after saying "from you a *korban* to *Hashem*," it concludes "from the animal, from the cattle, and from the flock." "From you a *korban* to *Hashem*" refers to the *nefesh elokis*, but because "the mystery of the *korban* ascends to the mystery of the *Ein Sof*," above *Hashem*, for this there must be "from the animal"—the *avodah* with the *nefesh habehamis* and the body—and there must be a specific *avodah* in this, "from the cattle and from the flock," to contemplate and deepen into the details of his soul's matters and his *avodah* in the body and *nefesh habehamis*, and then it is "to His will before *Hashem*."

e) Now, the introduction to the *parsha* of the *korbanos* is "And He called to Moshe," which is the empowerment[ftnref_2477_כו]) for the *avodah* of the *korbanos*. For even though "from you a *korban* to *Hashem*," and "from you and upon you the matter depends to be a *korban* to *Hashem*," because he is in a low state, and the more he contemplates, the more he sees his lowliness and distance and falls in his spirit, for this the opening is "And He called to Moshe," which is the giving of ability and strength to each and every person to be a *korban* to *Hashem*, and even "before *Hashem*." Therefore, it is not said who is calling[ftnref_2478_כז]) because it is from a place most high and awesome, the light of the *Ein Sof* that encompasses all worlds, the "You are holy" that is above your name "Holy." For Moshe is the shepherd of Israel, and it is stated in the Midrash[ftnref_2479_כח]) that they tested him by the fact that he tended to the smallest ones, for from this specifically is the proof that he is a shepherd as required. And so it is in every generation, "the extension of Moshe in every generation", who are the shepherds of Israel in every generation; they are the ones who draw down strength for the *avodah* of *korbanos*, for they are the *memutza* (intermediary) who connects, an intermediary between *Hashem* and you. He is the intermediary who connects, who unifies worlds with *Elokus* through souls. This is the making of the dwelling place for Him in the lower realms, "And they shall make Me a *Mikdash*, and I will dwell within them"—within each and every one—which is incumbent upon each and every one.

One of the primary *avodos* in this is the *avodah* of *korbanos*, as mentioned above: to contemplate his standing and situation *ada'ata d'nafshi*, without being deceived, and nevertheless, they tell him "from you and upon you the matter depends" to rectify so that he will be perfect without blemish, and to offer "from you a *korban*" and a *korban* "from the animal." This is through the *hiskashrus* (connection), that he adds to it and ascends, in the "extension of Moshe in every generation," for in this extension, the *Atzmus* is also found. Through this, he connects with the "And He called to Moshe," for "no thought can grasp Him at all," and the "flame of *Yah*" is drawn down, the fire from above, in which the *korban* is included, and through this, "I will dwell within them." Likewise, regarding our generation, the shepherd and *Nasi* of Israel, who showed us the straight path, that we should walk in his ways and paths, "the deed which they shall do and the deed which they shall not do"—through this, the strength was given and drawn down so that each and every one, in whatever situation he finds himself, in one moment and in one instant, it is possible to prepare to offer "from you a *korban* to *Hashem*," so that his powers and senses will be included in the fire from above, and even more so, that he will also offer the body and *nefesh habehamis*, "from the animal, from the cattle, and from the flock," and through this, they will be "to His will before *Hashem*," above *Hashem*. As the *Rebbe* says in the *maamar*, that "when the *sitra achra* is subdued, the glory of the Holy One, blessed be He, is exalted in all the worlds", for the *sovev kol almin* (that which encompasses all worlds), which is equal in all the worlds, will be drawn down through the *avodah* of subduing the *sitra achra*, the *avodah* with the body and *nefesh habehamis*. And when each and every one[ftnref_2486_ל]) fulfills this, then the entire life-force of this world will ascend to *kedushah*, and "the glory of *Hashem* will fill the entire earth", which is the ultimate purpose of the days of *Mashiach* and the resurrection of the dead, the purpose and perfection of the creation of the worlds, speedily in our days.

## 10 Shevat, 5713

"I have come into my garden [ftnref_2490_א], my sister, my bride." The Midrash Rabbah states (in its place): "Into my garden" — into my *ginuni*, the place where My essence was initially. The essence of the *Shechinah* was originally in the lower realms. Due to the sin of the Tree of Knowledge and the subsequent sins, the *Shechinah* was removed from below to above, up to the seventh heaven. The *Tzaddikim* who followed, up to Moshe Rabbeinu—who is the seventh, and "all sevenths are beloved "—drew the *Shechinah* down from above to below, into this physical earth. Regarding this drawing down, it is written : "And they shall make Me a sanctuary, and I will dwell in them (*betocham*)." It does not say "in it" (*betocho*), but "in them" (*betocham*)—within each and every Jew. This means that because the *Tzaddikim* perform all these matters themselves and effect this within the people of their generation, through this they draw the *Shechinah* into the lower realms just as it was in the beginning.

Moreover, they draw the very essence of the *Shechinah* into the lower realms, as discussed in the *maamar* of the day of *histalkus* and the *yahrzeit*, explaining the statement of the *Zohar* : "When the *sitra achra* is subdued, the glory of the *Kudsha Brich Hu* is exalted in all worlds." This means the light that shines in all worlds equally. This drawing down is accomplished through the *avodah* of *iskafya* of the *sitra achra*, which, among the *avodos* of the *Mishkan*, is the *avodah* of the offerings, and specifically the act of the *ketores*. This is also the reason that the *Mishkan* was made specifically of *shittim* wood. *Shittah* means a deviation, and also implies *shtus* (folly). That is, there is a middle path, and a deviation to either side—above or below—is called *shittah*. The deviation above the middle path is *shtus d'kedushah* (folly of holiness), and the deviation below is *shtus* of the opposite side. The path of *Torah* and *mitzvos* in general is the middle path, as the Rambam writes in *Hilchos Deos*. Deviation above the middle path is *shtus d'kedushah*; one must have this *shtus d'kedushah*, especially when one needs to be careful not to fall into the *shtus* of the opposite side, which is achieved specifically through *shtus d'kedushah*. As the saying goes, "His folly helped the elder," meaning the *shtus d'kedushah* helps and rectifies the *shtus* of the opposite side. This is as it is written regarding the future: "And it shall water the valley of *shittim*," through which "the glory of the *Kudsha Brich Hu* is exalted in all worlds [ftnref_2502_ב]."

2) To understand the general concept of the *shtus* of the opposite side, because of which there must be *shtus d'kedushah*, through which "the glory of the *Kudsha Brich Hu* is exalted in all worlds," it is written : "If his wife goes astray (*tishteh*)," which Rashi explains as "deviating from the ways of modesty." It is also written : "And Israel dwelt in *Shittim*," and the Midrash [ftnref_2503_ג] states that *Shittim* is a place that causes *shtus*, through which one comes to commit the sin of immorality. As our Sages said regarding the verse "If his wife goes astray," a person does not commit a sin unless a spirit of *shtus* enters him. For an individual Jew, by his own nature, it is impossible to commit any sin. As the *Zohar* [ftnref_2504_ד] says regarding the verse "If a soul (*nefesh*) sins," that the *Torah* and the *Kudsha Brich Hu* are astonished by it, saying "If a soul sins" (in astonishment). This means that even at the level of *nefesh*—which is not the essence of the *neshamah*, and furthermore, it is the lowest of the five names given to it [ftnref_2505_ה]—the *Zohar* emphasizes that the concept of sin applies specifically to the *nefesh*, for at the level of *neshamah* and even *ruach*, sin is impossible. Nevertheless, even if there is a sin at this level, it is viewed with astonishment. Furthermore, even if it is only unintentional—since the verse speaks of an unintentional sin—it is still impossible except through the spirit of *shtus* that enters him. By his own nature, it is the nature of every Jew that he does not want and cannot be separated from *Elokus*. It is only through the spirit of *shtus* that covers the truth, making it seem to him that even while committing a sin he remains in his Jewishness, that it is possible for him to commit a sin. But when the truth shines within him—the aspect of "The truth of *Havayah* is forever "—he feels that through the sin, even a prohibition of the Sages or a minor detail of the words of the Scribes, he is utterly separated from *Elokus*, even more so than the *kelipos* and *sitra achra*. As the Alter Rebbe writes in *Tanya* [ftnref_2516_ו], a person who transgresses His will is worse and lower than the impure animals, etc., which do not change their function. Even though "he does not see, their *mazal* sees," therefore, in a person whose *tzelem Elokim* has not departed from his face, they do not have dominion over him, for "His command guards their spirit," as it is written : "And the fear of you and the dread of you shall be upon every beast," etc. Only when the "image of us, after our likeness " is not visible in him do they have dominion. Similarly, the nations of the world and the *kelipos* and *sitra achra* do not transgress His will; rather, they call Him "the God of gods," meaning they say that they too have control and dominion, but they do not rebel against Him, for they call Him "the God of gods" and never transgress His will. A person who commits a sin, who rebels against His will, is worse than the *kelipos* and *sitra achra* and the things influenced by them, which are the impure animals and wild beasts, to the point that even a mosquito, which "enters but does not exit," precedes you.

3) Seemingly, it is difficult to understand how a person who commits a sin is more separated than the *kelipa* and *sitra achra* because they do not transgress the King's will and call Him "the God of gods." After all, calling Him "the God of gods" is idolatry, as they attribute control to the stars or the celestial ministers, and in this they are transgressing the will of the Supreme One, blessed be He. If so, why is the person who commits a sin worse than them? The matter is that "God of gods" is the concept of *shituf* (partnership), and it is specifically Jews who are warned against *shituf*, not the descendants of Noach, as the Rema [ftnref_2517_ז] and others write. This is also implied by the Rambam (in *Sefer HaMitzvos*), who writes that the warning against *yichud* (unity) is from the verse : "Hear O Israel, *Havayah* is One," which implies that the descendants of Noach are not warned about this, for the verse specifically says "Israel." The matter is that regarding the Divine vitality in the worlds, there are two modes of vitality: *Memalei Kol Almin* (filling all worlds) and *Sovev Kol Almin* (surrounding all worlds). The vitality of *Memalei* comes in a state of enclothement within the worlds, as our Sages say : "Just as the soul fills the body, so the Holy One, blessed be He, fills the world." This means that just as the manifest vitality that enclothes itself in the body comes in a differentiated manner according to the nature of the limbs—the vitality in one limb is not the same as the vitality in another, for due to the change in the limbs, there is also a change in the vitality—since the vitality changes according to the nature of the limbs, it is understood that the body occupies a place relative to this vitality. Therefore, it changes according to the nature of the limbs, and because it occupies a place relative to the vitality, although it feels the vitality and is *batel* to it, it is not *batel* in its existence. Likewise, it is understood regarding the light of *Memalei*, which is in a state of enclothement in the worlds, that the *bittul* of the created beings to this vitality is not a *bittul* of existence. This is not the case with the light of *Sovev Kol Almin*, nor the vitality that is not manifest in the body, relative to which the created beings do not occupy any place at all. Therefore, this vitality is in a state of concealment; although one understands that the vitality of *Sovev* also exists, one does not feel it. It is like the matter of the creation of *yesh me'ayin* (something from nothing), where the *ayin* (nothingness) is in concealment; although the *yesh* (something) knows and understands that the *ayin* that brings it into being exists, it does not feel the *ayin* because it is in concealment.

4) Based on this, one can understand why specifically Jews were warned against *shituf* and not the descendants of Noach. The souls of Israel are rooted in the *Atzmus* (Essence), and below that—in the light of *Sovev*. Since at this level there is no place given to anything other than *Elokus*, they believe that "there is nothing else "—not only that there is no *Elokus* besides Him, but that there is no existence besides Him, and certainly that there is no thing that has any control or dominion. All this is because their root is from such a level where there is no place given to any existence, and therefore they were commanded regarding this. This is not the case with the nations of the world, who have no connection to the light of *Sovev*, for their root is from the externality of the Will. As is known, the souls of Israel are rooted in the internality of the Will, and the nations of the world are rooted in the externality of the Will. Just as below in a person, the internality of the Will is in something that is itself the purpose, whereas that which is only secondary to something else does not contain the internality of the Will but only the externality of the Will alone, so too it is understood above: the souls of Israel, who are the purpose, are rooted in the internality of the Will, and the nations of the world are rooted in the externality of the Will. Especially the *kelipa* and *sitra achra*, whose entire purpose is for man to overcome them, to subdue them. Since their root is from the externality of the Will alone, and the externality of the Will comes in the light of *Memalei*—for in the light of *Memalei* there is a place given to something outside of it—therefore they are not warned against *shituf*. Only through comprehension and meditation do they know, and are warned, that there is an existence above them and their vitality, and they call Him "the God of gods." Therefore, a person who commits a sin is worse than the *kelipa* and *sitra achra*. For even though their calling Him "the God of gods" is idolatry, which is the opposite of His will, they are not doing the opposite of the level of will to which they have a connection. This is not the case with a Jew who commits a sin; because his root is from the internality of the Will, even though that same thing is not a rebellion for the nations of the world, for a Jew it is a rebellion, since it is the opposite of the Will with which he is connected. Therefore, he is worse than the *kelipa* and *sitra achra* and is utterly separated from *Elokus*. And because it is the nature of every Jew that he does not want and cannot be separated from *Elokus*, it is impossible for him to commit a sin, were it not for the spirit of *shtus* that covers the truth, because of which it seems to him that he is not thereby separated from *Elokus*. The test for this is that when there is a matter where there is no room to err and think that he is not separated from *Elokus*, then even the simplest and lowest person stands in *mesiras nefesh* not to transgress His will, since he knows that through the sin he becomes separated from *Elokus*, heaven forbid. As the Mitteler Rebbe writes [ftnref_2535_ח], even one who increases his wickedness with all the sins in the world, even he sacrifices his soul in practice for the sanctification of the Name, since he knows that he becomes separated from *Elokus*, and this he cannot do under any circumstances. And since the source of this is because the souls of Israel are rooted in the *Atzmus*, which is above differentiation as mentioned above, it is found in each and every one, without any differentiation at all—meaning it is the same in the greatest of the great and the simplest of the simple. Furthermore, even the simplest of the simple, when the movement of *mesiras nefesh* is awakened in him, all the matters of his soul align with this movement; even in speech and action alone, although his mouth and heart are not equal, he stands in *mesiras nefesh*, for this movement is above differentiation, and therefore all the powers of his soul are included in it.

5) The explanation of the matter that it is the nature of every Jew that he does not want and cannot be separated from *Elokus* is found in *Etz Chaim* [ftnref_2536_ט]: there is one small spark of the Creator that enclothes itself in one spark of the created, called *Yechidah*. The mode of this enclothement is that they become one thing. This is found in each and every Jew, regardless of his manifest powers and the matters stemming from the other four names it has. Now, although the spark of the created has its root from the *keilim* (vessels), as the Tzemach Tzedek writes at length [ftnref_2537_י], and the *keilim* are matters of limitation and differentiation, it is known that the root of the souls is from the internality of the *keilim*. Especially the level of *Yechidah*, whose root is from the "quarry of the souls," which is a garment for the "quarry of the *Sefiros*," which are the externality of *Adam Kadmon* and the internality of *Adam Kadmon*. The quarry of the souls is the externality of *Adam Kadmon*, and the quarry of the *Sefiros* is the internality of *Adam Kadmon*. These are not two separate things; rather, the souls, whose root is from the internality of the *keilim*, the internality of the *keilim* is unified with the light that enclothes itself within them. Just as in the physical, the internality of the vessel is in close proximity to the thing enclothed within it, so too it is in the spiritual, that the internality of the vessel is unified with the light enclothed within it, and even more so, it is unified with the essence of the light. The externality of the vessel, whose purpose is to influence outward, not only is it not unified with the light enclothed within it, but the light that passes through the vessel and is drawn outward is only the light that is drawn to the other—that is, the light that is capable of influence and revelation to something outside of it. This is not the case with the internality of the vessel; besides being unified with the light within it, it is also unified with the essence of the light, which in the order of *hishtalshelus* (evolution) does not come to something outside of it. This is similar to the difference between the influence of the intellect and the influence of the seed. The influence of the intellect, which a teacher imparts to his student, is only an external influence, and therefore "it does not remain in the student's mind," for although he understands everything the teacher told him, it does not remain in his mind, because it is only an external influence. This is not the case with the influence of the seed, which is an internal influence to beget one like himself; what he begets is not only like his manifest matters and manifest powers, but like his essence. Therefore, it is possible for the power of the son to be greater than the power of the father, but this too is from the power of the father, because the begetting of one like himself is like the essence. This begetting of one like himself to the essence is because it is an essential influence, where he imparts the essence to him, and it comes in the essence of the light that is unified with the internality of the vessel. Although this too comes through the externality of the vessel and the drawing down is outside of it, it does not come through the order of *hishtalshelus* but through a break—a leap. For in the order of *hishtalshelus*, it is impossible for it to come to something outside of it, because it is an essential influence. Likewise, it is understood above regarding the root of the souls, that their root is from the *Atzmus* and is drawn down through the essence of the light as well. This drawing down is not in the angels but specifically in the souls, because their root is from the internality of the *keilim* that are unified with the essence of the light, and through this they are unified with the root of the essence of the light. Based on this, it is understood that although the root of the souls is from the *keilim* or from the quarry of the souls, which is the externality of *Adam Kadmon*, nevertheless, since the internality of the vessel is unified with the essence of the light as mentioned, it is also unified with the internality of *Adam Kadmon*. As is explained in *Likkutei Torah* on the *maamar* "LaMenatzeach Al HaSheminis," that the *bris milah* is above *Shabbos*. *Shabbos* is the seven days of the cycle, and rains descend on *Shabbos*, which is the concept of the externality of *Adam Kadmon*. *Milah* is the internality of *Adam Kadmon*, which is above *Shabbos*, and therefore it must pass through one *Shabbos* first. Nevertheless, it has a connection to *Shabbos*, which is the externality of *Adam Kadmon*, for through passing through one *Shabbos*, which is the externality of *Adam Kadmon*, it is possible to have the matter of *milah*, which is the internality of *Adam Kadmon*. Likewise, it is understood regarding the root of the souls, that although their root is from the externality of *Adam Kadmon*, they are also unified with the internality of *Adam Kadmon*. Based on this, it is understood why it is the nature of every Jew that he cannot be separated from *Elokus*; because their root is in the *Atzmus* and is, as it were, like Him, and in the *Atzmus* there is no place given to any matter other than *Elokus*, therefore it is the nature of every Jew that he cannot be separated from *Elokus* in any way.

6) However, due to the spirit of *shtus* that enters him, it is possible for him to commit a sin. The matter of the spirit of *shtus* is the intensity of desire for physical pleasures, and it comes from this: although it is in permitted things, because he is immersed in it, and especially the vitality and pleasure he has in it, it cools him and takes away from him the taste for *Elokus*. As the *Zohar* [ftnref_2546_יא] says, "The strength of the body is the weakness of the soul." This does not refer to the strength of the body in the literal sense, for on the contrary, having a healthy and whole body is one of the ways of Hashem [ftnref_2547_יב]. As is known from the teaching of the Baal Shem Tov on the verse : "If you see the donkey," etc.: "If you see the donkey"—when you look with good insight into your materiality, which is the body, you will see that it hates the soul that longs for *Elokus* and spirituality. And you will further see that it is "crouching under its burden," which the Holy One, blessed be He, gave to the body so that it would be refined through *Torah* and *mitzvos*, and the body is lazy in fulfilling them. And perhaps it will enter your heart to "refrain from helping it" so that it can fulfill its mission, because if you begin with self-afflictions to break the materiality, that is not the way the light of *Torah* will dwell. Rather, "you shall surely help with it," to refine the body and purify it, and not to break it with self-afflictions. It is also known what the Maggid [ftnref_2548_יג] said: "A small hole in the body becomes a large hole in the soul." From all this it is understood that "the strength of the body" does not refer to the body, but rather to the animal soul, that the strength of the animal soul, which rules over him and allows itself to enter into physical pleasures—even though they are permitted things—nevertheless, this cools the heat that should be in holiness and takes away the taste that should be in *Elokus*. Therefore, regarding the future, it is written: "And it shall water the valley of *Shittim*." A valley in the literal sense is from water, and the valley of *Shittim* is the aspect of physical pleasures. As the Rebbe Maharash [ftnref_2549_יד] said, water causes all kinds of pleasure to grow, for although many things are needed for growth, the essence of the growth is from the water. The reason why physical pleasures are *shtus* is that, besides the fact that many physical pleasures end in bitterness, even those things that are pleasurable, it is known regarding the concept of "And all my innards (*kirbi*) shall say, 'Hashem, who is like You'," that *kirbi* refers to the angels, who are called by the name "innards." Just as the innards are what separate the food from the waste, and the waste is pushed out, so too above there are angels who separate the influence of the pleasure from above, especially the pleasures of holiness, from what is waste in their estimation, which evolves downward, and from this physical pleasures are formed. Therefore, physical pleasure is called by the name *shtus*, because in truth it is waste. If so, taking pleasure in it is *shtus*, especially when he knows that through physical pleasures he will lack the pleasure in *Elokus*. This is a great *shtus*, that he exchanges physical pleasure—which is truly waste—for Divine pleasure, which is the essence of pleasure.

7) The cause of the spirit of *shtus* is found in the Mishnah regarding "If his wife goes astray": "Just as her deed is the deed of an animal, so her offering is the food of an animal." This means that the cause is because he is an animal, lacking the *Da'as* (knowledge/connection) of holiness. This is the cause of the desire for physical things, which is *shtus*, and because of this he can err and think that through the sin he commits he does not become separated from *Elokus*. Although he has intellect and understanding in holiness, it is not enough, and it must be the aspect of *Da'as* specifically. Therefore, almost all the souls in our generations are called "seed of an animal," for although most of them are capable of grasping and understanding matters of holiness in general, and especially the greatness of the Creator, blessed be He, from books and teachers—a great grasp and understanding—nevertheless they are called "seed of an animal" because they lack the aspect of *Da'as* and feeling in their souls. The concept of *Da'as* is as written in *Tanya*, that it is from the expression "And the man knew (*yada*)," etc., which is the aspect of connection and attachment to the thing. He does not meditate on the matter in a general way—for then it is easily disconnected from the intellect—but he connects himself to the thing; he is immersed in the matter. At a higher level, the concept of *Da'as* is the aspect of feeling, that he feels the thing, not only feeling it in his mind but also feeling it in his heart. This is the true concept of *Da'as*, as the Rebbe [ftnref_2555_טו] said regarding the reason why a minor is not obligated in *mitzvos*: although he understands the matter thoroughly, he is not a "master of *Da'as*." The intention is that he does not have a feeling for the preciousness and greatness of the matter, and because he does not have this feeling, it is impossible to obligate him and place upon him the responsibility of a positive or negative commandment. As the language is in the matter of vows and *hekdesh*, that they must know in whose name they are vowing and sanctifying; the precision here is "knowing" specifically—the aspect of feeling. At a higher level, it is the concept of recognition, for feeling can exist even for a thing that is not manifest to him, as "the heart knows the bitterness of its soul " even though the cause is in concealment from him. Recognition is when it comes into manifestation until it is verified to him as if he sees it literally, and he does not need proofs and evidence for it. This lack of *Da'as* is the cause of the spirit of *shtus*, which covers the truth and misleads the person, so that it seems to him that even when he transgresses the Supreme Will he remains in his Jewishness. In order to remove the spirit of *shtus* of the opposite side, it is specifically through *shtus d'kedushah*. Although in general one should walk in the middle path, this is only when he has walked and continues to walk in the straight path all his days. But when he has deviated from this path to the extreme of the opposite side, he must deviate to the other extreme specifically. This is similar to what is explained regarding a *baal teshuvah* (penitent), that he should not say "I do not want, and what can I do, for my Father in heaven has decreed upon me," but rather he must say "I do not want," for as a *baal teshuvah* he needs a special fence [ftnref_2556_טז]. Likewise, it is in this matter: since through the *shtus* of the opposite side and the sins they removed the *Shechinah*, in order that there be "And I will dwell in them" afterward, there must be "And they shall make Me a sanctuary" of *shittim* wood specifically—*shtus d'kedushah*.

8) Just as "And they shall make Me a sanctuary" and the *avodah* within it were through Moshe and through Aharon [ftnref_2564_יז], and the essence [ftnref_2565_יח] of Aharon's *avodah* is "When you light the lamps," for "The lamp of *Havayah* is the soul of man," and the seven lamps are the seven levels in the *avodah* of Hashem. The concept of Aharon is "loving the creatures and bringing them close to *Torah*," for even those who are far from the *Torah* of *Havayah* and His *avodah*—and therefore are called simply "creatures," for they have no merit other than that they are created, meaning that the Holy One, blessed be He, created them [ftnref_2566_יט]—even in them he drew down the aspect of love, *ahavah rabbah* (great love). This is the general difference between the love of Avraham and the love of Aharon, that the love of Aharon is *ahavah rabbah*. Through Moshe and Aharon were the drawings down in the *Mishkan*, and Moshe and Aharon are both equal. Likewise, it is in every generation, for "the extension of Moshe in every generation," and especially the *Nesi'im*, whose every matter—whether in *Torah*, in *mitzvos*, or in the leadership of Israel—is in love of Israel, love of *Torah*, and love of *Havayah*. Regarding the love of Israel, the Rebbe [ftnref_2567_כ] adds in the name of the Alter Rebbe that "Love your neighbor as yourself " is a vessel for "Love *Havayah* your God," and the love of Israel is greater, for one loves what the Beloved loves. This they drew down into all the Chassidim, those who are connected and belong to them, each according to his matter, standing, and situation. One must toil oneself, but since one needs the giving of strength and Divine assistance in general, and especially from that soul of which he is a spark, this is the drawing down that comes through the *Nesi'im*—a sanctuary of *shittim* wood—through which they clarify the *shtus* of the opposite side, and the *Shechinah* [ftnref_2568_כא] dwells in the work of his hands, and "the pleasantness of *Havayah* be upon us, and the work of our hands establish it " with success, until the coming of Mashiach Tzidkeinu, speedily in our days.

## Notes on the Maamar "Basi L'Gani," 5713

### Notes on the Maamar "Basi L'Gani," 5713

### From the Rebbe

In response to many questions regarding the Maamar "Basi L'Gani," 5713, at the end of section 2, which states: "The nations of the world, the kelipos, and the sitra achra do not transgress His will, may He be blessed; rather, they call Him 'G-d of gods,' etc., but nevertheless, they do not rebel against Him, may He be blessed."

Seemingly, there is a difficulty:
1) We find that the nations of the world do transgress the seven Noahide commandments that they were commanded to observe.

The answer is simple: it is along the lines of what is explained regarding Bnei Yisrael—that it is due to a "spirit of folly" that enters them, which covers and conceals the truth, and this is in contradiction to the fact that they call Him "G-d of gods." But in and of themselves, as they are influenced by the kelipos and sitra achra, they are in a state where they would not transgress or rebel. This is like Bilaam, who said, "I cannot transgress the word of Hashem" (see Tanya, Chapter 24), even though he committed the sin with his donkey (Sanhedrin 105b; Zohar Vol. I, 128b), which is one of the seven Noahide commandments.

2) We find that the nations of the world deny the existence of G-d entirely.

This difficulty is not directed at the Maamar, but rather at the words of our Sages (Menachos, end of the tractate) who state that they call Him "G-d of gods" (and it is derived there from the verse, "From the rising of the sun to its setting," etc.), and this statement of our Sages is the foundation of what is explained in the Maamar.

The explanation for this is understood from what is written in Sefer HaMitzvos by the Tzemach Tzedek, at the end of the Mitzvah of the Unity of Hashem; see there. And this is not the place to elaborate.

3) In the Maamar there, at the beginning of section 7, it is stated that almost all the souls in our generations are called by the name "seed of an animal." Where is the source for this?

It is in Torah Or by the Alter Rebbe (author of the Tanya and the Shulchan Aruch), at the beginning of Parshas Mishpatim. It is based on what is written in the Zohar, Vol. II, at the beginning of Parshas Mishpatim (94, bottom side). See Ramaz there. See also Likutei Torah by our Rebbe the Alter Rebbe, Parshas Tzav (8b and onwards); Shaarei Teshuvah (by his son, the Mitteler Rebbe), Part I, the discourse beginning "Shishim Hemah"; and in many other places.

## 10 Shevat, 5719

"I have come into My garden, My sister, My bride." The Rebbe, the author of the *hillula*, explains this in the *maamar* based on the Midrash: "Into My garden" — into My *ginuni* (My bridal chamber), the place where My essence was initially. The essence of the *Shechinah* was originally in the lower realms, for at the beginning of creation, the world was created in its fullness. Through the sin of the Tree of Knowledge and the subsequent sins, the *Shechinah* was removed from below to above, up to the seventh heaven. The *tzaddikim*, through their *avodah*, drew the *Shechinah* down from above to below. This drawing down began with Avraham, who was the first, and he brought it down from the seventh heaven to the sixth, and so on, until Moshe, who was the seventh—and "all sevenths are beloved"—brought it down from the first heaven to the earth below. Therefore, it was said to Moshe, "And they shall make Me a sanctuary, and I will dwell among them." In its literal sense, this refers to the drawing down of *Elokus* to be below on earth. Furthermore, as our Sages say, "In it" (*betocho*) is not written, but "among them" (*betocham*)—within each and every one of Israel.

Based on this, the *maamar* explains why the *Mishkan*—for the *Mishkan* is a *Mikdash* and the *Mikdash* is a *Mishkan*—was made of *shittim* wood. *Shittim* connotes *hetiah* (deviation), as it is written, "The people wandered (*shatu*) about." It refers to a deviation from the path of righteousness and the middle way, which occurs in two ways: a deviation from the middle way toward the negative, and a deviation toward the good. The deviation toward the negative is as our Sages say, "A person does not commit a sin unless a spirit of folly (*shetus*) enters him." This *shetus*—the folly of the world and the folly of the *sitra achra*—must be transformed into the *shetus* of *kedushah*, which is *avodah* that transcends *taam v'daas* (reason and intellect). This transformation—from the *shetus* of the *sitra achra* to the *shetus* of *kedushah*—is the essence of the *avodah* of the *Mishkan* and *Mikdash*. Through this, "I will dwell among them" is achieved, so that the essence of the *Shechinah* is in the lower realms: "I have come into My garden, into My bridal chamber."

Based on this, the *maamar* also explains why the *shittim* wood is called *kerashim* (boards). All names in the Torah are precise and correspond to their inner content. The letters of *kerash* are the same as *sheker* (falsehood) and *kesher* (connection). This means that one must take the *sheker* of the world, transform it, and make of it the *kerashim* of the *Mishkan*—a state of *kesher*, connecting all worldly matters to the *Shechinah*, and to the essence of the *Shechinah*, which is the inwardness and essence of the *Ein Sof*. The *maamar* explains in detail the letters *kuf* and *reish* and how they correspond to *kedushah*, and how one must transform them from their state as "letters of falsehood" to matters of goodness and *kedushah*, as explained at length in the preceding sections.

2) The *maamar* (in section 9) explains that this is also why the *kerashim* are called *omdim* (standing), as it is written, "And you shall make the boards for the *Mishkan* of *shittim* wood, standing." It explains that *omdim* refers to *amudim* (pillars)—the letters are the same. As it is stated in *Maseches Yoma* regarding the verse "shittim wood standing," *omdim* refers to *ma'amidim* (that which supports). The *maamar* explains that the function of a pillar is to connect the roof and the floor so they are as one. This is the concept of the *kerashim* called *amudim*: they are like the *mitzvos*, which are called *amudim* because they draw down and connect the *Ohr Ein Sof* with the worlds. Thus, the *kerashim* also connect the *Ohr Ein Sof* with the worlds.

Regarding the *mitzvos* as *amudim*, the Alter Rebbe explains in *Iggeres HaKodesh* that the Supreme Will is called *Keter*, and it is stated in the prayer of R. Nechunya ben HaKaneh that in the *Keter* there are 620 pillars of light. He explains that just as pillars in a large building stand on the ground and their tops are connected to the ceiling, so too the *mitzvos* stand from the "height of heights"—the Supreme Will—and are drawn down to the earth. The *mitzvos* are clothed in physicality, and even those *mitzvos* not clothed in physical objects were given to the physical human, who possesses free will, to incline his heart toward the good.

From the precise language of the *maamar*, it is understood that there is a distinction between the *kerashim* and the *mitzvos*. The *maamar* says the *kerashim* are "like" the *mitzvos* (an analogy only), meaning the *mitzvos* are actual pillars, while the *kerashim* are *like* the *mitzvos*. Therefore, regarding the *mitzvos*, it says they "draw down and connect" the *Ohr Ein Sof* and the worlds, whereas regarding the *kerashim*, it does not use the term "draw down," but rather that they "connect" the *Ohr Ein Sof* with the worlds, "like" the *mitzvos*.

In *Tanya*, there is also a distinction between the analogy of the pillars and the subject of the *mitzvos*. Regarding the pillars, it says they stand on the ground and their top is connected to the ceiling, while regarding the *mitzvos*, it says they stand at the height and are drawn down to the earth. This change between the analogy and the subject is understood simply: the pillars begin by standing on the ground, and from there they reach the ceiling. The *mitzvos*, however, stand above—as we say, "Who has sanctified us with His *mitzvos*," the *mitzvos* of Above—and from there they are drawn down below. This corresponds to the well-known principle that there is a difference between a foundation below and a foundation above. Below, the foundation is at the bottom, and the building is constructed upon it. Above, the foundation is above the building. Therefore, regarding the pillars, it says they "stand on the ground"—*nitzav* (standing) implies strength, as in "The king stood (*nitzav*)"—for below, the strength and foundation are on the ground, and the building is built upon it. Regarding the *mitzvos*, it says they "stand at the height of heights," because above, the strength and foundation are above.

3) However, one must understand why the foundation below is different from the foundation above. This is understood based on what is explained regarding *yesh hanivra* (the created being). The fact that in its own feeling it has no prior cause or reason is because its creation is from the *Atzmus* (Essence), whose existence is from Itself and has no prior cause or reason; only He alone has the power and ability to create *yesh* from *ayin* and absolute nothingness, as explained in *Iggeres HaKodesh*. Therefore, in the future, when the truth and inwardness of all things will be revealed, it will be revealed in the created *yesh* that its true root is the True *Yesh* that has no cause or reason.

With this, we understand "the end is embedded in the beginning": it is precisely in the "end"—the physical *yesh*, than which there is nothing lower—that the "beginning," which is above the level of "head," is embedded. The "beginning," which is the *Atzmus*, is found precisely in the "end." This is the dwelling place for His *Atzmus*, which will be revealed in the future, though in truth it is so even now. This is the reason why below, the foundation is specifically at the bottom—the *yesh* of *Asiyah*—and the higher worlds are built upon it. Conversely, above, it is explained in *Tanya* that the inner intent is not in the higher worlds; they are merely an illumination, and their entire purpose is only so that through them the inner intent may be completed specifically below. The light and illumination from above are more sublime, and therefore above, the pillars stand at the "height of heights," because the foundation and strength of the matter are above, and from there it is drawn down below.

4) Yet, one still needs to understand why the created *yesh* is the "True *Yesh*" and why this is not found in spirituality. Seemingly, the "True *Yesh*" should be in spiritual matters, which are called "higher" because they are higher in level. The matter is as the Mitteler Rebbe explains with three analogies regarding the principle that "everything that is higher, higher still, descends and is clothed and revealed in the lower, lower still."

The first analogy is of a great torch whose light is vast and shines from afar; when one needs to illuminate a distant place, a great torch is required. Thus, it is understood that the superiority of the great torch over a candle or a small torch is recognized specifically in the distant place; there, the "higher" quality of the torch is revealed. Even though the light that reaches the distance is very small—to the point that if a candle were lit there, it would shine more than the light of the torch—nevertheless, it is precisely in this that the superiority and strength of the torch are recognized.

The second analogy is of a barrel full of water, or a wise man full of wisdom. Because of the abundance of water in a barrel filled to the brim, some spills outside, and it is precisely in this that the abundance of the water is recognized. Even though the water that spills outside is negligible in quantity compared to the water inside the barrel, it is precisely in this that it is recognized that the barrel is filled to the brim. The same applies to wisdom: through the "droppings of wisdom" and the "idle chatter of scholars," it is recognized that the influence comes from a great sage who is connected to the depth of wisdom.

The third analogy is of the drop of physical seed, from which a new essence is born—a new entity from one essence to another. In contrast, in the influence of intellect, which is a spiritual influence, a new birth cannot occur; only when the student is a person of intellect can he understand the intellect the teacher has imparted. Thus, the superiority of the physical drop is understood: specifically from it is born a new essence, an essence different from the first, which is the influence of the offspring.

One can explain these three analogies: the descent of the "higher, higher still" to the "lower, lower still" is the drawing down from the *Atzmus* to *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah*. In *Atzilus*, "He and His life are one, He and His causes are one," and it is called the "world of *Elokus*," which is not "low." The "low" refers to the creation, the worlds of *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah*. The order of influence in these worlds is in the manner of "light, water, and firmament," as explained at length in the *maamarim* of the month of Tishrei. These are the three analogies: the torch is the light, the barrel is the water, and the drop is the firmament, which becomes physical and a new essence.

It was explained in the *maamarim* of Tishrei that this concept of "higher, higher still" applies not only in *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah*, but also in *Atzilus* and *Keter* and even higher, up to the light before the *Tzimtzum*. Thus, it is understood that the three analogies of the torch, the barrel, and the drop apply there as well. In every place and every level, the revelation of the "higher, higher still" is in the "lower, lower still" (meaning, in the lowest of that world, the highest of that world is revealed). In the general order of *Hishtalshelus*, the "lower, lower still" is in *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah*, and down to the world of *Asiyah*. There, the "higher, higher still"—which is above the "higher, higher still" of the level of *Atzilus*, and above *Atzilus*, and before the *Tzimtzum*, which is His *Atzmus* and Essence—is revealed in the "lowest of the low." The doubling of the word "lowest" indicates the most low possible, than which there is nothing lower, which is the world of *Asiyah*.

This is the meaning of the pillars standing specifically on the ground. When one must build a great sanctuary—which is the greatness of Hashem, for "in the place of His greatness, there you find His humility"—then, in the entire order of *Hishtalshelus*, even though there is the aspect of "higher, higher still" at all levels, the revelation of the "higher, higher still"—which is the *Atzmus*—is in the "lowest of the low," which is the world of *Asiyah*. And within *Asiyah* itself, not in spiritual *Asiyah*, but in physical *Asiyah*. From this, it is understood that even within physical *Asiyah* itself, one needs the "lowest of the low," meaning that the revelation of the *Atzmus* is not in the spiritual *avodah* of this world, but specifically in physical *Asiyah*. This is also what is emphasized in *Likutei Torah* in the *maamarim* on *Tzitzis* regarding the analogy of the pillars standing on the ground: for the pillars to support the building and the ceiling above, they must be in hard earth, not loose soil. So too, the *mitzvos* must be in hard earth, as it concludes there, "Action is above," which refers to the *mitzvos* that are clothed specifically in physicality.

5) With this, we can understand the precision of the language in the *maamar* regarding the difference between the *kerashim* and the *mitzvos*: that the *kerashim* are "like" (an analogy only) the *mitzvos*, and also why regarding the *kerashim* it says "connect" rather than "draw down." According to what was explained in the previous sections, the *avodah* of the *kerashim* is a spiritual *avodah* within man. As it is explained regarding the *avodah* of the sacrifices, a person must work with the fire of the *nefesh habahamit*—which is the "fire of the commoner" within man—and the fire of the *nefesh elokit*—which is the "fire from above" within man. One must refine the *nefesh habahamit* in the details of its levels, as explained above regarding the ox, the sheep, and the goat. Therefore, although this *avodah* is in the physical world, in the context of this world itself, it is a spiritual *avodah*, not a physical *avodah*. It is not the actual *Asiyah* (of which it is said "Action is above"), and it is not "hard, strong earth."

This is also what the Baal Shem Tov said, that the *keresh* (board) is the person whose task is to connect and bind the worlds with *Elokus*. As it is stated in *Tikkunei Zohar* on the verse "ten cubits is the length of the board," this is the measure of the body of a person, for man is composed of ten powers: seven *middos* and three *mochin*. This is the *avodah* of the *kerashim*: the *avodah* of the *nefesh elokit* and *nefesh habahamit*, to take the physical things—the *shetus* of the *sitra achra*—and transform them into the *shetus* of *kedushah*. Since the *avodah* of the *kerashim* is a spiritual *avodah* within man, one cannot draw down the *Ohr Ein Sof* through it, because the drawing down of the light is from the *Atzmus*, and the drawing down from the *Atzmus* is only through the *avodah* of the "lowest of the low," which is the fulfillment of *mitzvos* in physicality. Therefore, regarding the *mitzvos*, it says they "draw down."

The *mitzvos* are in physicality, as the Rebbe, the author of the *hillula*, explains in several *maamarim* that even the *mitzvos* of the "duties of the heart" must be felt in the physical flesh, in the brain and heart. For example, in fear, there must be a physical contraction in the brain and heart. Similarly, in comprehension, there must be an addition of "lines and wrinkles" in the physical brain from the contemplation and depth of Torah study. Likewise, in the general *avodah*, which must be with *taanug* (delight) from one's service to his Creator, the *taanug* must add "fatness," as it is written, "A good report makes the bones fat"—a physical fatness in the physical flesh.

This is what the *maamar* means when it says the *kerashim* are an analogy for the *mitzvos*: through the *kerashim*, the "higher, higher still" is also drawn down, but not the "highest of the high"—only in the *mitzvos* is the "highest of the high," which is the *Atzmus*, drawn down. This is the matter of the *mitzvos*: they are drawn from the *Atzmus* and connect the *Ohr Ein Sof* with the worlds. When a person fulfills the *mitzvos*, initially he connects them with the worlds—this is the beginning of the *avodah* through the spiritual *avodah* of the *kerashim*—and afterwards (through fulfilling them in physicality), he connects them with the *Atzmus*.

This is similar to what we find in the Torah, that Dovid would connect the Torah of Above with the Holy One, blessed be He. So too with the *mitzvos*: before performing them, in the blessing of the *mitzvos*, we say "Who has sanctified us with His *mitzvos*"—the *mitzvos* that were already drawn down in Supreme Kindness—and he draws them onto himself and connects them to the worlds. Through his performance of the *mitzvos*, the Holy One, blessed be He, "nods His head," which is the drawing down of the *Atzmus*. Similarly, in Torah, the Holy One, blessed be He, reads and repeats along with him. In all matters, the Holy One, blessed be He, acts "measure for measure": what He commands Israel to do, He does Himself. This means that through the performance of the Jewish person, he draws them down anew and with added strength from the *Atzmus*.

This is what the Maggid said regarding the verse, "You shall keep My statutes and My judgments, which a man shall do and live by them." "My statutes" refers to thought, which is a special garment like the letters of engraving. "My judgments" refers to speech, as it is written, "The word of a king is power," and "A king establishes the land by judgment." "Which a man shall do" refers to action, which is the purpose. Through this, "and live by them," for through the *avodah* in thought, and drawing from thought through speech to the level of action, he draws life into the *mitzvos*. This is the *avodah* of the *kerashim*, which is a spiritual *avodah* in man, through which one comes to the fulfillment of *mitzvos* in physicality. Through this, one draws down the *Atzmus* and connects it to the worlds, and as the *maamar* says, "the roof and the floor are as one," so that in the created *yesh*, the True *Yesh* will be revealed.

6) The *maamar* continues by explaining the hint of the letter *kuf*, whose left leg extends far below. In general, this is what is written, "Rebuke the beast of the reed (*kaneh*)." The *kaneh* refers to the long reed that extends below; it is the male of the *kelipah*. The "beast of the reed" is the female of the *kelipah*, the level of *Malchus* of the *kelipah*, which receives from the male. One must eradicate them; this is "Rebuke the beast of the reed." In the *avodah* of man, the male of the *kelipah* refers to the negative *middos*, and the female of the *kelipah* refers to becoming a "trodden threshold," the acceptance of the yoke of the *sitra achra*. One must "rebuke the beast of the reed"—one must eradicate the *kaneh* of the *kuf* and break the pride of the tyrants. By cutting the *kaneh* of the *kuf*, it becomes a *heh*, and then it becomes the word *hineh* (behold). *Hineh* indicates revelation, and revelation is the matter of *kedushah*. Therefore, in the future, when all matters of *kedushah* in their inwardness will be revealed—so that they will be seen tangibly with physical eyes—it is written, "And it shall be said on that day, 'Behold (*hineh*), this is our G-d.'"

The revelation of the future is through the *avodah* of now, as the *maamar* explains in the previous sections and mentions in this section in outline: the *avodah* of "transforming darkness into light and bitterness into sweetness," where one transforms the *sitra achra* and makes of it goodness and *kedushah*. Especially when there are concealments, hiddenness, and trials, and yet one is not affected by them at all—on the contrary, through the trials, he strengthens himself with added power and added strength to perform his *avodah*—through this, he transforms from the *shetus* of the *sitra achra* to the *shetus* of *kedushah*. Through this, the revelation of the future will come: "And it shall be said on that day, 'Behold, this is our G-d'—this, this" (the word "this" is repeated twice), for the level of "I, Hashem, have not changed" will be revealed. This is through the *avodah* of now, specifically in physicality.

7) The matter is as the Tzemach Tzedek explains at length regarding the verse, "Those who come shall take root, Yaakov shall blossom and bud, and Israel shall fill the face of the world with fruit." The *mitzvos* are called "sowing." Just as in physical sowing, the sowing must be in the earth specifically, and only then will there be blossoming, so too with the sowing of the *mitzvos*: in order for them to perform their effect—which is "blossoming and budding," the revelation of the future, "Behold, this is our G-d"—it is through the sowing of the *mitzvos* specifically in the physical earth.

This is in two respects: both regarding the *mitzvos* themselves, which are clothed in physicality, and regarding the person performing them. The *mitzvos* were given specifically to the soul in a body—not to the soul as it is above, and not to the angels, but to the soul as it is specifically in the physical body. It is in this that the seed of the *mitzvos* is sown, through which the blossoming occurs. As he explains there regarding the drawing down of the "Self-Exaltation," which is His *Atzmus* and Essence: "There is no king without a people," from the word *umot* (nations/peoples) and *nifradim* (separated). Therefore, the drawing down of kingship and Self-Exaltation is not through the angels, and certainly not through the souls as they are above, for they are in a state of *bitul* (nullification), as it is written, "As Hashem, the G-d of Israel, lives, before whom I have stood." Rather, the drawing down of kingship and Self-Exaltation is through the *avodah* of the souls as they are below, clothed in a physical body, in this world where there are two paths, as it is written, "See, I have set before you... and it is possible to choose, G-d forbid, the opposite." This is the level of *umot* and *nifradim*, through which the revelation of kingship and Self-Exaltation—which is above all lights and revelations—occurs. This is the level of "I, Hashem, have not changed."

Although "I, Hashem, have not changed" is constant—for this is the truth of the matter—nevertheless, for this level to be in tangible revelation, as it is written, "Behold... this" (everyone points with their finger), the tangible revelation will be specifically in the future, and it is through the *avodah* of now, below, specifically in physicality.

The matter is as the Rebbe Maharash explained regarding the verse, "I have loved you with an everlasting love, therefore I have drawn you with kindness." It is stated in *Tanna D'bei Eliyahu* that the love of the Holy One, blessed be He, for Israel—lest you say it is a love of three years, ten years, or a hundred years—is not so; rather, "I have loved you with an everlasting love." The Rebbe Maharash explains in the *hemshech* of *Mayim Rabim* that *olam* (everlasting) is a term for eternity. This is an everlasting love, an eternal love. This does not contradict what is said elsewhere, that "everlasting love" is a lower level than "great love." Here it is explained that "everlasting love" is the most sublime love, above which there is none. He explains that there are two meanings to *olam*: eternity, and "world" (*olam*), as in "two worlds," and as it is written, "Blessed be Hashem, the G-d of Israel, from the world and to the world."

Although there are countless worlds, as it is written, "And worlds without number"—do not read *alamot* (maidens) but *olamot* (worlds)—nevertheless, in general, they are divided into two categories: *Alma D'Itgalya* (the revealed world) and *Alma D'Itkassya* (the hidden world). *Alma D'Itgalya* is in a state of extreme measure, limitation, and *Tzimtzum*. When love comes from contemplation of the light within the measure, limitation, and *Tzimtzum* of the world—which in general is the level of *Memaleh Kol Almin*—from this one comes to love. Since the love comes from something measured, limited, and restricted, it is a restricted love and lower than "great love." *Alma D'Itkassya*, however, is above measure and limitation, similar to what is stated in the Gemara regarding "that which is hidden from the eye, upon which a blessing rests"—meaning it has no limitations. This is the meaning of "everlasting love" (*ahavas olam*), from the word for eternity, which is higher in level not only than the *ahavas olam* of limitation—the level of *Memaleh*—but also than the level of *Sovev*.

This is the meaning of "love of three years, ten years, and a hundred years." The divine vitality as it is in limitation in the three worlds of *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah* is the "three years," for since in every world there is the matter of "world, year, soul," the three worlds are called "three years." "Love of ten years" is the level of *Atzilus*, for in *Atzilus* there is the matter of the ten *Sefiros*, the level of "ten years." It is higher than *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah*, for in *Atzilus* there are ten *Sefiros* without *Mah* (what/limitation). Although they are ten *Sefiros*—ten and not nine, ten and not eleven—nevertheless, they are without *Mah*, and it is higher than *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah*, for the influence to *Beriah, Yetzirah, and Asiyah* from *Atzilus* is through the *Parsa* (partition). *Atzilus* is from the word *etzlo* (with Him) and *samuch* (near), and it is called the "world of unity." Yet, even *Atzilus* is in limitation, and this is not the ultimate love. "Love of a hundred years" is the light as it is in the level of *Keter*, which is above *Atzilus*. All the worlds of the *Ein Sof* above *Atzilus* are all included in the level of *Keter*, and this is the matter of "a hundred years," as explained in the Zohar regarding the verse, "And the life of Sarah was a hundred years"—that a hundred years is the level of *Keter*, the place that includes everything, the supreme place. The ten *Sefiros*, as each is included in ten, is the matter of a hundred years.

Nevertheless, even *Keter* is included in the worlds, and although it is the level of *Sovev*, even the *Sovev* has a connection to the worlds in the aspect of *Sovev*, which is why it is called "surrounding the worlds." However, the matter of "everlasting love" (*ahavas olam*) from the word for eternity is from the *Atzmus*, which is without limit, and is higher even than the level of "a hundred years." In general, it is the ten *Sefiros* in the *Atzil* (the Emanator), the hidden ten *Sefiros*; from this aspect, it is an eternal love.

The meaning of "Blessed be Hashem, the G-d of Israel, from the world and to the world" is that one must draw down from the highest world to the lowest world, so that it is felt how it is all one, and as the *maamar* says, "the roof and the floor are as one." These are the two meanings of "everlasting love": *olam* as its literal meaning, which is this physical world—for "a verse does not depart from its literal meaning"—and in the world as its literal meaning, drawing down the level of *olam* from the word for eternity. This is as explained above, that the level of "I, Hashem, have not changed"—the absence of change and that which is above changes, which is the *Atzmus* that is above all lights (for in every level of light there is change)—the drawing down of the level of "I, Hashem, have not changed" is specifically in this lower world and in the future.

8) Regarding "I, Hashem, have not changed," the Rebbe (Rashab) explains that, seemingly, this is not understood, for there is an order of *Hishtalshelus*, and the light changes from world to world and is restricted according to the measure of each world, until in this world the light is extremely restricted and is in a state of total concealment and hiddenness—which is why it is called "lower," for there is nothing lower than it in level—and this is a change in the light. He explains there that, nevertheless, this is not a change from the perspective of the lights. Even though there are many differences in the light—the light as it is in *Asiyah* is not like as it is in *Yetzirah*, and in *Yetzirah* not like in *Beriah*, and in *Beriah* not like in *Atzilus*, and so on higher—these differences are only from the perspective of the worlds, for the world is not a vessel to receive a higher light, but the light itself is present in its perfection and fullness below just as it is above.

But it is still not understood, for the very fact that the worlds cannot receive it is a change. He explains that even the change from the perspective of the worlds and the recipients is not a change, for the fact that the recipients cannot receive the light is due to the "shield" that exists over the *Havayah* (the Name of Hashem), which is the concealment of the name *Elokim*. The name *Elokim* is a holy name among the seven names that may not be erased. Therefore, regarding the light itself, this is not a concealment at all, for "no essence hides an essence," as is known. This means that even from the perspective of the concealments and hiddenness, there is no change in the light at all.

But it still needs to be understood, for seemingly there is a change in the light from the perspective of the created beings, for the created beings are distinct from one another, and the creation of the beings is from the aspect of the light of the "word of Hashem," and for every created being there is a speech distinct from another. If so, this is a change in the light. He explains that in truth, even this is not a change. He explains this based on the analogy of the sunlight explained in *Tanya*: specifically when the light goes outside the luminary, it is in a state of "light," but while it is in the luminary, it is not an existence at all, for it is nullified in existence entirely in the luminary. Therefore, the "word of Hashem," which constantly creates the beings and is their entire existence—and as is known, this is not like a goldsmith who fashions a vessel, where the vessel continues to exist without the craftsman because it is *yesh mi-yesh* (something from something) and the craftsman did not create the existence of the being—is different in the creation of *yesh* from *ayin* and absolute nothingness. The light must constantly create the beings, and it is their entire existence. The light is always present in the luminary, for in the luminary the matter of *Tzimtzum* and removal, G-d forbid, does not apply. It is present in every place, even in the place of the created *yesh*, and the speech that creates, sustains, and gives it life is there. Since the beings and the light that creates them are always present in the luminary, they are nullified entirely, like the nullification of the sun's radiance in the sun. If so, it is understood that even from the perspective of the existence of the being, there is no change at all.

Through contemplation of "I, Hashem, have not changed," a desire is created in him for two things, in spirituality and in physicality. The first is that when he contemplates that the entire order of *Hishtalshelus* does not cause any change at all, he will desire that all his powers and desires be only in *Elokus*. The second is that when he contemplates that "I, Hashem, have not changed" and everything is found in the *Atzmus*, he will desire that even in the created *yesh*, he will not need to be nourished by things in whose externality *Elokus* is not apparent, but rather that he be nourished by the *Atzmus*, nourished by the radiance of the *Shechinah*.

The difference between these two is: the first, that all his spiritual powers—and in general, the inner powers, desire, and delight—should be only in *Elokus*, is within the power of man, and this is the *avodah* demanded of him. This is the *avodah* of the sacrifices: to offer the blood and the fat. The blood is the soul—the vitality—and the fat is the delight, the "fervor and taste," to offer them on the altar

## B’s”d. Motzaei Shabbos Parshas Beshalach, 11 Shevat (Maamar 2), 5728

"I have come to My garden, My sister, My bride." In the maamar of the day of his histalkus and hillula, the Rebbe, the Baal HaHillula, brings the Midrash Rabbah (ad loc.): "To My garden" — to My *ginuni* (bridal chamber), to the place where it was originally rooted. For the *ikar Shechinah* (the essence of the Divine Presence) was originally in the lower realms. However, due to undesirable matters (beginning with the sin of the Tree of Knowledge), the Shechinah withdrew from below to above, up to the seventh heaven. Subsequently, the tzaddikim arose (beginning with Avraham Avinu—"Avraham was one") and drew the Shechinah down from above to below, until Moshe came—who is the seventh (and "all sevenths are beloved")—and brought it down below, to the earth. This matter (the descent and drawing down of the Shechinah to the earth below) was accomplished primarily through the Mishkan that Moshe made, as it is written: "And they shall make Me a sanctuary, and I will dwell among them."

This is why the Mishkan was made of *shittim* wood: because the drawing down of the *ikar Shechinah* below is through the avodah of *iskafya* (subjugation) and *is’hapcha* (transformation). As the saying goes, "When the *sitra achra* is subjugated, the glory of the Ribono shel Olam is exalted in all worlds"—this is the drawing down and revelation of the *atzmus* (essence) of the *Ohr Ein Sof* that is beyond relevance to the worlds [which is why this drawing down is called *istalek*, for it is a revelation of the light that is in a state of transcendence], the *ikar Shechinah*. And since this drawing down is through the avodah of *iskafya* and *is’hapcha*, the Mishkan was made of *shittim* wood. *Shittim* comes from the word *shtus* (folly)—that is, the *shtus* of the *sitra achra* ("A person does not commit a sin unless a spirit of *shtus* enters him"). Through the avodah of "And you shall make the boards for the Mishkan of *shittim* wood standing," they transform the *shtus* of the *sitra achra* into *shtus* of *kedushah* ("*shittim* wood standing"). Through this, "I will dwell among them" is accomplished—the drawing down of the *ikar Shechinah* into the lower realms.

2) The maamar continues that this is also the meaning of one of the avodos [it can be said that "one of the avodos" means the special and primary avodah] in the Beis Hamikdash: the avodah of the *korbanos*. For the avodah of *korbanos* involves taking an animal (whose vitality is from *kelipat nogah*) and making it a *korban* (offering) to Hashem. This is especially so according to the interpretation of the Alter Rebbe on the verse, "When a man among you brings an offering," that the fact that it says "When a man among you brings" (and not "When a man brings from among you") is to hint that the *korban* must be (not only from the animal in the literal sense, but also) "from you"—*fun eich alein*—from the "animal" in the heart of man. When one makes the "animal" within himself (*nefesh habehamis*) a *korban* to Hashem, this is the concept of *iskafya* and *is’hapcha*.
